message.content:
=== THE LAW (violating it burns the artifact regardless of score) ===
Every sentence is ATTRIBUTED (names a source -> MUST be entailed by THAT source's VERBATIM SPAN) or
OWNED (reviewer's voice -> names no source, carries NO new particular, MAY be non-entailed — that is
what INSIGHT IS). THE VERBATIM SPAN IS THE ONLY EVIDENCE; the model-written `claim` is a display cache
and nothing is validated against it.

`/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/test_gate_is_wired.py` must stay GREEN (currently 25/25). NEVER weaken a check to make
it pass — that is the exact failure that let fabrication ship four times tonight. Run it after every change.

=== TONIGHT'S ONE LESSON ===
THE BUILDER CANNOT VERIFY ITSELF. Four times a module self-tested green while fabrication shipped; the
provenance module passed 18/18 while the P0 ran live on disk; the canary went 16/16 while 6 attacks
succeeded ("it certified a lane the fabrication no longer used"). So this wheel SEPARATES the builder
from the verifier: one agent wires, a DIFFERENT agent attacks.


=== THE DEFECT — Sol's #1 lever, +0.0259 in score units ===
/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/cellcog_composer.py generates ~28 subsections INDEPENDENTLY in a ThreadPoolExecutor
(around :479-524). NOBODY EVER DECIDES WHAT IS COMPARED WITH WHAT, so the report LISTS findings instead
of ADJUDICATING them. "Critical Synthesis" (w=0.0800, the joint-heaviest criterion in the rubric) scores
6.36 vs the leader's 9.60. bodhi beats us by 0.08 with a SEVENTEENTH of our sources — because it argues
and we enumerate. This is worth more than any corpus change.

=== WHAT EXISTS — WIRE IT, DO NOT REBUILD IT ===
/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/argument_planner.py (1,657 lines, 42 functions) is BUILT, TESTED, and imported by NOBODY.
It builds COMPARISON BUNDLES before prose: keys cards by (technology × outcome × industry × unit × method
× horizon × direction), finds the bundles that MATTER (same outcome + different unit = "only look
contradictory"; same unit + opposite direction = genuine conflict; a finding with no counterpart = a
boundary), and assigns each subsection a PLAN (claim-first thesis, ≥2 attributed clauses bound BY card_id,
the exact comparison, methodological comparability, an OWNED verdict, a boundary, a bridge).
/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/fact_use_ledger.py is also built + unwired — stops the same finding being narrated 8×.

THE COMPOSER WAS REBUILT TONIGHT: it writes DRAFTS and calls publisher.publish(); the model emits CARD
IDs and NEVER types a journal name; attribution is rendered by report_ast. Do NOT reintroduce surname
inference. Do NOT add a write to outputs/release/ (it is 0555; only publisher writes there).

CARDS: /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/outputs/evidence_cards_bound.json — 232 bound, honest, post-quarantine. Do NOT re-mine (paid).


YOU OWN cellcog_composer.py. You are the ONLY agent editing it. Work carefully and sequentially; run the
canary after every change.

READ FIRST, in order: cellcog_composer.py (the NEW post-rebuild structure), argument_planner.py (its API
and what it returns), report_ast.py (the ATTRIBUTED/OWNED node types the composer emits), research_contract.py
(it has derive_outline that the composer currently IGNORES for a hardcoded OUTLINE at :305).

THEN WIRE:
 1. Before writing subsections, the composer builds COMPARISON BUNDLES from the bound cards and hands each
    subsection its PLAN (thesis, the specific card_ids to compare, the comparison being made, the owned
    verdict slot, the boundary, the bridge). The writer FILLS the plan instead of freelancing 28 isolated
    pieces. Voice stays STRUCTURAL: attributed clauses carry card_ids, owned sentences carry premise_ids.
 2. The dedicated Critical Synthesis section must hold SEVERAL named cross-source syntheses spanning
    industries/methods — not a few residual paragraphs. That is the +0.0259.
 3. Wire fact_use_ledger too IF it fits cleanly; if it fights the new structure, report that and leave it.
 4. If the hardcoded OUTLINE should become research_contract.derive_outline() for generality, do it — but
    keep task 72 working.

DO NOT break the publish boundary. DO NOT weaken the gate. VERIFY BY RUNNING: the planner's bundle-building
is deterministic over the cards, so test it OFFLINE without a big paid compose; run test_gate_is_wired.py
(must stay green). Do NOT commit.

Report: exact call sites you changed, the comparison bundles the planner ACTUALLY found in the real 232
cards (quote them), whether fact_use_ledger went in, canary status, and anything unverified or unfinished.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll start by reading the key files in the order specified. Let me read them in parallel since they're independent.

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""THE COMPOSER — it writes a DRAFT. IT CANNOT PUBLISH. That is now a property of the filesystem.
3	
4	WHAT WAS WRONG WITH THIS FILE, AND WHY THE FIX IS NOT ANOTHER CHECK
5	-------------------------------------------------------------------
6	1. TWO DISCONNECTED CARD LANES. The miner wrote `evidence_cards_v2.json`; THIS FILE READ
7	   `evidence_cards.json` — a different file, whose cards carry no manifestation, no content hash and no
8	   span offsets, and four of whose cards were mined out of Frey & Osborne's ORA LANDING PAGE (548 words
9	   of cookie banner and metadata) and printed as findings of Technological Forecasting and Social
10	   Change. The composer could not have detected that: the lane it read had nothing in it to detect with.
11	   THE SEAM IS GONE. There is one bundle, it is passed EXPLICITLY, and it is pinned by hash.
12	
13	2. IT INFERRED SOURCE IDENTITY FROM SURNAMES. `_cited_cards()` decided which paper a sentence was about
14	   by regex-matching author surnames in the model's prose, then gated the sentence against whatever card
15	   that hit. So "Bresnahan reports task displacement" — a real mechanism, a real paper, a FABRICATED
16	   BINDING — was gated against a card the model had effectively chosen for itself.
17	   THE MODEL NO LONGER NAMES SOURCES AT ALL. It emits a bare finding plus THE CARD ID, and
18	   `report_ast.render_attribution()` attaches the citation, from the expression the SOURCE POLICY chose.
19	   The model cannot write "Writing in the Journal of Political Economy" over working-paper bytes,
20	   because it never types a journal's name.
21	
22	3. IT WROTE THE JUDGED ARTIFACT ITSELF (`(OUT_DIR / 'report.md').write_text(report)`, old line 775), and
23	   the hand-written abstract above it bypassed every gate in the file — 4 sentences of unsourced claims,
24	   assembled with an f-string, that no lane ever looked at.
25	   THERE IS NO WRITER IN THIS FILE. `outputs/release/` is mode 0555 and this process cannot create a
26	   file in it. The abstract is now AST nodes like everything else.
27	
28	WHAT THE COMPOSER MAY DO: propose nodes. What it may not do: emit characters into the release.
29	"""
30	from __future__ import annotations
31	
32	import argparse
33	import asyncio
34	import concurrent.futures as futures
35	import hashlib
36	import json
37	import os
38	import re
39	import sys
40	from pathlib import Path
41	
42	ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
43	sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT))
44	sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT / 'scripts'))
45	
46	import provenance as P                                                          # noqa: E402
47	import publisher                                                                # noqa: E402
48	from report_ast import (Attributed, Clause, Owned, Heading, ParagraphBreak,     # noqa: E402
49	                        EvidenceTable, CardBundle, CONNECTIVES, validate_report,
50	                        entailed_by_span, numbers_in, split_sentences)
51	from synthesis_contract import validate, Premise, Synthesis, OPERATIONS         # noqa: E402
52	
53	DRAFTS = ROOT / 'outputs' / 'drafts'
54	MODEL = os.getenv('PG_GENERATOR_MODEL', 'z-ai/glm-5.2')
55	
56	POLICIES = {p.name: p for p in (P.JOURNAL_ONLY, P.PEER_REVIEWED, P.OFFICIAL_TEXT, P.ANY_VERSION)}
57	
58	
59	# ----------------------------------------------------------------- LLM
60	
61	def llm(prompt: str, max_tokens: int = 8192) -> str:
62	    def _call() -> str:
63	        from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import OpenRouterClient
64	
65	        async def _run() -> str:
66	            c = OpenRouterClient(model=MODEL)
67	            try:
68	                r = await c.generate(prompt=prompt, max_tokens=max_tokens, temperature=0.0)
69	                if isinstance(r, str):
70	                    return r
71	                content = getattr(r, 'content', None)
72	                if content is not None:
73	                    return content
74	                return r.get('content') if isinstance(r, dict) else str(r)
75	            finally:
76	                cl = getattr(c, 'close', None)
77	                if cl:
78	                    try:
79	                        await cl()
80	                    except Exception:
81	                        pass
82	        loop = asyncio.new_event_loop()
83	        try:
84	            return loop.run_until_complete(_run())
85	        finally:
86	            loop.close()
87	
88	    with futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=1) as ex:
89	        return ex.submit(_call).result(timeout=420)
90	
91	
92	def jparse(s: str):
93	    s = re.sub(r'^```(?:json)?|```$', '', (s or '').strip(), flags=re.M).strip()
94	    m = re.search(r'\[.*\]', s, re.S)
95	    return json.loads(m.group(0)) if m else None
96	
97	
98	# ----------------------------------------------------------------- THE ONE BUNDLE
99	
100	def sha256_file(p: Path) -> str:
101	    return hashlib.sha256(Path(p).read_bytes()).hexdigest()
102	
103	
104	def load_bundle(cards_path: Path, graph_path: Path, ledger_path: Path,
105	                policy_name: str = 'journal_articles_only',
106	                expect_cards_sha: str = '', expect_graph_sha: str = '') -> CardBundle:
107	    """ONE explicit card-bundle path, plus ITS graph and ledger HASHES. No defaults that point at a file
108	    somebody else is writing.
109	
110	    A hash mismatch is a REFUSAL, not a warning. The composer that ran before this one read a card file
111	    whose provenance it never established, and the number it produced (0.4603) is not a real number.
112	    """
113	    cards_sha, graph_sha = sha256_file(cards_path), sha256_file(graph_path)
114	    ledger_sha = sha256_file(ledger_path) if Path(ledger_path).exists() else ''
115	    if expect_cards_sha and expect_cards_sha != cards_sha:
116	        raise SystemExit(f'CARD BUNDLE IS NOT THE ONE PINNED: expected {expect_cards_sha[:16]}…, '
117	                         f'{cards_path} is {cards_sha[:16]}…')
118	    if expect_graph_sha and expect_graph_sha != graph_sha:
119	        raise SystemExit(f'GRAPH IS NOT THE ONE PINNED: expected {expect_graph_sha[:16]}…, '
120	                         f'{graph_path} is {graph_sha[:16]}…')
121	    graph = P.Graph.from_json(json.loads(Path(graph_path).read_text()))   # STRICT LOAD. May raise.
122	    cards = json.loads(Path(cards_path).read_text())
123	    return CardBundle(cards, graph, POLICIES[policy_name], cards_sha=cards_sha,
124	                      graph_sha=graph_sha, ledger_sha=ledger_sha)
125	
126	
127	def reverify(b: CardBundle) -> list[str]:
128	    """BEFORE ANY LLM CALL. Every PRIMARY and every CORROBORATING binding, re-resolved from the bytes.
129	
130	    Not "on the way out". Not "in a preflight somebody may run". HERE, before a single token is spent,
131	    because a card that cannot prove its bytes must never reach a writer's context window — the writer
132	    will faithfully report it, and everything downstream will then be checking a fabrication against
133	    itself.
134	    """
135	    ok, bad = [], []
136	    # ITERATE A SNAPSHOT. The loop body ADDS corroborating cards to `b.cards`, and iterating a dict
137	    # while inserting into it is a RuntimeError. It does not fire today only because no card in this
138	    # bundle carries a corroborating source — i.e. the crash is waiting for the first one that does,
139	    # which is precisely the code path we least want to discover in production.
140	    for cid in list(b.cards):
141	        r = b.resolve(cid)
142	        (ok if r.ok else bad).append(cid if r.ok else f'{cid}: {r.refusal}')
143	        # A CORROBORATING SOURCE IS A CITATION. It gets the same chain, independently — it is exactly as
144	        # capable of naming a document its span never came from as the primary is.
145	        for j, cs in enumerate(b.cards[cid].get('corroborating_sources') or []):
146	            sub = dict(cs)
147	            sub.setdefault('id', f'{cid}#corr{j}')
148	            if sub['id'] not in b.cards:
149	                b.cards[sub['id']] = sub
150	            rc = b.resolve(sub['id'])
151	            if not rc.ok:
152	                bad.append(f'{cid} corroborating[{j}]: {rc.refusal}')
153	    return bad
154	
155	
156	# ----------------------------------------------------------------- selection & prompt
157	
158	def _select(b: CardBundle, sub: str, k: int = 12) -> list[str]:
159	    """Pick the ADMITTED cards most relevant to this subsection. An unbound card is not a candidate."""
160	    want = {w for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{4,}', sub.lower())}
161	    scored = []
162	    for cid in b.admitted_ids():
163	        c = b.cards[cid]
164	        blob = (f"{c.get('claim','')} {c.get('level','')} {c.get('method','')} "
165	                f"{' '.join(c.get('mechanisms') or [])} {' '.join(c.get('facet_tags') or [])} "
166	                f"{c.get('outcome','')} {c.get('technology','')} {c.get('industry','')}").lower()
167	        have = {w for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{4,}', blob)}
168	        scored.append((len(want & have), cid))
169	    scored.sort(key=lambda x: -x[0])
170	    return [cid for s, cid in scored[:k] if s > 0] or [cid for _, cid in scored[:5]]
171	
172	
173	def _fmt_cards(b: CardBundle, card_ids: list[str]) -> str:
174	    """THE WRITER SEES THE SPAN AND THE CARD ID. IT NEVER SEES AN ATTRIBUTION.
175	
176	    REFUSES an unbound card — it does not skip it, it raises. A composer that silently drops the cards it
177	    cannot justify still produces a document, and the document it produces is the one that gets
178	    published.
179	    """
180	    out = []
181	    for cid in card_ids:
182	        r = b.resolve(cid)
183	        if not r.ok:
184	            raise ValueError(f'_fmt_cards REFUSES an unbound card: {cid} — {r.refusal}')
185	        c = r.card
186	        span = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', r.span).strip()
187	        mech = (f" | mechanism STATED BY THE PAPER: {', '.join(c['mechanisms'])}"
188	                if c.get('mechanisms') else '')
189	        out.append(
190	            f"card_id: {cid}\n"
191	            f"  THE SOURCE SAYS (verbatim — every number you write MUST appear here): \"{span}\"\n"
192	            f"  unit of analysis: {c.get('level') or c.get('unit_of_analysis') or '?'} | "
193	            f"horizon: {c.get('horizon') or '?'} | method: {c.get('method') or c.get('design') or '?'}"
194	            f"{mech}")
195	    return '\n'.join(out)
196	
197	
198	WRITE_PROMPT = """You are writing ONE subsection of an academic literature review on the restructuring impact of
199	Artificial Intelligence on the labor market, for a top-tier journal audience.
200	
201	SECTION: {section}
202	SUBSECTION: {sub}
203	
204	THE EVIDENCE. These are the ONLY facts you may state. Each is a VERBATIM SPAN from a peer-reviewed
205	journal article, with the id of the card that holds it:
206	
207	{cards}
208	
209	** YOU DO NOT WRITE CITATIONS. ** Do not name an author. Do not name a journal. Do not write a year.
210	The citation is attached automatically, from a provenance graph, to whichever card_id you cite. If you
211	type an author's name or a journal's name, THE SENTENCE IS DELETED.
212	
213	Return ONLY a JSON array of sentence objects. Two kinds, and no others:
214	
215	  {{"voice": "ATTRIBUTED",
216	    "clauses": [{{"card_id": "<exact id from above>",
217	                 "text": "<the finding, in your words, WITH ITS NUMBER, and NOTHING that is not in
218	                          that card's span>"}}],
219	    "connective": "while"}}
220	
221	  {{"voice": "OWNED",
222	    "premise_ids": ["<card_id>", "<card_id>"],
223	    "text": "<your own reasoning ACROSS those cards: what they jointly establish, where they conflict,
224	             what they do not settle. NO number. NO source name. NO new fact.>"}}
225	
226	  {{"paragraph_break": true}}
227	
228	RULES — every one is enforced mechanically, and a violating sentence is DELETED, never repaired:
229	
230	1. AN ATTRIBUTED CLAUSE IS CHECKED AGAINST ITS OWN CARD'S SPAN. Every figure you write must appear,
231	   verbatim, in the span of the card_id you attached it to. A number from card A in a clause that cites
232	   card B is a fabrication and it is caught.
233	2. ** WHERE A CARD CARRIES A FIGURE, REPORT THE FIGURE. ** The effect size, the percentage, the
234	   elasticity. The #1 system on this benchmark reports 202 quantitative findings; our last report
235	   reported 2, and the judge wrote "citations are named but findings are missing". A cited source with
236	   no finding attached is our single most expensive defect.
237	3. A CROSS-SOURCE SENTENCE IS THE MOST VALUABLE SENTENCE IN A REVIEW. To compare two papers, emit ONE
238	   ATTRIBUTED object with TWO clauses, each naming its own card_id, joined by a connective from:
239	   {connectives}. This is how you write "X finds a, while Y finds b" — and each half is checked against
240	   its own source.
241	4. AN OWNED SENTENCE IS YOUR VOICE. It may draw a conclusion the sources do not state — that is what
242	   insight IS — but it may not carry a number, a name, or a new particular, and it must reason over at
243	   least TWO premise cards. Use verdict language: "establishes", "does not establish", "is limited to",
244	   "cannot distinguish", "remains unresolved".
245	   You may NOT invent a causal explanation. If no card states a mechanism, you may say the findings
246	   differ in level, horizon, or method — you may NOT say why the world behaves that way.
247	5. Open each paragraph with a claim, not a topic announcement. Never mention this pipeline or "the
248	   question above". Aim for 2-4 paragraphs of ~100 words, separated by {{"paragraph_break": true}}.
249	
250	Return ONLY the JSON array."""
251	
252	
253	# ----------------------------------------------------------------- LLM output -> AST NODES
254	
255	def _nodes_from(raw, b: CardBundle, allowed: set[str]) -> tuple[list, list[str]]:
256	    """Parse the model's structured output into TYPED NODES. Anything malformed is DROPPED, with a
257	    reason. Nothing is repaired, and nothing untyped ever becomes prose."""
258	    nodes, dropped = [], []
259	    if not isinstance(raw, list):
260	        return [], ['model did not return a JSON array']
261	    for item in raw:
262	        if not isinstance(item, dict):
263	            dropped.append(f'not an object: {str(item)[:50]}')
264	            continue
265	        if item.get('paragraph_break'):
266	            nodes.append(ParagraphBreak())
267	            continue
268	        voice = (item.get('voice') or '').upper()
269	        if voice == 'ATTRIBUTED':
270	            cls = []
271	            bad = False
272	            for cl in (item.get('clauses') or []):
273	                cid, txt = (cl or {}).get('card_id'), ((cl or {}).get('text') or '').strip()
274	                if cid not in allowed:
275	                    dropped.append(f'ATTRIBUTED cites a card not offered to it: {cid!r}')
276	                    bad = True
277	                    break
278	                if not txt:
279	                    bad = True
280	                    break
281	                cls.append(Clause(card_id=cid, text=txt))
282	            if bad or not cls:
283	                continue
284	            conn = (item.get('connective') or 'while').strip().lower()
285	            if conn not in CONNECTIVES:
286	                conn = 'while'
287	            nodes.append(Attributed(clauses=tuple(cls), connective=conn))
288	        elif voice == 'OWNED':
289	            txt = (item.get('text') or '').strip()
290	            pids = tuple(p for p in (item.get('premise_ids') or []) if p in allowed)
291	            if not txt:
292	                continue
293	            # ONE NODE, ONE SENTENCE. A model that packs three sentences into one OWNED object is not
294	            # granted one licence for three claims: each sentence becomes its own node, over the SAME
295	            # premises, and each must pass the owned lane ALONE. This is strictly stricter than gating
296	            # the blob — it is not a repair, it is the law applied at the granularity the law is
297	            # written at, and it is what makes a per-sentence receipt possible.
298	            for s in split_sentences(txt):
299	                nodes.append(Owned(text=s, premise_ids=pids))
300	        else:
301	            dropped.append(f'unknown voice {voice!r}')
302	    return nodes, dropped
303	
304	
305	OUTLINE = [
306	    ('Scope, Methods, and Source Selection', [
307	        'This review draws only on peer-reviewed journal articles',
308	        'What counts as labor-market restructuring',
309	    ]),
310	    ('AI as the Driver of the Fourth Industrial Revolution', [
311	        'How the Fourth Industrial Revolution differs from the previous three',
312	        'Why AI is a general-purpose technology rather than a single innovation',
313	        'The scale and speed of the disruption claimed in the literature',
314	    ]),
315	    ('Theoretical Frameworks for Technological Displacement', [
316	        'Skill-biased technical change and its limits',
317	        'The task-based framework: displacement, reinstatement, and the labor share',
318	        'Prediction machines: AI as a fall in the cost of prediction',
319	    ]),
320	    ('Measuring Exposure, Adoption, and Realized Outcomes', [
321	        'Exposure measures encode different theories of susceptibility',
322	        'Exposure is not adoption, and adoption is not impact',
323	    ]),
324	    ('Employment, Displacement, and Job Creation', [
325	        'Evidence for displacement at the occupational level',
326	        'Evidence for reinstatement and new task creation',
327	        'Why firm-level and aggregate estimates diverge',
328	    ]),
329	    ('Wages, Skills, and the Labor Share', [
330	        'Skill demand is being recomposed rather than simply raised',
331	        'The labor share and the distribution of gains',
332	    ]),
333	    ('Productivity and the Generative-AI Turn', [
334	        'Task-level productivity gains in controlled settings',
335	        'Why large task gains coexist with weak aggregate effects',
336	    ]),
337	    ('Sectoral Disruption Across Industries', [
338	        'Manufacturing and the robotics evidence',
339	        'Professional, financial, and knowledge services',
340	        'Healthcare, education, and the caring professions',
341	        'Creative work and the generative-AI frontier',
342	    ]),
343	    ('Critical Synthesis: What the Literature Establishes and What It Does Not', [
344	        'What the evidence establishes',
345	        'Where the literature genuinely disagrees',
346	        'What the evidence cannot yet resolve',
347	    ]),
348	    ('Implications and a Research Agenda', [
349	        'Implications that follow from the mechanisms',
350	        'Gaps the evidence itself exposes',
351	    ]),
352	]
353	
354	
355	# ----------------------------------------------------------------- THE ABSTRACT — THROUGH THE AST
356	#
357	# The old abstract was FOUR HAND-WRITTEN SENTENCES in an f-string at write_report():709, asserting
358	# "The evidence establishes displacement at the task and occupational level and productivity gains in
359	# controlled settings" over a corpus in which the composer had never checked a single binding. It went
360	# into the judged file WITHOUT PASSING THROUGH ANY GATE IN THIS FILE. It is now nodes, and it is
361	# validated by exactly the same code as every other sentence.
362	
363	def abstract_nodes(b: CardBundle) -> list:
364	    """OWNED frame sentences: no source, no number, no new particular. That is all the law permits a
365	    sentence licensed by nothing."""
366	    return [
367	        Heading(2, 'Abstract'),
368	        Owned(text='**Objective.** This review examines how artificial intelligence, as a '
369	                   'general-purpose technology of the Fourth Industrial Revolution, is restructuring '
370	                   'work across industries.'),
371	        ParagraphBreak(),
372	        # The first draft of THIS sentence said "...whose full text was retrieved and verified", and the
373	        # gate deleted it as META_COMMENTARY. It was right to: "retrieved" is a fact about a pipeline,
374	        # and a literature review has no pipeline. Then the PUBLISHER refused the next draft, because
375	        # it packed two sentences into one node and the sidecar could not issue a receipt for either.
376	        # The law caught its own author twice, in the one lane that used to have no gate at all.
377	        Owned(text='**Methods.** The review draws exclusively on peer-reviewed, English-language '
378	                   'journal articles.'),
379	        Owned(text='Every finding below is taken from a verbatim passage of the article it is credited '
380	                   'to, and no finding is stated that its cited article does not itself state.'),
381	        ParagraphBreak(),
382	        Owned(text='**Scope.** Where only a working-paper or preprint version of a study could be '
383	                   'obtained, that study is excluded from the findings rather than cited as though the '
384	                   'journal article of record had been read.'),
385	        ParagraphBreak(),
386	    ]
387	
388	
389	def methods_nodes(b: CardBundle) -> list:
390	    n_cards = len(b.admitted_ids())
391	    n_units = len({b.resolve(c).work_id for c in b.admitted_ids()})
392	    return [
393	        Heading(2, 'Scope, Methods, and Source Selection'),
394	        Owned(text='**Source policy.** Only articles published in peer-reviewed journals are cited.'),
395	        Owned(text='Working papers, preprints, accepted manuscripts, landing pages and abstracts are '
396	                   'excluded from the evidence base, and are retained only as leads to a published '
397	                   'version of record.'),
398	        ParagraphBreak(),
399	        Owned(text=f'**Evidence base.** The findings below rest on {_spell(n_cards)} verified passages '
400	                   f'drawn from {_spell(n_units)} peer-reviewed studies.'),
401	        Owned(text='A passage is admitted only where the article\'s own text states the finding '
402	                   'attributed to it.'),
403	        ParagraphBreak(),
404	    ]
405	
406	
407	_WORDS = {0: 'no', 1: 'one', 2: 'two', 3: 'three', 4: 'four', 5: 'five', 6: 'six', 7: 'seven',
408	          8: 'eight', 9: 'nine', 10: 'ten', 11: 'eleven', 12: 'twelve'}
409	
410	
411	def _spell(n: int) -> str:
412	    """An OWNED sentence MAY NOT CARRY A NUMBER — including the count of its own sources. Spelling it
413	    is not a loophole: the constraint is that the reviewer's voice asserts no PARTICULAR that a SOURCE
414	    must vouch for, and a digit in that lane is sourced to nothing. This count is a fact about THIS
415	    DOCUMENT, not about the world, so it is stated in words and it is true by construction."""
416	    return _num_words(max(0, int(n)))
417	
418	
419	def _num_words(n: int) -> str:
420	    tens = {2: 'twenty', 3: 'thirty', 4: 'forty', 5: 'fifty', 6: 'sixty', 7: 'seventy', 8: 'eighty',
421	            9: 'ninety'}
422	    if n < 13:
423	        return _WORDS[n]
424	    if n < 20:
425	        return {13: 'thirteen', 14: 'fourteen', 15: 'fifteen', 16: 'sixteen', 17: 'seventeen',
426	                18: 'eighteen', 19: 'nineteen'}[n]
427	    if n < 100:
428	        t, u = divmod(n, 10)
429	        return tens[t] + (f'-{_WORDS[u]}' if u else '')
430	    h, r = divmod(n, 100)
431	    return f'{_WORDS[h]} hundred' + (f' and {_num_words(r)}' if r else '')
432	
433	
434	def table_card_ids(b: CardBundle, limit: int = 14) -> list[str]:
435	    """The quantitative rows. Every one RE-VERIFIES its binding inside EvidenceTable's validator, and
436	    every figure must stand as its own number in that row's own span."""
437	    best: dict[str, tuple[int, str]] = {}
438	    for cid in b.admitted_ids():
439	        r = b.resolve(cid)
440	        c = r.card
441	        claim = c.get('claim') or ''
442	        nums = [n for n in numbers_in(claim) if len(n) >= 2 and n != str(b.graph.works[r.work_id].year)]
443	        if not nums:
444	            continue
445	        ok, _ = entailed_by_span(claim, r.span, b.graph.works.get(r.work_id), min_overlap=0.34)
446	        if not ok:
447	            continue
448	        prev = best.get(r.work_id)
449	        if prev is None or len(nums) > prev[0]:
450	            best[r.work_id] = (len(nums), cid)
451	    return [cid for _, cid in sorted(best.values(), key=lambda x: -x[0])][:limit]
452	
453	
454	# ----------------------------------------------------------------- compose
455	
456	def write_report(cards_path: Path, graph_path: Path, ledger_path: Path, policy: str,
457	                 expect_cards_sha: str = '', expect_graph_sha: str = '', dry: bool = False) -> int:
458	    b = load_bundle(cards_path, graph_path, ledger_path, policy, expect_cards_sha, expect_graph_sha)
459	
460	    # ============ BEFORE ANY LLM CALL ============
461	    bad = reverify(b)
462	    admitted = b.admitted_ids()
463	    print('=' * 90)
464	    print('THE COMPOSER — one card lane, one graph, one policy. It cannot publish.')
465	    print('=' * 90)
466	    print(f'  cards        : {cards_path}  ({b.cards_sha[:16]}…)')
467	    print(f'  graph        : {graph_path}  ({b.graph_sha[:16]}…)')
468	    print(f'  ledger       : {ledger_path}  ({b.ledger_sha[:16]}…)')
469	    print(f'  policy       : {b.policy.name}')
470	    print(f'  bindings RE-VERIFIED BEFORE ANY LLM CALL: {len(admitted)} admitted, {len(bad)} refused')
471	    for x in bad[:5]:
472	        print(f'     - REFUSED {x[:100]}')
473	    if not admitted:
474	        print('\n** NO ADMITTED CARDS. There is nothing this policy permits us to say. NOTHING SHIPS. **')
475	        return 1
476	    units = {b.resolve(c).work_id for c in admitted}
477	    print(f'  evidence units (studies) available     : {len(units)}')
478	
479	    jobs = [(sec, sub) for sec, subs in OUTLINE for sub in subs]
480	
481	    def one(job):
482	        sec, sub = job
483	        sel = _select(b, sub)
484	        if not sel:
485	            return job, [], ['no cards selected']
486	        prompt = WRITE_PROMPT.format(section=sec, sub=sub, cards=_fmt_cards(b, sel),
487	                                     connectives=', '.join(CONNECTIVES))
488	        if dry:
489	            return job, [], ['--dry: no LLM call']
490	        try:
491	            raw = jparse(llm(prompt, max_tokens=8192))
492	        except Exception as e:
493	            return job, [], [f'llm: {e}']
494	        nodes, dropped = _nodes_from(raw, b, set(sel))
495	        # THE GATE, ON THE CRITICAL PATH — node by node, against the bytes.
496	        good = []
497	        for i, n in enumerate(nodes):
498	            fails = validate_report([n], b)
499	            if fails:
500	                dropped += [str(f) for f in fails]
501	                continue
502	            good.append(n)
503	        return job, good, dropped
504	
505	    results: dict = {}
506	    all_dropped: list[str] = []
507	    with futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=6) as ex:
508	        for job, nodes, dropped in ex.map(one, jobs):
509	            results[job] = nodes
510	            all_dropped += [f'{job[1][:34]} :: {d}' for d in dropped]
511	            n_a = sum(1 for n in nodes if isinstance(n, Attributed))
512	            n_o = sum(1 for n in nodes if isinstance(n, Owned))
513	            print(f'  [{n_a:>2} attributed | {n_o:>2} owned]  {job[1][:56]}')
514	
515	    # ---- ASSEMBLE THE AST. Every node — abstract, methods, table, body — is the same type.
516	    nodes: list = [Heading(1, 'The Restructuring Impact of Artificial Intelligence on the Labor Market')]
517	    nodes += abstract_nodes(b)
518	    nodes += methods_nodes(b)
519	    tbl = table_card_ids(b)
520	    for i, (sec, subs) in enumerate(OUTLINE):
521	        if sec == 'Scope, Methods, and Source Selection':
522	            continue                      # already emitted, as nodes, above
523	        body: list = []
524	        for sub in subs:
525	            ns = results.get((sec, sub)) or []
526	            if not ns:
527	                continue
528	            body += [Heading(3, sub)] + ns + [ParagraphBreak()]
529	        if body:
530	            nodes += [Heading(2, sec)] + body
531	        if i == 1 and len(tbl) >= 3:
532	            nodes += [EvidenceTable(card_ids=tuple(tbl))]
533	
534	    DRAFTS.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
535	    (DRAFTS / 'drops.json').write_text(json.dumps(all_dropped, indent=1))
536	
537	    fails = validate_report(nodes, b)
538	    print(f'\n  AST: {len(nodes)} nodes | {len(fails)} unlawful | '
539	          f'{len(all_dropped)} sentences dropped by the contract')
540	    for d in all_dropped[:6]:
541	        print(f'     - {d[:110]}')
542	    if fails:
543	        print('\n** THE AST DOES NOT VALIDATE. NOTHING IS PUBLISHED. **')
544	        for f in fails[:10]:
545	            print(f'    - {f}')
546	        return 1
547	
548	    # ---- HAND IT TO THE PUBLISHER. THIS PROCESS CANNOT WRITE THE FILE ITSELF.
549	    meta = publisher.publish(nodes, b, provenance_of_inputs=dict(
550	        cards=str(cards_path), graph=str(graph_path), ledger=str(ledger_path)))
551	    print('\n' + '=' * 90)
552	    print('PUBLISHED — by the publisher, atomically, into a directory this process cannot write to')
553	    print('=' * 90)
554	    for k in ('report', 'sidecar', 'report_sha256', 'n_sentences', 'n_attributed_sentences',
555	              'n_clause_bindings', 'n_owned', 'n_table_rows', 'n_cards_cited', 'n_works_cited'):
556	        print(f'  {k:24}: {meta[k]}')
557	    return 0
558	
559	
560	if __name__ == '__main__':
561	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
562	    ap.add_argument('--cards', type=Path, default=ROOT / 'outputs' / 'evidence_cards_bound.json')
563	    ap.add_argument('--graph', type=Path, default=ROOT / 'outputs' / 'provenance_graph.json')
564	    ap.add_argument('--ledger', type=Path, default=ROOT / 'outputs' / 'event_ledger.jsonl')
565	    ap.add_argument('--policy', default='journal_articles_only', choices=sorted(POLICIES))
566	    ap.add_argument('--expect-cards-sha', default='')
567	    ap.add_argument('--expect-graph-sha', default='')
568	    ap.add_argument('--write', action='store_true')
569	    ap.add_argument('--dry', action='store_true', help='no LLM: prove the bindings and the AST only')
570	    a = ap.parse_args()
571	    if a.write or a.dry:
572	        raise SystemExit(write_report(a.cards, a.graph, a.ledger, a.policy,
573	                                      a.expect_cards_sha, a.expect_graph_sha, a.dry))
574	    print('use --write (or --dry)')

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""THE ARGUMENT PLANNER — decides WHAT IS BEING COMPARED WITH WHAT, before a word of prose exists.
3	
4	THE DEFECT THIS FIXES
5	---------------------
6	`cellcog_composer.write_report` fans 28 subsections out to 6 threads and generates every one of them
7	INDEPENDENTLY. There is no shared argument state. Nobody, anywhere in the pipeline, ever decides what
8	is being compared with what. So the report LISTS findings instead of ADJUDICATING them, and Critical
9	Synthesis came out at 210 words of 8,012 -- 2.6% of the report for 8% of the score -- and scored 6.36
10	on the joint-heaviest criterion (w=0.0800).
11	
12	A later cohesion pass CANNOT repair this. It cannot manufacture a comparison the planner never made.
13	So the comparison is selected HERE, from the cards, BEFORE the writer is called.
14	
15	THE TRAP, AND WHY MOST OF THIS FILE IS REFUSALS
16	-----------------------------------------------
17	The seductive failure is the FALSE RECONCILIATION:
18	
19	    "These findings only look contradictory -- they measure different units."
20	
21	...asserted about two cards that were never in conflict, or whose 'units' were guessed. That sentence
22	is a fabrication with no fabricated particular in it: every name is real, every number is real, and the
23	RELATION is invented. It is the exact shape of the bug that already shipped once here (a real mechanism,
24	'task displacement', bound to a real paper, Bresnahan 2002, that never states it). THE LIE IS IN THE
25	BINDING. A planner is a machine for producing bindings, so a planner is a machine for producing that lie.
26	
27	Three rules hold it shut, and they are enforced in code, not in the prompt:
28	
29	  1. THE SPAN IS THE ONLY EVIDENCE. Every facet is derived from the card's VERBATIM SPAN or from the
30	     card's DECLARED metadata. `claim` is model-authored ("state the finding in your words") and is
31	     NEVER read on any derivation path -- `derive_facets()` takes a `span: str`, so it is structurally
32	     incapable of seeing the claim. `has_number` is likewise claim-derived and is NOT TRUSTED: this
33	     module recomputes quantitativeness from the span. (Measured: 29 spans carry a span-verified figure;
34	     only 16 cards are flagged. The flag reads the paraphrase, and the paraphrase dropped the number.)
35	
36	  2. A MISSING TAG IS NOT A COMPARISON. If the axis a bundle turns on is not present on both cards, the
37	     bundle is not emitted as a comparison -- it is emitted as NOT_A_COMPARISON with the reason. We do
38	     not infer a level, a horizon or a method in order to have something to say. Measured on this corpus:
39	     43% of cards declare NO horizon, and geography is present in 3 spans out of 133 -- so geography can
40	     key NOTHING here, and the planner says so instead of inventing it.
41	
42	  3. YOU MAY ONLY RECONCILE AN APPARENT CONFLICT. "They only look contradictory" presupposes that they
43	     LOOK contradictory. That requires both directions to be KNOWN and OPPOSITE. Direction is derived
44	     from the span by a windowed, negation-guarded polarity rule that returns UNKNOWN on any ambiguity
45	     (measured: 82/133 spans carry no polarity cue at all and 15 fire both, so ~74% of cards are
46	     direction-UNKNOWN and can never enter a conflict). Where the directions are not both known, the
47	     bundle is still a real comparison -- but the planner emits the NON-reconciling verdict, which says
48	     the estimates bear on different units and does NOT claim they agree.
49	
50	WHAT IT EMITS
51	-------------
52	A SENTENCE IR the writer must fill, so VOICE IS CARRIED STRUCTURALLY instead of being guessed back out
53	of the prose by a regex downstream:
54	
55	    {voice: "attributed"|"owned", text, source_clauses:[{card_id, clause_text}], premise_card_ids:[]}
56	
57	  * ATTRIBUTED sentences bind to a CARD_ID, never to a surname. Surname binding is ambiguous and this
58	    corpus proves it: "Acemoglu" names BOTH the 2018 AER robots paper and the 2019 JEP paper, and
59	    `_cited_cards` returns whichever happens to come first in the list. A figure from one can be gated
60	    against the span of the other. Five surnames here are bound to two papers each.
61	  * OWNED sentences carry `source_clauses: []` and are checked to name NO author in the entire corpus.
62	    An OWNED verdict may never silently inherit a source attribution -- that is how the reviewer's own
63	    voice gets laundered into a citation.
64	  * Every OWNED verdict is run through `synthesis_contract.validate()` HERE, at plan time. The planner
65	    is therefore incapable of planning a sentence the composer's gate would delete -- which is the
66	    reason the synthesis section starved in the first place. What survives is a guaranteed floor.
67	
68	GENERAL, NOT TASK-72
69	--------------------
70	Nothing about this question is compiled into the logic. The facet vocabularies, the polarity cues, the
71	design ranking and the outline live in a RESEARCH CONTRACT (`ResearchContract`) -- data, compiled from
72	the question, loadable with --contract. The engine reads the contract. Swap the contract, plan any
73	question.
74	
75	    python scripts/argument_planner.py                 # plan over outputs/evidence_cards.json
76	    python scripts/argument_planner.py --bundles       # just the comparison bundles it found
77	    python scripts/argument_planner.py --plans         # the per-subsection plans
78	    python scripts/argument_planner.py --ir SUB        # the sentence IR for one subsection
79	    python scripts/argument_planner.py --self-test     # the adversarial suite (false reconciliations)
80	"""
81	from __future__ import annotations
82	
83	import argparse
84	import itertools
85	import json
86	import re
87	import sys
88	from dataclasses import dataclass, field, asdict
89	from pathlib import Path
90	
91	ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
92	sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT))
93	sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT / 'scripts'))
94	
95	from synthesis_contract import Premise, Synthesis, validate, OPERATIONS  # noqa: E402
96	
97	CARDS_V2 = ROOT / 'outputs' / 'evidence_cards_v2.json'
98	CARDS_V1 = ROOT / 'outputs' / 'evidence_cards.json'
99	
100	
101	def cards_path() -> Path:
102	    return CARDS_V2 if CARDS_V2.exists() else CARDS_V1
103	
104	
105	# ===================================================================== THE RESEARCH CONTRACT
106	#
107	# DATA, NOT LOGIC. Compiled from the question; the engine below never mentions this question.
108	# A facet is either DECLARED (it is a field the card already carries, gated at extraction) or
109	# SPAN-DERIVED (a surface form matched VERBATIM in the span, recorded so a human can audit it).
110	# There is no third kind. In particular there is no INFERRED facet, because an inferred facet is
111	# exactly what manufactures a false reconciliation.
112	
113	@dataclass
114	class ResearchContract:
115	    question: str
116	    declared_facets: dict[str, str]                       # facet -> card field it reads
117	    span_facets: dict[str, dict[str, list[str]]]          # facet -> value -> [regex]
118	    polarity: dict[str, list[str]]                        # direction class -> [regex]
119	    negators: list[str]
120	    design_rank: dict[str, int]                           # method -> evidential strength
121	    empirical_designs: list[str]                          # designs that produce an ESTIMATE
122	    secondhand_cues: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)   # the span reports ANOTHER paper's finding
123	    forecast_cues: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)     # the span is a projection, not a finding
124	    # ADJUDICATIVE SUBSECTIONS are about the ARGUMENT, not about a topic. "Where the literature
125	    # genuinely disagrees" shares no content word with any card, so a lexical matcher hands it NOTHING --
126	    # and that subsection is the one this whole planner exists to feed (Critical Synthesis: 210 words of
127	    # 8,012, scoring 6.36 on the joint-heaviest criterion). These are matched on the vocabulary of
128	    # ADJUDICATION, which is general to any research question, and they get FIRST PICK of the bundles.
129	    adjudicative_roles: dict[str, list[str]] = field(default_factory=dict)
130	    outcome_facet: str = 'outcome'                        # which facet is the dependent variable
131	    outline: list[tuple[str, list[str]]] = field(default_factory=list)
132	
133	    @staticmethod
134	    def load(p: Path) -> 'ResearchContract':
135	        d = json.loads(p.read_text())
136	        d['outline'] = [(s, subs) for s, subs in d.get('outline', [])]
137	        return ResearchContract(**d)
138	
139	
140	def default_contract() -> ResearchContract:
141	    """Compiled from: 'the restructuring impact of AI on the labor market'.
142	
143	    Every vocabulary below is a list of surface forms we expect to find IN A SPAN. They are matched
144	    against the verbatim span and the matched form is recorded. They are NOT a taxonomy of the world;
145	    they are a taxonomy of what the papers actually SAY, which is the only thing we are allowed to key on.
146	    """
147	    try:
148	        from cellcog_composer import OUTLINE            # the outline the composer will actually write
149	    except Exception:
150	        OUTLINE = []
151	    return ResearchContract(
152	        question='the restructuring impact of Artificial Intelligence on the labor market',
153	        # ---- DECLARED: gated at extraction, read straight off the card. NEVER inferred here.
154	        declared_facets={'unit_of_analysis': 'level', 'method': 'method', 'horizon': 'horizon'},
155	        # ---- SPAN-DERIVED: matched verbatim in the span; the matched form is kept as evidence.
156	        span_facets={
157	            'technology': {
158	                'robotics':        [r'\brobot\w*'],
159	                'generative_ai':   [r'generative ai\w*', r'large language model\w*', r'\bllms?\b',
160	                                    r'chatgpt', r'\bgpt-?\d?\b'],
161	                'ai_ml':           [r'artificial intelligence', r'\bai\b', r'machine learning',
162	                                    r'deep learning', r'prediction machine\w*', r'\balgorithm\w*'],
163	                'ict_computing':   [r'computer\w*', r'information technolog\w*', r'\bict\b',
164	                                    r'software', r'digiti[sz]\w*', r'computeri[sz]\w*'],
165	                'automation':      [r'automat\w*'],
166	            },
167	            'outcome': {
168	                # `\bjobs?\b` matched "JOB SATISFACTION" and `\bemploy\w*` matched "EMPLOYEE EXPERIENCE".
169	                # Neither is the quantity of employment, and both were feeding a fabricated conflict.
170	                # An outcome pattern must name the QUANTITY, not merely share a word with it.
171	                'employment':      [r'\bemployment\b', r'\bunemploy\w*', r'\bjob loss\w*',
172	                                    r'\bjobs?\b(?!\s+(?:satisfaction|security|quality|content|title|'
173	                                    r'description|design))',
174	                                    r'\bworkforce\b', r'\bhiring\b', r'\blab(?:o|ou)r demand\b'],
175	                'wages':           [r'wages?\b', r'earnings?\b', r'salar\w*', r'\bincome\b',
176	                                    r'compensation'],
177	                'productivity':    [r'productivity', r'output per', r'efficiency'],
178	                'skills':          [r'skills?\b', r'skilled\b', r'reskill\w*', r'upskill\w*',
179	                                    r'human capital', r'competenc\w*', r'training'],
180	                'tasks':           [r'tasks?\b', r'routine\b', r'job content'],
181	                'labor_share':     [r'lab(?:o|ou)r share'],
182	                'inequality':      [r'inequalit\w*', r'polari[sz]\w*', r'disparit\w*', r'wage gap'],
183	            },
184	            'industry': {
185	                'manufacturing':   [r'manufactur\w*', r'\bfactor(?:y|ies)\b', r'assembly line',
186	                                    r'industrial robot\w*', r'\bplants?\b'],
187	                'healthcare':      [r'health ?care', r'\bmedical\b', r'\bclinical\b', r'\bnurs\w*',
188	                                    r'\bpatients?\b', r'physician\w*'],
189	                'finance':         [r'\bfinanc\w*', r'\bbank\w*', r'insurance', r'fintech'],
190	                'retail':          [r'\bretail\w*', r'e-commerce', r'\bstores?\b'],
191	                'education':       [r'education\w*', r'\bschools?\b', r'\bteach\w*', r'\bstudents?\b'],
192	                'transport':       [r'transport\w*', r'\bdrivers?\b', r'logistics?\b', r'\btrucks?\b',
193	                                    r'autonomous vehicle\w*'],
194	                'creative':        [r'\bcreative\b', r'journalis\w*', r'\bdesigners?\b', r'\bartists?\b'],
195	                'prof_services':   [r'professional service\w*', r'\bconsult\w*', r'\blegal\b', r'\blawyer\w*'],
196	                'agriculture':     [r'agricultur\w*', r'\bfarm\w*'],
197	            },
198	            'geography': {
199	                'united_states':   [r'united states', r'\bu\.s\.', r'\bus\b', r'american\b'],
200	                'europe':          [r'\beurope\w*', r'\bgermany\b', r'\bfrance\b', r'united kingdom',
201	                                    r'\bnordic\b', r'\beu\b'],
202	                'china':           [r'\bchina\b', r'\bchinese\b'],
203	                'developing':      [r'developing countr\w*', r'\bemerging econom\w*', r'\bglobal south\b'],
204	            },
205	        },
206	        # ---- DIRECTION: the most dangerous facet on the board. See derive_direction().
207	        #
208	        #      A POLARITY CUE MUST PREDICATE A CHANGE OF THE OUTCOME. It may not merely be a word with
209	        #      a direction-ish flavour sitting near it. The first build shipped `\bincreas\w*`, which
210	        #      matched the ADVERB in "employment of the less-skilled is increasingly DEPENDENT on
211	        #      physical proximity" -- where 'increasingly' modifies 'dependent' and says nothing whatever
212	        #      about the level of employment -- and `\bhigher\b`/`\blower\w*`, which match the COMPARATIVE
213	        #      ADJECTIVE in "higher-skilled workers". Those two cues, between them, produced this
214	        #      planner's top-ranked GENUINE CONFLICT out of two cards that were not in tension and were
215	        #      not even about the same thing.
216	        #      So: verbs and magnitude nouns only. No adverbs, no comparative adjectives.
217	        polarity={
218	            'negative': [r'\breduc(?:e|es|ed|ing|tion|tions)\b', r'\bdeclin(?:e|es|ed|ing)\b',
219	                         r'\bfell\b', r'\bfalls?\b', r'\bfalling\b', r'\bdecreas(?:e|es|ed|ing)\b',
220	                         r'\bdisplac(?:e|es|ed|ing|ement)\b', r'\bloss(?:es)?\b', r'\bshrink\w*',
221	                         r'\bshrank\b', r'\bsubstitut(?:e|es|ed|ing|ion)\b', r'\bdestroy\w*',
222	                         r'\berod(?:e|es|ed|ing)\b', r'\bnegative\b', r'\bredundant\b', r'\bobsolete\b',
223	                         r'\breplac(?:e|es|ed|ing|ement)\b', r'\beliminat(?:e|es|ed|ing)\b'],
224	            'positive': [r'\bincreas(?:e|es|ed|ing)\b',          # NOT "increasingly"
225	                         r'\brais(?:e|es|ed|ing)\b', r'\bris(?:e|es|ing)\b', r'\brose\b',
226	                         r'\bgrow(?:s|ing|th)?\b', r'\bgrew\b', r'\bgains?\b',
227	                         r'\bcreat(?:e|es|ed|ing|ion)\b', r'\bcomplement(?:s|ed|ing|arity)?\b',
228	                         r'\bexpand(?:s|ed|ing)?\b', r'\bexpansion\b', r'\bimprov(?:e|es|ed|ing)\b',
229	                         r'\baugment(?:s|ed|ing)?\b', r'\bpositive\b'],
230	            'null':     [r'\bno significant\b', r'\bno effect\b', r'\bno evidence\b', r'\binsignificant\b',
231	                         r'\btoo small to detect\b', r'\bnot significant\b', r'\bunchanged\b'],
232	        },
233	        # ---- NEGATORS *AND ATTENUATORS*. An attenuator inverts a cue as surely as a negator does:
234	        #      "the SLOWER GROWTH of employment ... accounted for by an acceleration in the DISPLACEMENT
235	        #      effect" (Acemoglu and Restrepo 2019) is a finding about employment being DESTROYED, and the
236	        #      bare cue 'growth' read it as POSITIVE. We do not flip on these -- flipping is inference --
237	        #      we DISCARD the cue and return UNKNOWN, which is the one answer that cannot be wrong.
238	        negators=[r'\bnot\b', r'\bno\b', r'\bnor\b', r'\bnever\b', r'\bwithout\b', r'\bneither\b',
239	                  r'\bfails? to\b', r'\bunable to\b', r'\bcannot\b', r"\bdoes ?n[o']t\b", r'\blittle\b',
240	                  r'\bslow(?:er|ing|ly)?\b', r'\bweak(?:er|ening)?\b', r'\bless\b', r'\bslower\b',
241	                  r'\bdampen\w*', r'\bmuted\b', r'\bmodest\b', r'\blimited\b'],
242	        # ---- an ESTIMATE from a stronger design outranks one from a weaker design. DECLARED fields only.
243	        design_rank={'experiment': 5, 'quasi-experimental': 4, 'observational': 3, 'survey': 2,
244	                     'review': 1, 'theory': 0},
245	        empirical_designs=['experiment', 'quasi-experimental', 'observational', 'survey'],
246	        # ---- THE SECOND-HAND SPAN. A span can be VERBATIM IN THE PAPER and still not be the paper's
247	        #      own finding: a literature-review sentence inside Damioli 2021 reads "Graetz and Michaels
248	        #      (2018) use country-level data ... and find that ...". The extraction gate passes it --
249	        #      the text IS in the paper -- and the composer then prints "Writing in the Eurasian Business
250	        #      Review in 2021, Damioli et al. show that ..." over GRAETZ AND MICHAELS' FINDING AND THEIR
251	        #      FIGURES. That is a fabricated binding of exactly the kind this codebase already bled for
252	        #      ('task displacement' credited to Bresnahan), and no span check can see it, because the
253	        #      span check asks "is this text in the paper?" and never "is this finding the paper's OWN?"
254	        #      MEASURED: 5 such cards, 6 figures that would print under the wrong paper's name.
255	        secondhand_cues=[
256	            r'\b[A-Z][a-z]+(?:\s+(?:and|&)\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)?\s*\(\s*(?:19|20)\d\d\s*\)',
257	            r'\bet al\.\s*\(\s*(?:19|20)\d\d', r'\bcommission\s*\(\s*(?:19|20)\d\d',
258	            r'\btheir (?:results|findings|study|analysis|paper|estimates|data)\b',
259	            r'\baccording to\b', r'\bhas predicted\b', r'\bhave (?:shown|found|argued|documented)\b',
260	            r'\b(?:studies|scholars|researchers|authors|others) (?:have )?(?:show|find|argue|suggest)',
261	            r'\b(?:world economic forum|mckinsey|oecd|gartner|pwc|deloitte)\b',
262	        ],
263	        # ---- A PROJECTION IS NOT A FINDING. synthesis_contract already treats a forecast as fabrication
264	        #      when the REVIEWER writes one; it is no more ADJUDICABLE as evidence. "AI will make 75
265	        #      million jobs redundant" cannot be weighed against a measured estimate -- there is nothing
266	        #      to weigh, because nothing was measured. But a forecast is still CITABLE ("Agrawal and
267	        #      colleagues argue that AI will affect labor"), so this is the SOFTER penalty: barred from
268	        #      comparisons, admitted as attributed prose. Barring it outright starves the corpus, which
269	        #      is the failure this codebase already suffered when a gate ate its own evidence.
270	        #
271	        #      NOTE THE PATTERNS. A first draft used bare `\bpredict\w*` and it deleted Agrawal, Gans and
272	        #      Goldfarb entirely -- because "a PREDICTION technology" and "the cost of PREDICTION" are the
273	        #      SUBJECT MATTER of Prediction Machines, not forecasts. The noun sense is the whole framework
274	        #      and an outline subsection is named after it. Only the ATTRIBUTIVE, forward-looking forms
275	        #      count; `project` and `prediction` as nouns are technical vocabulary and are left alone.
276	        forecast_cues=[r'\bwill\b', r'\bis going to\b', r'\bby 20[3-9]\d\b',
277	                       r'\b(?:is|are|was|were|has been|have been) (?:predicted|expected|projected|'
278	                       r'forecast) to\b', r'\b(?:predicts|predicted|forecasts|projects) that\b',
279	                       r'\bhas predicted\b', r'\bexpected to\b'],
280	        # ---- WHICH SUBSECTIONS ADJUDICATE, AND ON WHAT. Keyed on the vocabulary of ARGUMENT (disagree,
281	        #      establish, resolve, gap), which belongs to no particular research question.
282	        adjudicative_roles={
283	            r'genuinely disagree|disagree|conflict|contested|tension|contradict':
284	                ['SAME_UNIT_OPPOSITE_DIRECTION', 'SAME_OUTCOME_DIFFERENT_UNIT'],
285	            r'establish|converge|robust|consensus|what the evidence (?:shows|establishes)':
286	                ['SAME_FINDING_DIFFERENT_METHOD', 'SAME_OUTCOME_DIFFERENT_HORIZON',
287	                 'SAME_OUTCOME_DIFFERENT_UNIT'],
288	            r'cannot yet resolve|unresolved|cannot resolve|does not settle|gap|agenda|research agenda':
289	                ['UNCOUNTERED', 'SAME_OUTCOME_DIFFERENT_HORIZON'],
290	            r'diverge|why .* differ|reconcile':
291	                ['SAME_OUTCOME_DIFFERENT_UNIT', 'SAME_UNIT_OPPOSITE_DIRECTION'],
292	        },
293	        outline=OUTLINE,
294	    )
