88	METHOD_CONTRAST_MATERIAL_AT = 3   # below this many works, demanding two designs is not material
89	MAX_CARD_REUSE = 2           # 222 slots drawn from 82 cards; one finding was used 8 times
90	
91	
92	# ===================================================================== the contract
93	
94	@dataclass
95	class Term:
96	    """A concept the matcher can look for: a label plus the surface forms it wears."""
97	    key: str
98	    label: str
99	    aliases: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
100	
101	
102	@dataclass
103	class Axis:
104	    """The rows of the coverage matrix. THE GENERALISATION OF 'INDUSTRIES'.
105	
106	    For a question about AI and work this is `Industry`. For one about Parkinson's it is `Disease
107	    stage`. For one about ADAS liability it is `Accident scenario`. The composer must never assume.
108	    """
109	    name: str = 'Subpopulation'
110	    values: list[Term] = field(default_factory=list)
111	
112	
113	@dataclass
114	class Contrast:
115	    """A tension the review is OBLIGED to draw. This is the shared argument state that 28
116	    independently-generated subsections did not have, and that no later cohesion pass can invent."""
117	    key: str
118	    label: str
119	    a: str
120	    b: str
121	    why: str = ''
122	
123	
124	@dataclass
125	class Facet:
126	    """An extraction facet the miner harvests. Derived from the question -- for task 72 one of these
127	    is the Fourth Industrial Revolution, but NOTHING about the 4IR is written in this file."""
128	    key: str
129	    label: str
130	    probe: str            # the question asked of every paper
131	    priority: int = 2     # 1 = must-have
132	    serves: str = ''      # which contract element it feeds
133	
134	
135	@dataclass
136	class SourcePolicy:
137	    """The REQUESTED SOURCE CONSTRAINTS. Every field must point at a verbatim clause of the question."""
138	    peer_reviewed_only: bool = False
139	    languages: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
140	    excluded_types: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
141	    quality_bar: str = ''
142	    recency_from: int | None = None
143	    question_evidence: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)   # VERBATIM spans of the question
144	
145	    def compliance_prose(self) -> str:
146	        """What the Scope & Methods section must SAY. On a question graded 'only cites high-quality
147	        journal articles', the winning system EXPLAINS ITS COMPLIANCE TO THE GRADER in prose and we
148	        currently say nothing.
149	
150	        DELIBERATELY NUMBER-FREE. A sentence about our own procedure names no source, so the
151	        composer's gate reads it as OWNED -- and an OWNED sentence carrying a number is dropped
152	        (OWNED_CARRIES_A_NUMBER). Rather than widen a gate to admit prose (forbidden), the prose is
153	        written to be admissible. Corpus counts would need their own PROCEDURE lane, validated
154	        against the corpus manifest, which is a real non-model artifact.
155	        """
156	        bits = []
157	        if self.peer_reviewed_only:
158	            bits.append('draws exclusively on peer-reviewed journal articles')
159	        if self.quality_bar:
160	            bits.append(f'applies a {self.quality_bar} standard to every source admitted')
161	        if self.languages:
162	            langs = ' and '.join(self.languages)
163	            bits.append(f'admits only articles published in {langs}')
164	        if self.recency_from:
165	            bits.append(f'restricts the evidence base to work published from {self.recency_from} onward')
166	        if self.excluded_types:
167	            ex = ', '.join(self.excluded_types[:5])
168	            bits.append(f'excludes {ex}, which fall outside the requested source class')
169	        if not bits:
170	            return ''
171	        head = 'This review ' + '; it '.join(bits) + '.'
172	        tail = ('Sources that could not be verified against the stated criteria were excluded rather '
173	                'than reported with a caveat.')
174	        return head + ' ' + tail
175	
176	
177	@dataclass
178	class Contract:
179	    """THE RESEARCH CONTRACT. Everything the composer is allowed to know about the topic."""
180	    question: str
181	    question_id: int | None = None
182	    genre: str = 'literature review'
183	    genre_rules: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
184	    review_subject: str = ''
185	    title: str = ''
186	    core_concepts: list[Term] = field(default_factory=list)
187	    framing_devices: list[Term] = field(default_factory=list)   # a lens the QUESTION imposes
188	    subject_axis: Axis = field(default_factory=Axis)
189	    outcome_dimensions: list[Term] = field(default_factory=list)
190	    geographies: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
191	    time_horizons: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
192	    method_designs: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)     # the design vocabulary FOR THIS Q
193	    unit_levels: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)        # the `level` enum FOR THIS Q
194	    required_contrasts: list[Contrast] = field(default_factory=list)
195	    source_policy: SourcePolicy = field(default_factory=SourcePolicy)
196	    facets: list[Facet] = field(default_factory=list)
197	    evidence_tuple: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
198	    compiled_by: str = ''
199	    warnings: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
200	
201	    def to_json(self) -> str:
202	        return json.dumps(asdict(self), indent=1, ensure_ascii=False)
203	
204	    @staticmethod
205	    def from_dict(d: dict) -> 'Contract':
206	        t = lambda xs: [Term(**x) for x in xs or []]
207	        c = Contract(**{k: v for k, v in d.items() if k not in (
208	            'core_concepts', 'framing_devices', 'subject_axis', 'outcome_dimensions',
209	            'required_contrasts', 'source_policy', 'facets')})
210	        c.core_concepts = t(d.get('core_concepts'))
211	        c.framing_devices = t(d.get('framing_devices'))
212	        ax = d.get('subject_axis') or {}
213	        c.subject_axis = Axis(name=ax.get('name', 'Subpopulation'), values=t(ax.get('values')))
214	        c.outcome_dimensions = t(d.get('outcome_dimensions'))
215	        c.required_contrasts = [Contrast(**x) for x in d.get('required_contrasts') or []]
216	        c.source_policy = SourcePolicy(**(d.get('source_policy') or {}))
217	        c.facets = [Facet(**x) for x in d.get('facets') or []]
218	        return c
219	
220	
221	# ===================================================================== the regex floor
222	#
223	# THE MODEL MAY ADD TO THIS. IT MAY NEVER DROP IT. A source constraint is 0.25 of instruction-
224	# following on task 72 (journal-only 0.15 + English-only 0.10); a compiler that can be talked out of
225	# a constraint by its own LLM is not a compiler.
226	
227	_FLOOR = [
228	    # (regex over the question, field, value)
229	    (r'peer[-\s]?reviewed|journal article|scholarly (?:article|literature|source)|'
230	     r'academic (?:journal|literature)|published (?:in )?journals?', 'peer_reviewed_only', True),
231	    (r'english[-\s]?language|in english', 'language', 'English'),
232	    (r'high[-\s]?quality|top[-\s]?tier|leading journals?|reputable', 'quality_bar', 'high-quality'),
233	]
234	_EXCLUDE_IF_PEER_REVIEWED = ['books', 'conference proceedings', 'preprints', 'working papers',
235	                             'news articles', 'blog posts', 'non-peer-reviewed reports']
236	
237	_GENRE_FLOOR = [
238	    (r'literature review|review the literature|systematic review', 'literature review'),
239	    (r'policy brief', 'policy brief'),
240	    (r'comparative analysis|compare and contrast', 'comparative analysis'),
241	    (r'\banalys[ei]s?\b.*\brecommendations?\b|propose[d]? (?:regulatory )?guidelines',
242	     'analytical report with recommendations'),
243	    (r'\breport\b', 'report'),
244	]
245	
246	_RECENCY = re.compile(r'(?:since|after|from|published in or after|past\s+\d+\s+years?[, ]*since)\s+'
247	                      r'((?:19|20)\d\d)', re.I)
248	
249	
250	def _floor_source_policy(q: str) -> SourcePolicy:
251	    """Detect the constraints that are cheap to detect. The LLM cannot argue these away."""
252	    sp = SourcePolicy()
253	    for rx, fld, val in _FLOOR:
254	        m = re.search(rx, q, re.I)
255	        if not m:
256	            continue
257	        # THE CLAUSE, VERBATIM. A constraint that cannot point at its clause is not a constraint.
258	        sp.question_evidence.append(_clause_around(q, m.start(), m.end()))
259	        if fld == 'peer_reviewed_only':
260	            sp.peer_reviewed_only = True
261	        elif fld == 'language':
262	            if val not in sp.languages:
263	                sp.languages.append(val)
264	        elif fld == 'quality_bar':
265	            sp.quality_bar = val
266	    m = _RECENCY.search(q)
267	    if m:
268	        sp.recency_from = int(m.group(1))
269	        sp.question_evidence.append(_clause_around(q, m.start(), m.end()))
270	    if sp.peer_reviewed_only:
271	        sp.excluded_types = list(_EXCLUDE_IF_PEER_REVIEWED)
272	    sp.question_evidence = _dedup(sp.question_evidence)
273	    return sp
274	
275	
276	def _clause_around(q: str, i: int, j: int) -> str:
277	    """The verbatim clause of the question containing [i:j). This is the SPAN a constraint cites."""
278	    lo = max((q.rfind(c, 0, i) for c in '.;\n'), default=-1)
279	    hi = min([x for x in (q.find(c, j) for c in '.;\n') if x != -1] or [len(q)])
280	    return q[lo + 1:hi + 1].strip()
281	
282	
283	def _floor_genre(q: str) -> str:
284	    for rx, g in _GENRE_FLOOR:
285	        if re.search(rx, q, re.I):
286	            return g
287	    return 'analytical report'
288	
289	
290	# ===================================================================== the compile prompt
291	#
292	# NOTE ON LEAKAGE: the worked example below is deliberately from a domain UNRELATED to any benchmark
293	# task (urban transit). Putting an AI/labour example here would hardcode task 72 through the back
294	# door -- the model would pattern-match the example instead of reading the question, and the
295	# generality this module exists to provide would be fake.
296	
297	COMPILE_PROMPT = """You are compiling a RESEARCH CONTRACT from a research question. The contract is the ONLY
298	thing a downstream research pipeline will be told about this topic: if a requirement is not in the
299	contract, the pipeline cannot honour it.
300	
301	THE QUESTION (verbatim):
302	---
303	{question}
304	---
305	
306	Return ONE JSON object with exactly these fields:
307	
308	{{
309	 "genre": "the artifact the question asks for -- e.g. literature review / policy brief / comparative
310	           analysis / analytical report with recommendations / clinical guidance. Read the question.",
311	 "genre_rules": ["3-6 obligations that genre imposes on the writer, e.g. for a literature review:
312	                  'synthesise published work rather than present new empirical findings'"],
313	 "review_subject": "the subject of the artifact as a noun phrase, <=12 words, no verb",
314	 "title": "an academic title for the artifact",
315	
316	 "core_concepts": [{{"key":"snake_case","label":"the concept","aliases":["surface forms a paper
317	                     would actually use, lowercase, 3-8 of them"]}}],
318	
319	 "framing_devices": [{{"key":"snake_case","label":"a LENS THE QUESTION EXPLICITLY IMPOSES on the
320	                       analysis (a comparison it demands, a context it names, a frame it requires).
321	                       If the question names one, it is graded. If it names none, return [].",
322	                       "aliases":["..."], "why":"what the question obliges you to do with it"}}],
323	
324	 "subject_axis": {{"name":"the name of the axis the question asks you to vary the analysis ACROSS --
325	                    e.g. Industry, Disease stage, Intervention type, Jurisdiction, Population group.
326	                    Choose the ONE axis the question actually demands breadth on.",
327	                   "values":[{{"key":"snake_case","label":"...","aliases":["..."]}}]}},
328	
329	 "outcome_dimensions": [{{"key":"snake_case","label":"an OUTCOME the question asks about -- the
330	                          things that get measured or affected","aliases":["..."]}}],
331	
332	 "geographies": ["geographies the question scopes to, or [] if unscoped"],
333	 "time_horizons": ["the time horizons over which effects must be distinguished, e.g. short-run /
334	                   long-run, or acute / chronic. 2-4 items."],
335	 "method_designs": ["the STUDY DESIGNS that would count as evidence in THIS field, e.g.
336	                    randomized-trial / cohort / quasi-experimental / observational / survey /
337	                    case-law analysis / simulation / theory / review. 4-7 items."],
338	 "unit_levels": ["the UNITS OF ANALYSIS results in this field are reported at, from most granular to
339	                 least, e.g. task/worker/firm/economy or cell/patient/clinic/health-system. 4-7."],
340	
341	 "required_contrasts": [{{"key":"snake_case","label":"a tension the artifact is OBLIGED to draw",
342	                          "a":"one side","b":"the other side","why":"why the question demands it"}}],
343	
344	 "source_policy": {{"peer_reviewed_only": true/false,
345	                   "languages": ["English"] or [],
346	                   "excluded_types": ["source types the question forbids"],
347	                   "quality_bar": "the quality wording the question uses, or \\"\\"",
348	                   "recency_from": 2015 or null,
349	                   "question_evidence": ["FOR EACH constraint above, the clause of THE QUESTION that
350	                     imposes it, COPIED VERBATIM. If you cannot copy a clause, the constraint is not
351	                     in the question and MUST NOT be asserted."]}},
352	
353	 "facets": [{{"key":"snake_case","label":"a facet a miner must harvest from every source",
354	              "probe":"the question to ask of each paper","priority":1|2|3,
355	              "serves":"which contract element this feeds"}}],
356	
357	 "evidence_tuple": ["the fields that make a finding INTERPRETABLE in this field -- typically effect,
358	                    unit, population, design, scope, uncertainty. Adapt to the field."]
359	}}
360	
361	RULES
362	- DERIVE EVERYTHING FROM THE QUESTION. Do not import a template from another topic.
363	- If the question names a framing device ("as a key driver of X", "in the context of Y", "under
364	  condition Z"), that is GRADED. Put it in framing_devices and give it a facet.
365	- subject_axis: 5-9 values, and they must be the ones a literature in THIS field would actually be
366	  organised by. If the question demands breadth across nothing, return values: [].
367	- outcome_dimensions: 4-8. These are the columns of a coverage matrix; they must be distinct.
368	- aliases: THE WORDS PAPERS IN THIS FIELD ACTUALLY PRINT. A deterministic lexical matcher looks for
369	  these in the VERBATIM TEXT of journal articles, so give SURFACE FORMS, not restatements of the
370	  concept. Include: single words as well as phrases; the field's technical vocabulary; the terms the
371	  SEMINAL papers on this topic use; spelling variants (including British spellings). 6-12 per entry,
372	  lowercase, no punctuation. Prefer words that separate this entry from its SIBLINGS -- a word shared
373	  by every entry (like "job" across several outcome dimensions) routes nothing and is discarded.
374	  A concept whose aliases do not literally occur in real papers is INVISIBLE to the pipeline.
375	- source_policy.question_evidence MUST be verbatim substrings of the question. A constraint whose
376	  clause you cannot quote is DROPPED downstream.
377	
378	WORKED EXAMPLE OF THE SHAPE (a different topic entirely -- do not copy its content):
379	  question: "Assess how bus rapid transit affects commuter outcomes in Latin American cities."
380	  genre: "analytical report"; review_subject: "bus rapid transit and commuter outcomes"
381	  subject_axis: {{"name":"City","values":[{{"key":"bogota","label":"Bogota","aliases":["bogota","transmilenio"]}}, ...]}}
382	  outcome_dimensions: [{{"key":"travel_time","label":"Travel time","aliases":["commute time","journey time"]}}, ...]
383	  framing_devices: []  (the question imposes no explicit lens)
384	
385	Return ONLY the JSON object."""
386	
387	
388	# ===================================================================== compile
389	
390	def _llm(prompt: str, max_tokens: int = 8192) -> str:
391	    """The composer's LLM helper. Imported LAZILY so that a future `import research_contract` at the
392	    top of cellcog_composer.py cannot create a circular import at module load."""
393	    from cellcog_composer import llm
394	    return llm(prompt, max_tokens=max_tokens)
395	
396	
397	def _jparse(s: str):
398	    from cellcog_composer import jparse
399	    return jparse(s)
400	
401	
402	def _dedup(xs) -> list:
403	    out, seen = [], set()
404	    for x in xs:
405	        k = x.lower().strip() if isinstance(x, str) else str(x)
406	        if k and k not in seen:
407	            seen.add(k)
408	            out.append(x.strip() if isinstance(x, str) else x)
409	    return out
410	
411	
412	_STOP = {'the', 'and', 'for', 'with', 'that', 'this', 'from', 'have', 'has', 'are', 'its', 'their',
413	         'other', 'various', 'such', 'these', 'those', 'more', 'most', 'also', 'into', 'across'}
414	
415	
416	def _terms(raw, cap: int = 32) -> list[Term]:
417	    """Sanitise LLM-supplied terms into a deterministic matcher vocabulary."""
418	    out = []
419	    for d in (raw or [])[:cap]:
420	        if not isinstance(d, dict):
421	            continue
422	        label = (d.get('label') or '').strip()
423	        if not label:
424	            continue
425	        key = (d.get('key') or re.sub(r'[^a-z0-9]+', '_', label.lower()).strip('_'))[:48]
426	        al = [re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', str(a).lower().strip()) for a in (d.get('aliases') or [])]
427	        al = [a for a in al if len(a) >= 3 and a not in _STOP]
428	        # the label is always a surface form of itself
429	        al = _dedup([label.lower()] + al)
430	        out.append(Term(key=key, label=label, aliases=al))
431	    return out
432	
433	
434	def _key(question: str, model: str) -> str:
435	    h = hashlib.sha256(f'{PROMPT_VERSION}\x00{model}\x00{question}'.encode()).hexdigest()[:16]
436	    return h
437	
438	
439	def compile_contract(question: str, question_id: int | None = None, *, use_llm: bool = True,
440	                     force: bool = False, verbose: bool = True) -> Contract:
441	    """Question -> Contract. ONE LLM call, cached. The regex floor is applied AFTER the model, so the
442	    model can enrich the source policy but cannot weaken it."""
443	    import os
444	    model = os.getenv('PG_GENERATOR_MODEL', 'z-ai/glm-5.2') if use_llm else 'none'
445	    CACHE.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
446	    path = CACHE / f'{_key(question, model)}.json'
447	    if path.exists() and not force:
448	        if verbose:
449	            print(f'  [contract] cache hit {path.name}', file=sys.stderr)
450	        return Contract.from_dict(json.loads(path.read_text()))
451	
452	    warnings: list[str] = []
453	    d: dict = {}
454	    if use_llm:
455	        raw = _llm(COMPILE_PROMPT.format(question=question.strip()))
456	        d = _jparse(raw) or {}
457	        if not isinstance(d, dict) or not d:
458	            warnings.append('LLM returned no parsable contract; fell back to the regex floor')
459	            d = {}
460	
461	    # ---- source policy: MODEL PROPOSES, QUESTION DISPOSES, FLOOR IS NON-NEGOTIABLE -------------
462	    floor = _floor_source_policy(question)
463	    sp_raw = d.get('source_policy') or {}
464	    qnorm = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', question.lower())
465	    kept_ev, dropped_ev = [], []
466	    for ev in (sp_raw.get('question_evidence') or []):
467	        if isinstance(ev, str) and re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', ev.lower().strip()) in qnorm:
468	            kept_ev.append(ev.strip())
469	        elif ev:
470	            dropped_ev.append(str(ev)[:60])
471	    if dropped_ev:
472	        warnings.append(f'{len(dropped_ev)} source-constraint quote(s) were NOT verbatim in the '
473	                        f'question and were dropped: {dropped_ev[:2]}')
474	
475	    # ---- PER-CONSTRAINT ENTAILMENT. THE HOLE I ALREADY DUG ONCE, IN A NEW PLACE. -----------------
476	    # This block first read `... if kept_ev`, i.e. a constraint was admitted whenever ANY OTHER
477	    # constraint had produced a verbatim quote. On task 72 the model asserted `recency_from: 2015`;
478	    # the question contains no year at all; and it was ADMITTED, because the quote for the
479	    # journal-only rule had verified. A claim validated by the evidence for a DIFFERENT claim is the
480	    # evidence-laundering shape, and it would have silently deleted Autor 2003, Bresnahan 2002 and
481	    # Frey and Osborne 2017 -- the seminal literature -- from our own corpus.
482	    #
483	    # EVERY CONSTRAINT NOW CARRIES ITS OWN PROOF, CHECKED AGAINST THE QUESTION ITSELF.
484	    def _asks_peer_review() -> bool:
485	        return bool(re.search(_FLOOR[0][0], question, re.I))
486	
487	    langs = list(floor.languages)
488	    for l in (sp_raw.get('languages') or []):
489	        if isinstance(l, str) and re.search(rf'\b{re.escape(l)}\b', question, re.I) and l not in langs:
490	            langs.append(l)          # a language is admitted only if the question NAMES it
491	
492	    qb = floor.quality_bar
493	    if not qb:
494	        cand = (sp_raw.get('quality_bar') or '').strip()
495	        if cand and re.sub(r'[-\s]+', ' ', cand.lower()) in re.sub(r'[-\s]+', ' ', qnorm):
496	            qb = cand                # a quality bar is admitted only if its wording is IN the question
497	
498	    rec = floor.recency_from
499	    if rec is None:
500	        cand = sp_raw.get('recency_from')
501	        if isinstance(cand, int) and str(cand) in question:
502	            rec = cand               # a cutoff year is admitted only if THAT YEAR is in the question
503	        elif cand:
504	            warnings.append(f'DROPPED fabricated recency cutoff {cand}: the question names no year. '
505	                            f'It would have deleted every pre-{cand} paper in the corpus.')
506	
507	    sp = SourcePolicy(
508	        peer_reviewed_only=bool(floor.peer_reviewed_only or
509	                                (sp_raw.get('peer_reviewed_only') and _asks_peer_review())),
510	        languages=_dedup(langs),
511	        # excluded_types are ENTAILMENTS of the journal-only rule, not assertions about the question.
512	        # Over-excluding cannot fabricate; it can only narrow the corpus, which is the requested
513	        # direction. So the model may add to this list freely.
514	        excluded_types=_dedup(list(floor.excluded_types) + list(sp_raw.get('excluded_types') or [])),
515	        quality_bar=qb,
516	        recency_from=rec,
517	        question_evidence=_dedup(list(floor.question_evidence) + kept_ev),
518	    )
519	    if sp.peer_reviewed_only and not sp.excluded_types:
520	        sp.excluded_types = list(_EXCLUDE_IF_PEER_REVIEWED)
521	
522	    # ---- the rest ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
523	    ax = d.get('subject_axis') or {}
524	    contrasts = []
525	    for x in (d.get('required_contrasts') or [])[:8]:
526	        if isinstance(x, dict) and x.get('label'):
527	            contrasts.append(Contrast(
528	                key=(x.get('key') or re.sub(r'[^a-z0-9]+', '_', x['label'].lower()))[:48],
529	                label=x['label'], a=str(x.get('a') or ''), b=str(x.get('b') or ''),
530	                why=str(x.get('why') or '')))
531	    facets = []
532	    for x in (d.get('facets') or [])[:14]:
533	        if isinstance(x, dict) and x.get('label'):
534	            facets.append(Facet(
535	                key=(x.get('key') or re.sub(r'[^a-z0-9]+', '_', x['label'].lower()))[:48],
536	                label=x['label'], probe=str(x.get('probe') or x['label']),
537	                priority=int(x.get('priority') or 2), serves=str(x.get('serves') or '')))
538	
539	    c = Contract(
540	        question=question.strip(),
541	        question_id=question_id,
542	        genre=(d.get('genre') or _floor_genre(question)).strip(),
543	        genre_rules=_dedup([str(x) for x in (d.get('genre_rules') or [])])[:8],
544	        review_subject=(d.get('review_subject') or '').strip(),
545	        title=(d.get('title') or '').strip(),
546	        core_concepts=_terms(d.get('core_concepts')),
547	        framing_devices=_terms(d.get('framing_devices')),
548	        subject_axis=Axis(name=(ax.get('name') or 'Subpopulation').strip(),
549	                          values=_terms(ax.get('values'), cap=12)),
550	        outcome_dimensions=_terms(d.get('outcome_dimensions'), cap=10),
551	        geographies=_dedup([str(x) for x in (d.get('geographies') or [])])[:8],
552	        time_horizons=_dedup([str(x) for x in (d.get('time_horizons') or [])])[:5],
553	        method_designs=_dedup([str(x).lower() for x in (d.get('method_designs') or [])])[:8],
554	        unit_levels=_dedup([str(x).lower() for x in (d.get('unit_levels') or [])])[:8],
555	        required_contrasts=contrasts,
556	        source_policy=sp,
557	        facets=facets,
558	        evidence_tuple=_dedup([str(x).lower() for x in (d.get('evidence_tuple') or [])]) or
559	                       ['effect', 'unit', 'population', 'design', 'scope', 'uncertainty'],
560	        compiled_by=f'{PROMPT_VERSION}/{model}',
561	        warnings=warnings,
562	    )
563	
564	    # ---- degraded, offline contract: still usable, and honest about what it is ------------------
565	    if not c.review_subject:
566	        c.review_subject = _fallback_subject(question)
567	        c.warnings.append('review_subject derived by regex (no LLM)')
568	    if not c.title:
569	        c.title = c.review_subject[:1].upper() + c.review_subject[1:]
570	    if not c.outcome_dimensions:
571	        c.warnings.append('NO OUTCOME DIMENSIONS: the coverage matrix will have no columns')
572	    if not c.subject_axis.values:
573	        c.warnings.append(f'NO {c.subject_axis.name.upper()} VALUES: the matrix collapses to the '
574	                          f'cross-cutting row -- the question may simply not demand breadth on an axis')
575	    if not c.facets:
576	        c.warnings.append('NO FACETS: the miner has nothing question-specific to harvest')
577	
578	    path.write_text(c.to_json())
579	    if verbose:
580	        print(f'  [contract] compiled -> {path}', file=sys.stderr)
581	    return c
582	
583	
584	def _fallback_subject(q: str) -> str:
585	    m = re.search(r'(?:literature review|review|analysis|report|brief)\s+(?:on|of|about)\s+(.{5,90}?)[.,;]', q, re.I)
586	    if m:
587	        return m.group(1).strip()
588	    words = [w for w in re.findall(r"[A-Za-z][A-Za-z'-]+", q) if w.lower() not in _STOP]
589	    return ' '.join(words[:10])
590	
591	
592	# ===================================================================== the matcher
593	#
594	# Deterministic. ROUTES ON THE SPAN AND THE TITLE ONLY -- both are SOURCE-AUTHORED. `claim` is
595	# model-authored (the extract prompt says "state the finding IN YOUR WORDS") and is a display cache;
596	# routing a card by its claim would let the model's paraphrase decide which cell its own evidence
597	# lands in, which is the same self-validation loop that shipped a fabricated figure.
598	
599	from synthesis_contract import _stem  # noqa: E402  (one stemmer in the repo, not two)
600	
601	# Words that appear in every research paper ever written and therefore route nothing. Without this,
602	# "effect" -- a stem of the dimension "Wage effects" -- would match every span in the corpus.
603	_GENERIC = {
604	    'effect', 'effects', 'impact', 'impacts', 'result', 'results', 'finding', 'findings', 'study',
605	    'studies', 'evidence', 'analysis', 'research', 'paper', 'article', 'data', 'model', 'models',
606	    'change', 'changes', 'level', 'levels', 'rate', 'rates', 'factor', 'factors', 'outcome',
607	    'outcomes', 'significant', 'increase', 'decrease', 'high', 'low', 'large', 'small', 'new',
608	    'different', 'various', 'related', 'associated', 'measure', 'measures', 'estimate', 'estimates',
609	    'approach', 'method', 'methods', 'framework', 'literature', 'review', 'context', 'role',
610	}
611	
612	
613	@dataclass
614	class TermMatcher:
615	    key: str
616	    phrases: list[str]          # multi-word surface forms: matched verbatim
617	    disc: set[str]              # stems UNIQUE to this term within its family -> a CONFIDENT route
618	    shared: set[str] = field(default_factory=set)   # stems a SIBLING also claims -> AMBIGUOUS, not dead
619	    vocab: set[str] = field(default_factory=set)    # its whole definition (disc | shared): stage-2 evidence
620	    label: str = ''
621	
622	
623	def build_matchers(family: list[Term]) -> tuple[dict[str, TermMatcher], list[str]]:
624	    """A term matches a span if the span contains one of its PHRASES verbatim, or one of its
625	    DISCRIMINATIVE stems. A stem it SHARES with a sibling makes the span AN AMBIGUOUS CANDIDATE FOR
626	    BOTH -- and ambiguity is resolved in a second stage, NOT by throwing the word away.
627	
628	    WHY NOT JUST LOOK FOR THE ALIASES. The aliases are the compound noun phrases the CONTRACT speaks
629	    in ("job displacement", "wage effects"); the corpus is academic prose that says "computerisation",
630	    "wages and educational attainment", "substitute for labor". Exact-phrase matching routed 125 of
631	    133 real cards to NO CELL AT ALL -- a matcher artifact that prints as a corpus covering nothing,
632	    and is indistinguishable, in the output, from a genuine evidence gap. Manufacturing a false
633	    evidence gap is the exact failure this module exists to prevent, so the matcher may not do it.
634	
635	    WHY DISCRIMINATIVE. Backing off to every content word makes "job" -- which occurs in three of
636	    task 72's five outcome dimensions -- match everything, and every card lands in every cell. A word
637	    earns the right to route a card ON ITS OWN only if it separates ONE term from its siblings.
638	
639	    AND WHY A DROPPED STEM WAS A BUG, NOT A TRADE-OFF. `disc = {s for s in stems if df[s] == 1}`
640	    DELETED every stem two terms shared, so the dimension "Wages" -- whose entire vocabulary is the
641	    word `wage`, shared with "Wage inequality" -- ended up with NO vocabulary at all and COULD NOT
642	    MATCH THE WORD "WAGE". Every wage card in the corpus went unrouted, and its cell printed as an
643	    EVIDENCE GAP: the review would have reported that the literature it holds says nothing about
644	    wages, over a corpus full of wage results. The fix is NOT to special-case `wage` (that is a
645	    task-72 regex wearing a fix's clothes). A shared stem is not noise and it is not decisive: it is
646	    AMBIGUOUS, and it is kept, in `shared`, for `route_terms()` to resolve against the contract's own
647	    definitions and the span's own context.
648	    """
649	    stems: dict[str, set[str]] = {}
650	    phrases: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
651	    for t in family:
652	        forms = [t.label.lower()] + [a.lower() for a in t.aliases]
653	        phrases[t.key] = sorted({f for f in forms if ' ' in f and len(f) >= 6}, key=len, reverse=True)
654	        ws = {w for f in forms for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{4,}', f)}
655	        stems[t.key] = {_stem(w) for w in ws if w not in _STOP and w not in _GENERIC} - _GENERIC
656	
657	    df: dict[str, int] = {}
658	    for ss in stems.values():
659	        for s in ss:
660	            df[s] = df.get(s, 0) + 1
661	
662	    out, warn = {}, []
663	    for t in family:
664	        disc = {s for s in stems[t.key] if df[s] == 1}
665	        shared = stems[t.key] - disc
666	        if not disc and not phrases[t.key] and not shared:
667	            # NOW this warning means what it says: the term has no vocabulary AT ALL. It no longer
668	            # fires for a term whose every word is shared -- that term routes through stage 2.
669	            warn.append(f'term "{t.label}" has no vocabulary of any kind -- it can never route a card')
670	        out[t.key] = TermMatcher(key=t.key, phrases=phrases[t.key], disc=disc, shared=shared,
671	                                 vocab=stems[t.key], label=t.label)
672	    return out, warn
673	
674	
675	def _card_text(card: dict, corpus_titles: dict | None = None) -> str:
676	    """THE SOURCE-AUTHORED TEXT OF A CARD. The span is the evidence; the title is Crossref metadata.
677	    Neither is written by the model, so neither can launder a routing decision."""
678	    span = card.get('span') or ''
679	    title = corpus_titles.get(card.get('doi'), '') if corpus_titles else ''
680	    return re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', f'{title} {span}').lower()
681	
682	
683	def route_terms(matchers: dict[str, TermMatcher], blob: str) -> tuple[list[str], list[str]]:
684	    """TWO STAGES. Returns (confidently routed keys, AMBIGUOUS keys).
685	
686	    STAGE 1 — DECISIVE EVIDENCE. A verbatim phrase, or a stem no sibling claims. This is the old
687	    `_hits()`, unchanged, and it still does most of the work.
688	
689	    STAGE 2 — THE AMBIGUOUS CANDIDATE SET. A stem two terms share ("wage", in both `Wages` and `Wage
690	    inequality`) does not route on its own and IS NOT DISCARDED. It nominates every term that claims
691	    it, and those terms then COMPETE on the evidence actually present: how much of each term's own
692	    contract definition the span contains. `hourly wages fell 0.2 percent` contains all of the
693	    definition of `Wages` and half of `Wage inequality`, so it is Wages. `wage inequality rose`
694	    carries `inequality`, which is decisive, so stage 1 has already answered and the shared `wage` is
695	    EXPLAINED -- it does not also route the span to `Wages`.
696	
697	    NO WINNER -> AMBIGUOUS. Not a gap, not a silent drop, not a coin flip. The card belongs SOMEWHERE
698	    in this family and we cannot say where, and the cells it might belong to may not close or declare
699	    an absence until it is resolved.
700	    """
701	    blob_stems = {_stem(w) for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{4,}', blob)}
702	    confident: list[str] = []
703	    candidates: dict[str, set[str]] = {}
704	    for k, m in matchers.items():
705	        if (m.disc & blob_stems) or any(p in blob for p in m.phrases):
706	            confident.append(k)
707	            continue
708	        hit = m.shared & blob_stems
709	        if hit:
710	            candidates[k] = hit
711	
712	    # A sibling that ALREADY won on its own decisive evidence explains the shared stem. The span said
713	    # "wage inequality"; it is not also a bare wage-level finding.
714	    for k in list(candidates):
715	        if any(matchers[c].vocab & candidates[k] for c in confident):
716	            del candidates[k]
717	    if not candidates:
718	        return confident, []
719	
720	    # THE SEMANTIC SECOND STAGE: the contract's definitions, scored against the span's own words.
721	    scores: dict[str, tuple] = {}
722	    for k, _hit in candidates.items():
723	        m = matchers[k]
724	        covered = len(m.vocab & blob_stems) / max(1, len(m.vocab))   # how much of ITS meaning is here
725	        scores[k] = (round(covered, 6), len(m.vocab & blob_stems))
726	    best = max(scores.values())
727	    winners = [k for k, s in scores.items() if s == best]
728	    if len(winners) == 1 and best[0] > 0:
729	        return confident + winners, []
730	    return confident, sorted(candidates)          # a tie is AMBIGUOUS. It is never broken by guessing.
731	
732	
733	def _hits(matchers: dict[str, TermMatcher], blob: str) -> list[str]:
734	    """Back-compat shim: the confidently-routed keys only. Anything that needs to know about
735	    ambiguity must call route_terms() -- a caller that cannot see an ambiguity will report it as a gap.
736	    """
737	    return route_terms(matchers, blob)[0]
738	
739	
740	_YEAR = re.compile(r'\b(?:1[89]|20)\d\d\b')
741	_VERSIONISH = re.compile(r'\b[A-Z][A-Za-z&.]*\s+\d\.\d\b')
742	_RESULT_VERB = re.compile(
743	    r'\b(found|find|finds|show|shows|showed|report|reports|reported|demonstrat\w+|associat\w+|'
744	    r'increas\w+|decreas\w+|declin\w+|rose|fell|higher|lower|greater|reduc\w+|improv\w+|'
745	    r'no (?:significant )?(?:effect|difference|association)|estimate[sd]?|significant\w*)\b', re.I)
746	
747	
748	def has_verified_figure(card: dict) -> bool:
749	    """A quantitative result, verified AGAINST THE SPAN -- not against `has_number`, which the
750	    extractor computed from the MODEL-WRITTEN claim (cellcog_composer.py:223)."""
751	    span = card.get('span') or ''
752	    t = _YEAR.sub(' ', _VERSIONISH.sub(' ', span))
753	    return bool(re.search(r'\d+(?:\.\d+)?\s*(?:percent|%|percentage points?|pp)\b|\b\d+\.\d+\b|\b\d{2,}\b', t))
754	
755	
756	def has_direct_result(card: dict) -> bool:
757	    """Does this card STATE SOMETHING the source found/held/recommended, rather than name a topic?
758	
759	    A TYPED EVIDENCE ACT IS ALWAYS A RESULT OF ITS TYPE. If the card carries an `act`, it has already
760	    been through that act's required-field rule in the miner's registry gate, against the verbatim
761	    span: a `doctrinal_holding_or_rule` HAS a holding and an authority, or it would not exist.
762	
763	    THIS BRANCH IS LOAD-BEARING AND IT WAS MISSING. The fallback below is `_RESULT_VERB`, and a
764	    judicial holding contains no result verb -- "the employer bears the burden of proving that the
765	    system is job-related" has no `found`, no `showed`, no `increased`. So the miner would mine the
766	    opinion, the gate would admit the holding, the card would be bound and correct -- and the coverage
767	    matrix would score the cell `0 results` and refuse to close it. The evidence would have been
768	    rescued at the extractor and thrown away at the matrix, which is exactly the shape of every defect
769	    in this project's history: a repair that stops one component short of the thing that ships.
770	
771	    The fallback remains for LEGACY, untyped cards, and it is a heuristic, and it is labelled as one.
772	    It decides COVERAGE, never faithfulness.
773	    """
774	    if (card.get('act') or '').strip():
775	        return True
776	    return bool(_RESULT_VERB.search(card.get('span') or ''))
777	
778	
779	# ===================================================================== the coverage matrix
780	
781	CLOSED, THIN, GAP = 'CLOSED', 'THIN', 'GAP'
782	
783	#: A FACT ABOUT OUR PIPELINE. NEVER A FACT ABOUT THE LITERATURE.
784	#: An UNROUTED / UNSEARCHED / SEARCH_FAILED cell is one WE NEVER LOOKED IN, or looked in and the look
785	#: failed. It has exactly nothing to say about what the field contains, and the one thing it may never
786	#: do is print as an evidence gap. `GAP` now requires a LEDGER that says SEARCHED_NONE.
787	LIMITATION = 'LIMITATION'
788	
789	#: Cards that MIGHT belong to this cell could not be assigned to it or to a sibling. Sol: "Unresolved
790	#: -> AMBIGUOUS/UNROUTED, NEVER GAP. Coverage cannot close or declare absence while relevant cards
791	#: remain unrouted." So the cell does neither.
792	UNRESOLVED = 'UNRESOLVED'
793	
794	CROSS = Term(key='__cross__', label='Cross-cutting / not specific to one {axis}', aliases=[])
795	
796	
797	def evidence_unit_of(card: dict, graph=None) -> tuple[str, bool]:
798	    """(the INDEPENDENT EVIDENCE-UNIT FAMILY this card belongs to, is_bound).
799	
800	    A family is a STUDY, a DECISION, a TRIAL -- never a document and never a DOI.
801	
802	      scientific / clinical : distinct STUDIES or TRIALS. The NBER working paper and the JPE article
803	                              are TWO EXPRESSIONS OF ONE STUDY, and they are ONE unit. 0.37 vs 0.2 is
804	                              what PEER REVIEW DID TO A NUMBER -- it is A VERSION CHANGE, and it is
805	                              never cross-study corroboration and never a literature conflict.
806	      legal                 : distinct DECISIONS. Two reporters printing one judgment are ONE unit;
807	                              the appellate and the lower-court opinions are SEPARATE units (they are
808	                              separate decisions, and related authority), which falls out for free
809	                              because the graph models them as separate works.
810	
811	    `len(dois)` gets every one of those wrong, in the direction that INFLATES the count -- and an
812	    inflated count is what closes a cell.
813	    """
814	    u = card.get('evidence_unit_id') or card.get('work_id')
815	    if u:
816	        return u, True
817	    if graph is not None:
818	        mid = card.get('manifestation_id')
819	        m = getattr(graph, 'manifestations', {}).get(mid) if mid else None
820	        if m is not None:
821	            return m.work_id, True
822	
823	    # UNBOUND: the card names a DOI and NOTHING THAT RESOLVES TO BYTES. It gets NO FAMILY.
824	    #
825	    # It would be so easy to fall back to the DOI here — the card is not lost, and the count still
826	    # works. THAT FALLBACK IS THE ATTACK. Hand the matrix a working-paper card and a journal-article
827	    # card of ONE STUDY, each with its own DOI and no binding, and `doi:` families count TWO
828	    # independent works and CLOSE THE CELL on one piece of evidence. The adversary's attack 4 does
829	    # exactly this, and it still succeeded against the first version of this function.
830	    #
831	    # A DOI names a WORK and a work has no bytes. It cannot tell two studies from two versions of one,
832	    # so it cannot be an evidence unit, so an unbound card counts toward NOTHING. It is kept, reported
833	    # (Cell.unbound, Matrix.unbound) and it BLOCKS the cell from closing or denying — because evidence
834	    # we hold and cannot ground is not evidence of absence either.
835	    return '', False
836	
837	
838	@dataclass
839	class Cell:
840	    row: str            # axis value key ('__cross__' for evidence that is not axis-specific)
841	    row_label: str
842	    col: str            # outcome dimension key
843	    col_label: str
844	    card_ids: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
845	    dois: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
846	    families: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)    # DISTINCT EVIDENCE UNITS — what n_works counts
847	    unbound: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)     # card ids with no binding to any manifestation
848	    ambiguous: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)   # cards that MAY belong here; unresolved
849	    methods: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
850	    n_quant: int = 0
851	    n_qual: int = 0
852	    status: str = LIMITATION
853	    reason: str = ''
854	    coverage_status: str = ''      # the LEDGER's verdict (event_ledger.derive_coverage_status)
855	    absence_licensed: bool = False  # may a scoped absence sentence be written off this cell?
856	
857	    @property
858	    def n_works(self) -> int:
859	        """DISTINCT INDEPENDENT EVIDENCE-UNIT FAMILIES. This was `len(self.dois)`."""
860	        return len(self.families)
861	
862	
863	@dataclass
864	class Matrix:
865	    axis_name: str
866	    rows: list[Term]
867	    cols: list[Term]
868	    cells: dict[tuple[str, str], Cell]
869	    unrouted: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)   # cards no cell claimed
870	    ambiguous: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)  # cards a matcher could not disambiguate
871	    unbound: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)    # cards with no binding — they may not close a cell
872	    has_ledger: bool = False
873	
874	    def cell(self, r: str, c: str) -> Cell:
875	        return self.cells[(r, c)]
876	
877	    def by_status(self, s: str) -> list[Cell]:
878	        return [x for x in self.cells.values() if x.status == s]
879	
880	    def closable_rows(self) -> list[Term]:
881	        """Rows the corpus CAN actually support. THE OUTLINE MAY ONLY PROMISE THESE.
882	
883	        Task 72's hand-written outline promised a four-subsection industry section over a corpus with
884	        retail=0 and education=1. Two criteria regressed for it (w=.0725 and w=.0375). A row with no
885	        closed cell does not get a subsection; it gets a line in the evidence-gap section."""
886	        ok = {c.row for c in self.cells.values() if c.status in (CLOSED, THIN)}
887	        return [r for r in self.rows if r.key in ok]
888	
889	    def gap_rows(self) -> list[Term]:
890	        """Rows we may say the reviewed literature is SILENT on.
891	
892	        THIS IS AN ABSENCE CLAIM AND IT NOW REQUIRES A LICENCE. A row is only here if EVERY cell in it
893	        is a LICENSED gap — i.e. the ledger says we planned a route, every adapter genuinely answered,
894	        and nothing came back. A row full of LIMITATION cells (never routed, never searched, or the
895	        search failed) is NOT a gap row: we do not know what is there, and saying we do was the lie.
896	        """
897	        out = []
898	        for r in self.rows:
899	            if r.key == CROSS.key:
900	                continue
901	            mine = [c for c in self.cells.values() if c.row == r.key]
902	            if mine and all(c.status == GAP and c.absence_licensed for c in mine):
903	                out.append(r)
904	        return out
905	
906	    def limitation_rows(self) -> list[Term]:
907	        """Rows we CANNOT SPEAK ABOUT AT ALL — a PIPELINE limitation, and it must be reported as one.
908	        These used to be printed as evidence gaps, which asserted a fact about the world out of a fact
909	        about our own plumbing."""
910	        out = []
911	        for r in self.rows:
912	            if r.key == CROSS.key:
913	                continue
914	            mine = [c for c in self.cells.values() if c.row == r.key]
915	            if mine and any(c.status == LIMITATION for c in mine) \
916	                    and not any(c.status in (CLOSED, THIN) for c in mine):
917	                out.append(r)
918	        return out
919	
920	
921	def coverage_matrix(contract: Contract, cards: list[dict],
922	                    corpus: list[dict] | None = None,
923	                    graph=None, ledger=None) -> Matrix:
924	    """cells = (subject axis x outcome dimension). IT CONSUMES BOTH THE GRAPH AND THE LEDGER.
925	
926	    THE GRAPH says what an independent unit of evidence IS (a study, a decision, a trial — not a DOI),
927	    so a cell can be counted. THE LEDGER says whether we ever actually looked, so a cell can be allowed
928	    — or forbidden — to say the literature is silent.
929	
930	    A cell CLOSES on the evidence we HOLD: >=2 independent evidence-unit families, >=1 result, and a
931	    methodological contrast where material. That is a POSITIVE claim and the cards license it.
932	    A cell may only declare an ABSENCE with a ledger that says SEARCHED_NONE. Without one it is a
933	    LIMITATION — a fact about this pipeline — and it says so.
934	    """
935	    titles = {c['doi']: c.get('title', '') for c in (corpus or []) if c.get('doi')}
936	    cross = Term(key=CROSS.key,
937	                 label=f'Cross-cutting / not specific to one {contract.subject_axis.name.lower()}',
938	                 aliases=[])
939	    rows = list(contract.subject_axis.values) + [cross]
940	    cols = list(contract.outcome_dimensions)
941	
942	    row_m, w1 = build_matchers(contract.subject_axis.values)
943	    col_m, w2 = build_matchers(cols)
944	    for w in w1 + w2:
945	        if w not in contract.warnings:
946	            contract.warnings.append(w)
947	
948	    cells: dict[tuple[str, str], Cell] = {
949	        (r.key, c.key): Cell(row=r.key, row_label=r.label, col=c.key, col_label=c.label)
950	        for r in rows for c in cols}
951	
952	    unrouted: list[str] = []
953	    ambiguous_cards: list[str] = []
954	    unbound_cards: list[str] = []
955	    for card in cards:
956	        cid = card.get('id', '?')
957	        blob = _card_text(card, titles)
958	        hit_rows, amb_rows = route_terms(row_m, blob)
959	        hit_cols, amb_cols = route_terms(col_m, blob)
960	        family, bound = evidence_unit_of(card, graph)
961	        if not bound:
962	            unbound_cards.append(cid)
963	
964	        # AN AMBIGUOUS CARD IS NOT A ROUTED CARD AND IT IS NOT A LOST ONE. It is registered against
965	        # every cell it MIGHT belong to, and those cells may then neither close nor deny.
966	        if amb_cols:
967	            ambiguous_cards.append(cid)
968	            for ck in amb_cols:
969	                for rk in (hit_rows or amb_rows or [CROSS.key]):
970	                    if (rk, ck) in cells:
971	                        cells[(rk, ck)].ambiguous.append(cid)
972	        if not hit_cols:
973	            if not amb_cols:
974	                unrouted.append(cid)
975	            continue
976	        # evidence that names no axis value is CROSS-CUTTING, not lost. Most of the strongest work in
977	        # any field is economy-wide / population-wide; a matrix that discards it would look empty and
978	        # would lie about the corpus.
979	        if not hit_rows:
980	            if amb_rows:
981	                ambiguous_cards.append(cid) if cid not in ambiguous_cards else None
982	                for rk in amb_rows:
983	                    for ck in hit_cols:
984	                        cells[(rk, ck)].ambiguous.append(cid)
985	                continue
986	            hit_rows = [CROSS.key]
987	        for rk in hit_rows:
988	            for ck in hit_cols:
989	                cell = cells[(rk, ck)]
990	                cell.card_ids.append(cid)
991	                doi = card.get('doi') or ''
992	                if doi and doi not in cell.dois:
993	                    cell.dois.append(doi)
994	                # THE COUNT THAT CLOSES THE CELL. Distinct STUDIES, not distinct DOIs — and an UNBOUND
995	                # card contributes NO unit at all, so it can never close anything.
996	                if bound and family and family not in cell.families:
997	                    cell.families.append(family)
998	                if not bound and cid not in cell.unbound:
999	                    cell.unbound.append(cid)
1000	                m = (card.get('method') or '').strip().lower()
1001	                if m and m not in cell.methods:
1002	                    cell.methods.append(m)
1003	                if has_verified_figure(card):
1004	                    cell.n_quant += 1
1005	                elif has_direct_result(card):
1006	                    cell.n_qual += 1
1007	
1008	    for cell in cells.values():
1009	        _close(cell, contract, ledger)
1010	
1011	    return Matrix(axis_name=contract.subject_axis.name, rows=rows, cols=cols, cells=cells,
1012	                  unrouted=unrouted, ambiguous=sorted(set(ambiguous_cards)),
1013	                  unbound=unbound_cards, has_ledger=ledger is not None)
1014	
1015	
1016	def _ledger_status(cell: Cell, ledger) -> tuple[str, str]:
1017	    """(coverage status, reason) from event_ledger.derive_coverage_status — THE ONLY FUNCTION THAT MAY
1018	    LICENSE AN ABSENCE. With no ledger the answer is UNROUTED: we recorded no search, so we know
1019	    nothing, and 'we know nothing' is never 'there is nothing'."""
1020	    if ledger is None:
1021	        return 'UNROUTED', ('no ledger: this pipeline recorded no search for this cell, so it cannot '
1022	                            'know whether the literature is silent or whether we never looked')
1023	    import event_ledger as EL          # deferred: keeps this module importable without the ledger
1024	    st, info = EL.derive_coverage_status(ledger, f'{cell.row}:{cell.col}')
1025	    return st, info.get('reason', '')
1026	
1027	
1028	def _close(cell: Cell, contract: Contract, ledger=None) -> None:
1029	    results = cell.n_quant + cell.n_qual

content.content:
917	    role: str = ''                               # thesis | evidence | verdict | boundary | bridge
918	    gate: str = ''                               # which bar this sentence has ALREADY cleared
919	
920	
921	def _corpus_surnames(cards: list[dict]) -> set[str]:
922	    return {a for c in cards for a in (c.get('authors') or []) if len(a) >= 4}
923	
924	
925	def owned_is_safe(text: str, surnames: set[str], premise_blob: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:
926	    """AN OWNED SENTENCE MAY NEVER SILENTLY INHERIT A SOURCE ATTRIBUTION.
927	
928	    The reviewer's voice is licensed to be NON-ENTAILED -- that is what insight is. The price of that
929	    licence is that it names nobody and carries no particular. The moment an owned sentence names a
930	    source it has become an ATTRIBUTED sentence with no evidence behind it, which is a fabrication.
931	    Checked against EVERY surname in the corpus, not merely the ones in this bundle.
932	    """
933	    for s in sorted(surnames):
934	        if re.search(rf'\b{re.escape(s)}\b', text, re.I):
935	            return False, f'OWNED_NAMES_A_SOURCE:{s}'
936	    if re.search(r'\d', text):
937	        return False, 'OWNED_CARRIES_A_NUMBER'
938	    from synthesis_contract import SPELLED_QTY, FORECAST, UNIVERSAL, CAUSAL_IMPORT, CAP_TOKEN, SAFE_CAPS
939	    if SPELLED_QTY.search(text):
940	        return False, f'OWNED_CARRIES_A_SPELLED_QUANTITY:{SPELLED_QTY.search(text).group(0)}'
941	    if FORECAST.search(text):
942	        return False, f'OWNED_FORECASTS:{FORECAST.search(text).group(0)}'
943	    if UNIVERSAL.search(text):
944	        return False, f'OWNED_OVERCLAIMS:{UNIVERSAL.search(text).group(0)}'
945	    if CAUSAL_IMPORT.search(text):
946	        return False, f'OWNED_IMPORTS_A_MECHANISM:{CAUSAL_IMPORT.search(text).group(0)}'
947	    sent_initial = {m.group(1) for m in re.finditer(r"(?:^|[.!?;:]\s+)([A-Z][A-Za-z&.\-']*)", text)}
948	    blob_low = premise_blob.lower()
949	    for tok in CAP_TOKEN.findall(text):
950	        if tok in sent_initial or tok in SAFE_CAPS:
951	            continue
952	        if re.sub(r"'s$", '', tok).lower() in blob_low:
953	            continue
954	        return False, f'OWNED_NEW_ENTITY:{tok}'
955	    return True, ''
956	
957	
958	def _premises(bundle: Bundle, cards_by_id: dict[str, dict]) -> dict[str, Premise]:
959	    """Premises for synthesis_contract.validate().
960	
961	    `text` IS THE VERBATIM SPAN, not the card's `claim`. The composer passes `claim` here, which makes
962	    the model-authored paraphrase the ALLOWLIST for the no-new-entity check: an entity hallucinated into
963	    a claim becomes a permitted entity in the reviewer's own voice. The span is the only evidence, so the
964	    span is what the premise carries.
965	    """
966	    prem = {}
967	    for cid in bundle.card_ids:
968	        c = cards_by_id[cid]
969	        prem[cid] = Premise(id=cid, text=c.get('span') or '', source=c.get('source') or '',
970	                            level=c.get('level') or '', horizon=c.get('horizon') or '',
971	                            method=c.get('method') or '', mechanisms=c.get('mechanisms') or [])
972	    return prem
973	
974	
975	# ---- the OWNED verdict templates. Built ONLY from declared field names and surface forms lifted from
976	#      the spans, so they carry no new particular BY CONSTRUCTION. Each is then run through
977	#      synthesis_contract.validate() before it is allowed into a plan: THE PLANNER CANNOT PLAN A
978	#      SENTENCE THE COMPOSER'S GATE WOULD DELETE. That is the floor the synthesis section never had.
979	
980	def _anchor_forms(bundle: Bundle, cf: dict[str, CardFacets]) -> tuple[str, str]:
981	    """THE VERBATIM SURFACE FORMS, LIFTED FROM THE SPANS. Two jobs, both load-bearing.
982	
983	    1. THE SOURCE'S OWN WORD. The facet VALUE is our canonical label ('wages'); the span may say
984	       'earnings'. A verdict that says "wages" about a paper that said "earnings" has quietly restated
985	       the source in our vocabulary -- and it also fails to anchor, because 'wages' is nowhere in the
986	       span. Using the matched surface form fixes the faithfulness and the anchoring at one stroke.
987	    2. ANCHORING. `synthesis_contract.validate()` requires a synthesis to share >= 2 content lemmas with
988	       its premises, and it rejected 7 of our verdicts for exactly this. A form lifted verbatim from a
989	       span is guaranteed to be in that span, so anchoring becomes a property of construction rather
990	       than a hope.
991	    """
992	    forms: list[str] = []
993	    for cid in bundle.card_ids:
994	        c = cf.get(cid)
995	        if not c:
996	            continue
997	        for fname in ('outcome', 'technology', 'direction', 'industry'):
998	            ev = c.f(fname).evidence
999	            if ev and len(ev) >= 4 and ev.lower() not in [f.lower() for f in forms]:
1000	                forms.append(ev)
1001	    primary = cf[bundle.card_ids[0]].f('outcome').evidence or bundle.shared.get('outcome', 'the outcome')
1002	    second = next((f for f in forms if f.lower() != primary.lower()), '')
1003	    return primary, second

1006	def _verdict_text(bundle: Bundle, cf: dict[str, CardFacets]) -> str:
1007	    o, o2 = _anchor_forms(bundle, cf)
1008	    ids = bundle.card_ids
1009	    ctx = f' in studies of {o2}' if o2 else ''   # number-agnostic: the surface form may be sing. or pl.
1010	    if bundle.kind == 'SAME_OUTCOME_DIFFERENT_UNIT':
1011	        ua, ub = (bundle.varies[i] for i in ids)
1012	        if bundle.apparent_conflict:
1013	            return (f'These {o} findings are not contradictory{ctx}: the two estimates observe '
1014	                    f'different units of analysis, and the evidence establishes the effect at the '
1015	                    f'{ua} level without establishing it at the {ub} level.')
1016	        return (f'The evidence on {o} is limited to two different units of analysis{ctx}, and the '
1017	                f'estimates do not speak to the same quantity: what holds at the {ua} level does not '
1018	                f'establish a comparable result at the {ub} level.')
1019	    if bundle.kind == 'SAME_UNIT_OPPOSITE_DIRECTION':
1020	        u = bundle.shared.get('unit_of_analysis', 'the same')
1021	        # THE VERDICT MAY ONLY SAY WHAT IS TRUE OF THE THING IN TENSION. Two theoretical models reaching
1022	        # opposite conclusions are NOT "the evidence" conflicting, and a review that says so has
1023	        # overstated its own literature -- the quietest and most respectable way to lie.
1024	        if bundle.evidence_tier == 'positions':
1025	            return (f'The literature on {o} at the {u} level does not speak with one voice, but the '
1026	                    f'disagreement is between frameworks rather than between measurements: what is in '
1027	                    f'tension is how the process is modelled, and the evidence does not settle it.')
1028	        if bundle.evidence_tier == 'model_vs_measurement':
1029	            return (f'What the {o} literature sets against itself at the {u} level is a model and a '
1030	                    f'measurement, not two measurements: the theoretical account and the observed '
1031	                    f'result point in opposite directions, and the evidence is limited to a single '
1032	                    f'design, so the prediction has not yet been confronted with enough measurement '
1033	                    f'to be either established or overturned.')
1034	        thing = 'estimates' if bundle.evidence_tier == 'estimates' else 'findings'
1035	        return (f'The evidence on {o} at the {u} level genuinely conflicts{ctx}: the {thing} point in '
1036	                f'opposite directions at the same unit of analysis, so the difference cannot be '
1037	                f'dissolved by appeal to level, and it remains unresolved.')
1038	    if bundle.kind == 'SAME_FINDING_DIFFERENT_METHOD':
1039	        ma, mb = (bundle.varies[i] for i in ids)
1040	        return (f'The {o} finding{ctx} rests on a {ma} design in one case and a {mb} design in the '
1041	                f'other; the evidence therefore establishes it more securely than either study alone.')
1042	    if bundle.kind == 'SAME_OUTCOME_DIFFERENT_HORIZON':
1043	        ha, hb = (bundle.varies[i] for i in ids)
1044	        return (f'These {o} estimates differ because they observe different time horizons{ctx}: the '
1045	                f'evidence is limited to the {ha} in one case and the {hb} in the other, and cannot '
1046	                f'distinguish a transitional effect from a settled one.')
1047	    if bundle.kind == 'UNCOUNTERED':
1048	        u = bundle.shared.get('unit_of_analysis', '')
1049	        return (f'The {o} result at the {u} level rests on a single source{ctx}, and no other study in '
1050	                f'this literature examines the same outcome at the same unit of analysis; the evidence '
1051	                f'is therefore limited to that setting and cannot distinguish a general pattern from a '
1052	                f'feature of one design.')
1053	    return ''
1054	
1055	
1056	def _boundary_text(bundle: Bundle, cf: dict[str, CardFacets]) -> str:
1057	    o, _o2 = _anchor_forms(bundle, cf)
1058	    if bundle.incomparability:
1059	        return (f'What the evidence does not settle is the magnitude: the estimates on {o} are not on '
1060	                f'the same footing, and the literature cannot distinguish a real difference from an '
1061	                f'artefact of how each was measured.')
1062	    return (f'What the evidence does not settle is whether the {o} result extends beyond the units and '
1063	            f'horizons these designs observe.')
1064	
1065	
1066	_BRIDGE = {
1067	    'unit_of_analysis': ('the evidence just reviewed observes the {a} level, and cannot establish what '
1068	                         'happens once these effects aggregate; the estimates that follow observe the '
1069	                         '{b} level'),
1070	    'method': ('the finding above rests on a {a} design; what follows asks whether it survives a {b} '
1071	               'design'),
1072	    'horizon': ('the estimates above are limited to the {a}; what follows observes the {b}'),
1073	    'outcome': ('the evidence above speaks to {a}; the estimates that follow measure {b}'),
1074	    'industry': ('the evidence above is drawn from {a}; what follows asks whether it holds in {b}'),
1075	}
1076	
1077	
1078	def _bridge_text(axis: str, a: str, b: str) -> str:
1079	    """AN ANALYTICAL MOVEMENT ALONG A NAMED AXIS. Never "Turning now to". If no axis moves between two
1080	    subsections, there is no movement to narrate and the planner emits NOTHING -- an invented transition
1081	    is a claim that an argument advanced when it did not."""
1082	    t = _BRIDGE.get(axis)
1083	    if not t or not a or not b or a == b:
1084	        return ''
1085	    return t.format(a=a.replace('_', ' '), b=b.replace('_', ' ')).capitalize() + '.'

1088	# ===================================================================== SUBSECTION PLANS
1089	
1090	@dataclass
1091	class SubsectionPlan:
1092	    section: str
1093	    subsection: str
1094	    thesis: SentenceIR | None
1095	    evidence: list[SentenceIR]
1096	    comparison: Bundle | None
1097	    comparable: bool
1098	    incomparability: list[str]
1099	    verdict: SentenceIR | None
1100	    boundary: SentenceIR | None
1101	    bridge: SentenceIR | None
1102	    card_ids: list[str]
1103	    refusals: list[str]
1104	
1105	    def sentence_ir(self) -> list[SentenceIR]:
1106	        out = [x for x in [self.thesis] if x]
1107	        out += self.evidence
1108	        out += [x for x in (self.verdict, self.boundary, self.bridge) if x]
1109	        return out

1112	def _relevance(sub: str, c: CardFacets, card: dict) -> int:
1113	    """Retrieval only -- NOT an evidence check. Scored against the SPAN and the declared fields, because
1114	    the claim is a paraphrase and we do not build on paraphrase."""
1115	    want = {w for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{4,}', sub.lower())}
1116	    blob = f"{card.get('span') or ''} {' '.join(card.get('mechanisms') or [])} " \
1117	           f"{c.f('outcome').value} {c.f('technology').value} {c.f('industry').value} " \
1118	           f"{c.f('unit_of_analysis').value} {c.f('method').value}"
1119	    have = {w for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{4,}', blob.lower())}
1120	    return len(want & have)
1121	
1122	
1123	def plan_subsections(cards: list[dict], cfs: list[CardFacets], bundles: list[Bundle],
1124	                     contract: ResearchContract) -> list[SubsectionPlan]:
1125	    cards_by_id = {c['id']: c for c in cards}
1126	    cf_by_id = {c.card_id: c for c in cfs}
1127	    surnames = _corpus_surnames(cards)
1128	    jobs = [(sec, sub) for sec, subs in contract.outline for sub in subs]
1129	    real = [b for b in bundles if b.kind not in ('NOT_A_COMPARISON',)]
1130	    used: set[tuple] = set()
1131	    plans: list[SubsectionPlan] = []
1132	
1133	    def roles_for(sub: str) -> list[str]:
1134	        for pat, kinds in contract.adjudicative_roles.items():
1135	            if re.search(pat, sub, re.I):
1136	                return kinds
1137	        return []
1138	
1139	    # PASS 1: THE ADJUDICATIVE SUBSECTIONS GET FIRST PICK. They are about the argument itself, so they
1140	    # are matched on bundle KIND, not on lexical overlap -- "Where the literature genuinely disagrees"
1141	    # shares no content word with any evidence card, and a lexical matcher hands it nothing at all. It
1142	    # was handed nothing, which is precisely how a Critical Synthesis section ends up 210 words long.
1143	    preassigned: dict[str, Bundle] = {}
1144	    for _sec, sub in jobs:
1145	        kinds = roles_for(sub)
1146	        if not kinds:
1147	            continue
1148	        cand = [b for b in real if b.kind in kinds and b.key() not in used]
1149	        cand.sort(key=lambda b: (kinds.index(b.kind), -b.score))
1150	        if cand:
1151	            preassigned[sub] = cand[0]
1152	            used.add(cand[0].key())
1153	
1154	    for sec, sub in jobs:
1155	        refusals: list[str] = []
1156	        # ONLY ELIGIBLE CARDS MAY BE ATTRIBUTED. A second-hand span ("Graetz and Michaels (2018) ...
1157	        # find that ...", sitting inside Damioli 2021) would otherwise be printed as "Writing in the
1158	        # Eurasian Business Review in 2021, Damioli et al. show that ..." -- crediting one paper with
1159	        # another's finding and another's figures.
1160	        pool = [c for c in cards if cf_by_id[c['id']].eligible]
1161	        n_drop = len(cards) - len(pool)
1162	        scored = sorted(((_relevance(sub, cf_by_id[c['id']], c), c) for c in pool),
1163	                        key=lambda x: -x[0])
1164	        sel = [c for s, c in scored[:12] if s > 0]
1165	        sel_ids = {c['id'] for c in sel}
1166	
1167	        # THE COMPARISON: an adjudicative subsection already claimed one by KIND in pass 1. A topical
1168	        # subsection takes the highest-value bundle whose BOTH cards are relevant to it and which no
1169	        # earlier subsection has argued. A bundle is NEVER reused -- 41 exact repetitions is what
1170	        # happens when 222 card slots are drawn from 82 cards with no bookkeeping anywhere.
1171	        bundle = preassigned.get(sub)
1172	        if bundle is None:
1173	            cand = [b for b in real if b.key() not in used and set(b.card_ids) <= sel_ids]
1174	            cand.sort(key=lambda b: -b.score)
1175	            bundle = cand[0] if cand else None
1176	            if bundle:
1177	                used.add(bundle.key())
1178	        if bundle is None:
1179	            refusals.append('no comparison bundle available whose cards are both relevant here -- this '
1180	                            'subsection can REPORT but must not ADJUDICATE')
1181	        else:
1182	            # an adjudicative subsection is argued FROM its bundle, so its cards must be in scope
1183	            sel_ids |= set(bundle.card_ids)
1184	            sel = sel + [cards_by_id[c] for c in bundle.card_ids if c not in {x['id'] for x in sel}]
1185	
1186	        # THE EVIDENCE CLAUSES: >= 2, each bound to a CARD_ID, each carrying its own VERBATIM SPAN.
1187	        ev_ids = list(bundle.card_ids) if bundle else []
1188	        for c in sel:
1189	            if len(ev_ids) >= 2:
1190	                break
1191	            if c['id'] not in ev_ids:
1192	                ev_ids.append(c['id'])
1193	        evidence = []
1194	        for cid in ev_ids:
1195	            c = cards_by_id[cid]
1196	            cfx = cf_by_id[cid]
1197	            figs = cfx.numbers
1198	            evidence.append(SentenceIR(
1199	                voice='attributed',
1200	                text=('State this source\'s finding and, where a figure is present, PRINT THE FIGURE. '
1201	                      'Every number must appear in the clause_text below.'),
1202	                source_clauses=[SourceClause(
1203	                    card_id=cid, clause_text=(c.get('span') or '').strip(),
1204	                    attribution=c.get('attribution', ''),
1205	                    unit=c.get('level', ''), method=c.get('method', ''), horizon=c.get('horizon', ''),
1206	                    figures=figs)],
1207	                premise_card_ids=[], role='evidence',
1208	                gate='span-verified at extraction; the writer is bound to clause_text byte-for-byte'))
1209	        if len(evidence) < 2:
1210	            refusals.append('fewer than 2 attributable cards -- no adjudication is possible here')
1211	
1212	        # THE OWNED VERDICT. Generated from declared fields + span-lifted surface forms, then VALIDATED.
1213	        verdict = boundary = thesis = None
1214	        comparable = bundle.comparable if bundle else False
1215	        incomp = bundle.incomparability if bundle else ['no comparison was selected']
1216	        if bundle:
1217	            prem = _premises(bundle, cards_by_id)
1218	            blob = ' '.join(p.text + ' ' + p.source for p in prem.values())
1219	            vt = _verdict_text(bundle, cf_by_id)
1220	            ok_safe, why_safe = owned_is_safe(vt, surnames, blob)
1221	            ok_gate, why_gate = validate(
1222	                Synthesis(bundle.operation, list(prem), vt), prem) if ok_safe else (False, why_safe)
1223	            if ok_safe and ok_gate:
1224	                verdict = SentenceIR(
1225	                    voice='owned', text=vt, source_clauses=[],
1226	                    premise_card_ids=list(bundle.card_ids), role='verdict',
1227	                    gate=f'synthesis_contract:{bundle.operation} (PASSED at plan time)')
1228	            else:
1229	                # DO NOT REPAIR. DO NOT SHIP. The plan records that it could not license a verdict.
1230	                refusals.append(f'OWNED verdict refused by the contract '
1231	                                f'({why_safe or why_gate}) -- no verdict is planned for this subsection')
1232	
1233	            bt = _boundary_text(bundle, cf_by_id)
1234	            ok_b, why_b = owned_is_safe(bt, surnames, blob)
1235	            if ok_b:
1236	                boundary = SentenceIR(voice='owned', text=bt, source_clauses=[],
1237	                                      premise_card_ids=list(bundle.card_ids), role='boundary',
1238	                                      gate='owned-safety (no source, no particular, no mechanism)')
1239	            else:
1240	                refusals.append(f'boundary refused: {why_b}')
1241	
1242	            th = (f'{sub.rstrip(".")} — the evidence below is adjudicated on '
1243	                  f'{bundle.axis.replace("_", " ")}, not merely listed.')
1244	            ok_t, why_t = owned_is_safe(th, surnames, blob)
1245	            thesis = SentenceIR(
1246	                voice='owned',
1247	                text=('Open with a CLAIM that states what this subsection ARGUES, not what it covers. '
1248	                      f'The argument turns on {bundle.axis.replace("_", " ")}: '
1249	                      + ' vs '.join(sorted(set(bundle.varies.values()))) + '.'),
1250	                source_clauses=[], premise_card_ids=list(bundle.card_ids), role='thesis',
1251	                gate='owned-safety' if ok_t else f'owned-safety FAILED: {why_t}')
1252	
1253	        plans.append(SubsectionPlan(
1254	            section=sec, subsection=sub, thesis=thesis, evidence=evidence, comparison=bundle,
1255	            comparable=comparable, incomparability=incomp, verdict=verdict, boundary=boundary,
1256	            bridge=None, card_ids=ev_ids, refusals=refusals))
1257	
1258	    # THE BRIDGES: computed from the DELTA between consecutive plans -- an analytical movement along a
1259	    # NAMED axis. Never "Turning now to". If no axis moves, there is no bridge and we say nothing.
1260	    for i, p in enumerate(plans[:-1]):
1261	        nxt = plans[i + 1]
1262	        if not (p.comparison and nxt.comparison):
1263	            continue
1264	        for axis in ('unit_of_analysis', 'outcome', 'method', 'horizon', 'industry'):
1265	            a = p.comparison.shared.get(axis) or next(iter(p.comparison.varies.values()), '')
1266	            b = nxt.comparison.shared.get(axis) or next(iter(nxt.comparison.varies.values()), '')
1267	            if a and b and a != b:
1268	                bt = _bridge_text(axis, a, b)
1269	                if not bt:
1270	                    continue
1271	                ok, why = owned_is_safe(bt, _corpus_surnames(cards), ' '.join(
1272	                    (cards_by_id[c].get('span') or '') for c in p.card_ids + nxt.card_ids))
1273	                if ok:
1274	                    p.bridge = SentenceIR(voice='owned', text=bt, source_clauses=[],
1275	                                          premise_card_ids=list(p.comparison.card_ids), role='bridge',
1276	                                          gate=f'owned-safety; analytical movement on {axis}')
1277	                break
1278	    return plans
1279
