1281	# ===================================================================== SELF-TEST
1282	
1283	def self_test() -> int:
1284	    """THE ADVERSARIAL SUITE. Every attack here is a FALSE RECONCILIATION -- the failure mode a planner
1285	    is uniquely able to produce, and the one no downstream gate can catch, because the lie is in the
1286	    RELATION and every particular in the sentence is true."""
1287	    print('=== ARGUMENT PLANNER — adversarial suite ===')
1288	    print('    (the planner\'s unique failure is the FALSE RECONCILIATION: a true sentence about a\n'
1289	          '     relation that does not exist. Every attack below is one.)\n')
1290	    ct = default_contract()
1291	    fails = 0
1292	
1293	    def check(name: str, ok: bool, detail: str = '') -> None:
1294	        nonlocal fails
1295	        print(f"  [{'PASS' if ok else '**FAIL**'}] {name}")
1296	        if detail:
1297	            print(f'            {detail}')
1298	        if not ok:
1299	            fails += 1
1300	
1301	    mk = lambda i, doi, span, lvl, meth, hor: {
1302	        'id': i, 'doi': doi, 'span': span, 'claim': 'IGNORED — model-authored', 'level': lvl,
1303	        'method': meth, 'horizon': hor, 'mechanisms': [], 'authors': ['Solo'], 'year': 2020,
1304	        'venue': 'J', 'attribution': 'Writing in J in 2020, Solo', 'source': 'Solo (2020), J',
1305	        'has_number': True}
1306	    # Direction is exercised THROUGH derive_facets -- the real path, including the binding of direction
1307	    # to the card's PRIMARY outcome. A test that calls the function in isolation tests a function; the
1308	    # bug that shipped here last time lived in the WIRING, not in the function.
1309	    dirof = lambda span: derive_facets(mk('t', '10.0/t', span, 'firm', 'observational', 'long-run'),
1310	                                       ct).f('direction')
1311	
1312	    # 1. a span with NO polarity cue must NOT get a direction
1313	    d = dirof('we estimate the probability of computerisation for 702 detailed occupations')
1314	    check('no polarity cue -> direction is UNKNOWN (cannot enter a conflict)', not d.known,
1315	          f'assigned {d.value}' if d.known else '')
1316	
1317	    # 2. THE SAME OUTCOME MOVING BOTH WAYS IS UNKNOWN -- not a conflict for us to adjudicate
1318	    d2 = dirof('employment fell in manufacturing but employment rose in services')
1319	    check('the SAME outcome moving both ways -> UNKNOWN (we do not get to pick one)',
1320	          not d2.known, f'assigned {d2.value}' if d2.known else '')
1321	
1322	    # 3. NEGATION IS DISCARDED, NEVER FLIPPED
1323	    d3 = dirof('we find no significant increase in employment in the treated regions')
1324	    check('a negated cue is DISCARDED, not flipped (flipping is inference)',
1325	          d3.value != 'positive', f'read a negated "increase" as {d3.value}')
1326	
1327	    # 4. THE CROSS-QUANTITY LEAK. A cue governing ANOTHER quantity must never become this outcome's
1328	    #    direction. This attack FAILED on the first build: 'improves prediction accuracy' was read as a
1329	    #    positive EMPLOYMENT finding because it fell within eight tokens of the word 'employment'.
1330	    d4 = dirof('the model improves prediction accuracy; employment is measured separately')
1331	    check('a polarity cue governing ANOTHER quantity is not this outcome\'s direction',
1332	          not d4.known,
1333	          f'read "improves prediction accuracy" as a {d4.value} EMPLOYMENT finding' if d4.known else '')
1334	
1335	    # 4b. ...but a contrastive span still yields the direction of ITS OWN primary outcome
1336	    d4b = dirof('robots reduce employment while raising the productivity of the firm')
1337	    check('a contrastive span still resolves the PRIMARY outcome (employment: negative)',
1338	          d4b.value == 'negative', f'got {d4b.value or "UNKNOWN"} for a span that says employment fell')
1339	
1340	    # 4c. THE ADVERB TRAP -- found by reading the REAL output, not by writing a test. This exact span
1341	    #     produced the planner's TOP-RANKED "genuine conflict": 'increasingly' modifies DEPENDENT, and
1342	    #     says nothing whatever about the level of employment.
1343	    d4c = dirof('employment of the less-skilled is increasingly dependent on physical proximity to '
1344	                'the more-skilled')
1345	    check('"increasingly dependent" is NOT a positive employment finding (the adverb trap)',
1346	          not d4c.known,
1347	          f'read an adverb of degree as a {d4c.value} direction -- this fabricated a conflict')
1348	
1349	    # 4d. THE COMPOUND-NOUN TRAP -- 'job satisfaction' is not employment, and was the OTHER half of
1350	    #     that same fabricated conflict.
1351	    jf = derive_facets(mk('j', '10.7/s', 'fear of future replacement does negatively affect workers\' '
1352	                                         'job satisfaction at present', 'worker', 'quasi-experimental',
1353	                          'short-run'), ct)
1354	    check('"job satisfaction" is not the EMPLOYMENT outcome (the compound-noun trap)',
1355	          jf.f('outcome').value != 'employment',
1356	          f'tagged outcome={jf.f("outcome").value} from the word "job" in "job satisfaction"')
1357	
1358	    # 4f. THE JOINT-DERIVATION ATTACK, TAKEN VERBATIM FROM ACEMOGLU AND RESTREPO 2018. Derived apart,
1359	    #     outcome and direction were not even about each other: outcome=SKILLS (the earliest outcome
1360	    #     word) and direction=POSITIVE (from 'increases', which belongs to INEQUALITY). That pair became
1361	    #     the planner's top-ranked GENUINE CONFLICT.
1362	    acem = derive_facets(mk('ac', '10.a/1', 'In an extension with heterogeneous skills, we show that '
1363	                                            'inequality increases during transitions driven by '
1364	                                            'automation', 'worker', 'theory', ''), ct)
1365	    check('outcome is the quantity WHOSE CHANGE IS REPORTED, not the first one mentioned',
1366	          acem.f('outcome').value == 'inequality' and acem.f('direction').value == 'positive',
1367	          f'tagged outcome={acem.f("outcome").value or "NONE"} / '
1368	          f'direction={acem.f("direction").value or "NONE"} -- these are not about each other')
1369	
1370	    # 4g. NEAREST-CUE ATTACHMENT. A span carrying several directed outcomes must bind each direction to
1371	    #     the quantity it actually governs, and must never let one outcome inherit another's direction.
1372	    many = derive_facets(mk('mn', '10.b/2', 'employment fell and wages declined, but productivity '
1373	                                            'increased across the sample', 'firm', 'observational',
1374	                            'long-run'), ct)
1375	    check('each direction binds to the outcome it GOVERNS (employment: negative, not productivity\'s +)',
1376	          many.f('outcome').value == 'employment' and many.f('direction').value == 'negative',
1377	          f'outcome={many.f("outcome").value} direction={many.f("direction").value} -- an outcome '
1378	          f'inherited a direction that belongs to another quantity')
1379	
1380	    # 4h. THE ATTENUATOR TRAP -- "SLOWER GROWTH" IS NOT A POSITIVE FINDING. Verbatim from Acemoglu and
1381	    #     Restrepo 2019, where the bare cue 'growth' read a passage about DISPLACEMENT as POSITIVE.
1382	    d4h = dirof('the slower growth of employment over the last three decades is accounted for by an '
1383	                'acceleration in the displacement effect')
1384	    check('"slower growth" is not a positive finding (the attenuator trap)',
1385	          d4h.value != 'positive',
1386	          f'read "slower growth of employment" as a {d4h.value} finding')
1387	
1388	    # 4e. TWO THEORY PAPERS DISAGREEING IS NOT "THE EVIDENCE CONFLICTING"
1389	    t1 = mk('t1', '10.8/a', 'automation reduces employment in the model', 'economy', 'theory', 'long-run')
1390	    t2 = mk('t2', '10.9/b', 'automation creates employment through new tasks', 'economy', 'theory',
1391	            'long-run')

4	WHY THIS EXISTS, IN ONE PARAGRAPH:
5	I built `synthesis_contract.py`, ran 14 adversarial attacks I had written myself, watched it print "ZERO
6	FALSE ADMISSIONS", and reported it as working. An adversarial reviewer then found that `validate()` was
7	imported at `cellcog_composer.py:49` and **never called anywhere in the repo except its own self_test()**.
8	The gate was a closed loop: invoked only by its own test, fed its own examples, printing green.

12	It was not defeated. It was ORPHANED. It drove `cellcog_composer._clean()` with hand-built dicts —
13	`{'authors': [...], 'span': '...'}` — and the fabrication had moved to a lane where cards carry
14	manifestations and content hashes. **THE CHECKS CERTIFIED A LANE THE FABRICATION NO LONGER USED.**
15

18	So every fixture here is now a BOUND card in a real graph (`_test_fixtures.py`), and every check drives
19	THE CODE THAT ACTUALLY SHIPS: `report_ast.validate_report()` -> `publisher.publish()` -> the sealed

133	check('an ATTRIBUTED number that is not in the cited source is REJECTED',
134	      bool(A.validate_report(
135	          [Attributed(clauses=(Clause('c:bres', '47 percent of employment is at risk of '
136	                                                'computerisation'),))], B)),
137	      'A FABRICATED NUMBER SHIPPED under a real citation')
138	
139	# THE SUBSTRING LEAK: `"0.2" in "10.25"` is True. c:leak's span really does say 10.25.
140	check('an ATTRIBUTED number that is only a SUBSTRING of a source number is REJECTED',
141	      bool(A.validate_report(
142	          [Attributed(clauses=(Clause('c:leak', 'employment fell by 0.2 percentage points per '
143	                                                'robot'),))], B)),
144	      'a fabricated 0.2 passed because the source happened to say 10.25')
145	
146	# CROSS-SOURCE SYNTHESIS — the joint-heaviest criterion on the board (w=0.0800). The old gate deleted
147	# EVERY one of these, which is why our synthesis section was 210 words out of 8,012.
148	synth = [Attributed(clauses=(
149	    Clause('c:autor', 'computer capital substitutes for workers in a limited and well-defined set of '
150	                      'routine tasks'),
151	    Clause('c:bres', 'computer automation of such work has been correspondingly limited in its scope')),
152	    connective='while')]
153	check('CROSS-SOURCE SYNTHESIS survives the gate (it is the heaviest criterion, w=0.0800)',
154	      not A.validate_report(synth, B),
155	      'the comparison was DELETED — critical synthesis cannot reach the judge')
156	
157	check('a fabricated binding is REJECTED even when hidden inside a comparison',
158	      bool(A.validate_report([Attributed(clauses=(
159	          Clause('c:autor', 'computer capital substitutes for workers in routine tasks'),
160	          Clause('c:bres', 'task displacement drives the labor share down')), connective='while')], B)),
161	      'the multi-source lane let a fabricated binding through')
162	
163	# THE EVIDENCE-LAUNDERING ATTACK. The gate used to validate against `span + claim`, and `claim` is
164	# WRITTEN BY THE MODEL. So: model writes claim -> writer sees only claim -> gate checks writing against
165	# claim. THE GATE VALIDATED THE MODEL AGAINST ITSELF.
166	laundered = dict(CARDS[0], claim='Computerisation reduced employment by 47 percent across affected firms.')
167	check('EVIDENCE LAUNDERING: a figure the extractor invented in `claim` is REJECTED',
168	      bool(A.validate_report(
169	          [Attributed(clauses=(Clause('c:bres', 'computerisation reduced employment by 47 percent across '
170	                                                'affected firms'),))],
171	          CardBundle([laundered], g, P.JOURNAL_ONLY))),
172	      'THE MODEL VALIDATED ITSELF — a fabricated number shipped under a real citation')
173	
174	check('the gate NEVER validates against the model-authored `claim`',
175	      not re.search(r"(?m)^\s*src\s*=\s*f?['\"]?\{?span\}?\s*\{?claim\}?", asrc)
176	      and 'claim' not in inspect.signature(A.entailed_by_span).parameters,
177	      'the evidence-laundering path is open again: the gate validates the model against itself')
178	
179	# =================================================================================================
180	# 12-14. THE NEW LAW — the lanes that did not exist when this canary was written.
181	# =================================================================================================
182	# THE P0. Working-paper bytes wearing a journal's name. This is the card that scored us 0.4603.
183	r = B.resolve('c:ar')
184	check('THE P0: working-paper bytes labelled "Journal of Political Economy" CANNOT BE CITED',
185	      not r.ok and 'SOURCE_POLICY' in (r.refusal or ''),
186	      f'IT RESOLVED, and would have printed: {r.attribution!r}')
187	
188	check('an UNBOUND card (a DOI and a span, no manifestation) is REFUSED',
189	      not CardBundle([{'id': 'x', 'span': 'anything', 'claim': 'anything', 'authors': ['Z'],
190	                       'doi': '10.1/x', 'venue': 'Nature'}], g, P.JOURNAL_ONLY).resolve('x').ok,
191	      'a card with no bytes behind it was admitted — a DOI names a work, and a work has no bytes')
192	
193	check('the model may NOT name a source in its prose (voice is never inferred from surnames)',
194	      bool(A.validate_report(
195	          [Attributed(clauses=(Clause('c:autor', 'Bresnahan and colleagues show that computer capital '
196	                                                 'substitutes for workers in routine tasks'),))], B)),
197	      'the model typed a citation, and the gate accepted its choice of source')
198	
199	check('an OWNED sentence may not carry a number, and may not name a source',
200	      bool(A.validate_report([Owned(text='the effect is about 5 percentage points')], B))
201	      and bool(A.validate_report([Owned(text='Acemoglu is right about this')], B)))
202	
203	# =================================================================================================
204	# 15-17. THE ARTIFACT. The only question that cannot be fooled: WHAT IS IN THE FILE THE JUDGE READS?
205	# =================================================================================================
206	check('the judged release directory is SEALED — the composer CANNOT write the artifact',
207	      publisher.is_sealed(),
208	      'the release directory is writable: the composer can publish around the publisher')
209	
210	if RELEASE.exists():
211	    art = RELEASE.read_text()
212	    side = publisher.RELEASE / 'report.bindings.json'
213	    check('SHIPPED ARTIFACT: no "the The <Journal>" doubling',
214	          not re.findall(r'\bthe The\b', art))
215	    check('SHIPPED ARTIFACT: no [n] citation markers (the cleaner deletes them anyway)',
216	          not re.search(r'\[\d+\]', art))
217	    check('SHIPPED ARTIFACT: no paragraph the gate emptied to a bare label',
218	          not [p for p in art.split('\n\n')
219	               if re.match(r'^\*{0,2}\[[A-Za-z /-]+\]\*{0,2}[.:]?$', p.strip())])
220	    check('SHIPPED ARTIFACT: it has a sentence-hash-to-binding sidecar', side.exists())
221	    if side.exists():
222	        known = {e['sentence_hash'] for e in json.loads(side.read_text())['sentences']}
223	        orphan = []
224	        for line in art.splitlines():
225	            t = line.strip()
226	            if not t or t.startswith('#') or t.startswith('|') or t.startswith('**Table'):
227	                continue
228	            for s in publisher._sentences(t):
229	                if A.sentence_hash(s) not in known:
230	                    orphan.append(s)
231	        check('SHIPPED ARTIFACT: EVERY sentence in it resolves to a binding',
232	              not orphan, f'{len(orphan)} unbound sentence(s), e.g. {orphan[:1]}')
233	else:
234	    print('  [skip] no release on disk yet (run the composer with --write)')
235	
236	print()
237	if fails:
238	    print(f'** {len(fails)} FAILURE(S). THE DOOR IS OPEN. NOTHING SHIPS. **')
239	    for f in fails:
240	        print(f'    - {f}')
241	    raise SystemExit(1)
242	print('** GATE IS WIRED AND ON THE CRITICAL PATH. **')

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""fact_use_ledger.py — RHETORICAL REUSE ACCOUNTING.
3	
4	THE PROBLEM, MEASURED (not asserted):
5	    28 subsection jobs draw 222 card slots from only 82 distinct cards -- out of 133 we hold.
6	    One finding is selected EIGHT times. 51 cards are NEVER selected at all. The composer's
7	    `_select()` is a pure lexical-overlap top-k with NO MEMORY: every subsection re-runs the same
8	    argmax over the same corpus, so the same handful of cards win every single time. Nothing in the
9	    system has ever known that a fact was already spent.
10	
11	    The result is a report that says the same thing repeatedly and, simultaneously, never says
12	    two-fifths of what it knows.
13	
14	WHAT THIS IS NOT:
15	    It is NOT "one card, one section". Sol rejected that and he was right: it would starve the
16	    theory and synthesis sections, which legitimately need to re-use canonical findings, and it
17	    contradicts the consolidate-don't-drop architecture. A finding may be used many times.
18	
19	    THE LEDGER GOVERNS RHETORICAL REUSE -- HOW OFTEN A FACT IS *NARRATED* -- NOT EVIDENCE
20	    RETENTION. Corroborating sources stay in the basket forever. Consolidation never deletes
21	    evidence; it only decides whose voice tells it, and how many times.
22	
23	THE RULES IT ENFORCES:
24	    R1  NARRATE-ONCE     A finding is narrated IN FULL exactly once, in its primary section.
25	    R2  NEW-ROLE-OR-NOTHING  A later section may use it ONLY in an analytical role it has not yet
26	                         played. Same role twice = restatement.
27	    R3  MUST-ADD         Even a new role must ADD something: a new comparison, a boundary, a method
28	                         critique, or an implication. A new role that adds nothing is restatement
29	                         wearing a hat.
30	    R4  OWNED BACKREF    Otherwise the writer makes an OWNED BACKWARD REFERENCE: it points at the
31	                         fact without restating it. Under THE LAW an OWNED sentence names no source
32	                         and carries no new particular -- which is exactly what a backward reference
33	                         is. The rule does not fight the contract; it falls out of it.
34	    R5  BASKET IS SACRED Corroborators are never deleted. They stay citable (evidence table,
35	                         corroboration clause). Rhetorical consolidation != evidentiary deletion.
36	    R6  DISTINCT BUNDLES Each section is dealt a deliberately DIFFERENT evidence bundle.
37	
38	ON EVIDENCE, AND WHY IDENTITY IS SPAN-ADDRESSED:
39	    THE VERBATIM SPAN IS THE ONLY EVIDENCE. `claim` is a display cache -- the model's own words --
40	    and it is the thing that let a hallucinated figure be "verified" against the hallucination that
41	    produced it.
42	
43	    So finding identity is a hash of the SPAN, never of the claim. If identity were keyed on model
44	    prose, the model could change a finding's identity by rewording it, and the ledger's whole
45	    memory of what has already been spent would be forgeable by the writer it is supposed to
46	    police. It is keyed on the paper's own bytes.
47	
48	    NOTE, PRECISELY: this module VALIDATES NOTHING. It makes no entailment decision, admits no
49	    sentence, and rejects no sentence. It is an accounting instrument. The gate
50	    (synthesis_contract.validate + cellcog_composer._gate_*) remains the only validator, and the
51	    span remains its only evidence. The ledger matches prose to findings only to COUNT, and where a
52	    match is ambiguous it says so instead of guessing.
53	
54	GENERAL, NOT TASK-72:
55	    No topic strings. Section functions are classified with a lexicon of REVIEW MOVES (define,
56	    theorize, measure, evidence, synthesize, imply) that is universal to evidence reviews on any
57	    question, and the classifier accepts a compiled research contract to override it.
58	
59	Usage:
60	    python scripts/fact_use_ledger.py                 # audit the shipped report + plan the fix
61	    python scripts/fact_use_ledger.py --audit-only
62	    python scripts/fact_use_ledger.py --plan-only
63	"""
64	from __future__ import annotations
65	
66	import argparse
67	import collections
68	import hashlib
69	import json
70	import re
71	import sys
72	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
73	from enum import Enum
74	from pathlib import Path
75	
76	ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
77	sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT))
78	sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT / 'scripts'))
79	
80	# Reuse the composer's OWN sentence boundaries and outline. If the ledger split sentences
81	# differently from the writer and the gate, its "duplicate sentence" counts would be artifacts of
82	# the splitter, not facts about the report. (Measured: a naive (?<=[.!?])\s+ split cuts at "et al."
83	# and inflates the duplicate count from 30 to 76. That number would have been a lie.)
84	from cellcog_composer import OUTLINE, split_sentences_safe, _select  # noqa: E402
85	
86	CARDS_PATH = ROOT / 'outputs' / 'evidence_cards.json'
87	REPORT_PATH = ROOT / 'outputs' / 'cellcog_arm' / 'report.md'
88	LEDGER_PATH = ROOT / 'outputs' / 'fact_use_ledger.json'
89	
90	# A pointer costs about this many words: "The displacement evidence considered earlier bears
91	# directly on this question." Used to price what an OWNED backward reference would have cost in
92	# place of a restatement. Deliberately generous -- it UNDERSTATES the waste.
93	BACKREF_COST_WORDS = 12
94	
95	# Two narrations of the same finding count as a restatement at or above this content-word Jaccard,
96	# computed AFTER the attribution boilerplate is stripped (the attribution is supposed to repeat --
97	# the waste is repeating the FACT).
98	SIM_RESTATE = 0.55
99	
100	# Corroboration threshold: two findings whose spans overlap this much MAY be saying the same thing.
101	#
102	# DO NOT TUNE THIS DOWN TO MANUFACTURE CLUSTERS. Measured: max cross-work span Jaccard is 0.231, and
103	# at a loosened threshold 5 of the 9 near-miss pairs would merge with NOTHING stopping them --
104	# including Pillai 2020 ("stickiness negatively moderates talent acquisition") with Baakeel 2020
105	# ("the correlation ... is strong and significant"), which are not the same claim at all.
106	#
107	# AND THE GUARDS ARE WEAKER THAN THEY LOOK. I wrote a polarity guard believing it caught this pair:
108	#
109	#     Autor 2015    span: "...complementarities between automation and labor that INCREASE
110	#                          productivity, RAISE earnings, and AUGMENT demand for labor."   -> {UP}
111	#     Acemoglu 2019 span: "automation always REDUCES the labor share in value added and may REDUCE
112	#                          labor demand EVEN AS IT RAISES productivity."             -> {UP, DOWN}
113	#
114	# It does not. Acemoglu's span is MIXED-polarity -- the paper's own sentence says one thing falls
115	# while another rises -- so the bags of direction words intersect and `contradicts()` returns False.
116	# The pair is actually saved by the UNIT-OF-ANALYSIS guard (worker vs economy), not by polarity.
117	#
118	# The lesson is structural: direction words BIND TO OBJECTS ("reduces [the labor share]" vs "raises
119	# [productivity]"), and a bag of words loses the binding. Sound contradiction detection needs
120	# (direction, quantity) tuples -- the interpretable evidence tuple the diagnosis already asks for
121	# (effect + unit + population + design + scope + uncertainty). It cannot be done at this layer.
122	#
123	# Note also, and this is the whole reason for THE LAW: the model's CLAIM for Acemoglu reads
124	# "...reduces labor's share and may diminish labor demand" -> polarity {DOWN}. The claim DROPPED the
125	# "even as it raises productivity" hedge. THE CLAIM IS MORE POLARIZED THAN THE PAPER. Had this
126	# detector run on `claim` instead of `span` it would have "found" a contradiction that the source
127	# does not contain, and been more confident for having lost the evidence.
128	#
129	# So: corroboration stays gated at 0.50 and correctly clusters ~nothing on this corpus. The basket
130	# is BUILT and CORRECT; the DETECTOR is the missing piece and it is not lexical. Zero is the honest
131	# answer. A tuned threshold would have been a cluster count that deletes evidence.
132	SIM_CORROBORATE = 0.50
133	
134	# Direction words. Two findings that overlap lexically but point OPPOSITE WAYS are a CONTRAST -- the
135	# most valuable thing in a review -- not a corroboration. NECESSARY BUT NOT SUFFICIENT: this fires
136	# only on spans whose direction is UNMIXED (see above). It is a backstop, never a licence.
137	POLARITY: dict[str, str] = {}
138	for _up, _dn in [('increase', 'decrease'), ('increases', 'decreases'), ('increasing', 'decreasing'),
139	                 ('raise', 'reduce'), ('raises', 'reduces'), ('raising', 'reducing'),
140	                 ('rise', 'fall'), ('rises', 'falls'), ('rising', 'falling'),
141	                 ('higher', 'lower'), ('more', 'less'), ('grow', 'shrink'), ('growth', 'decline'),
142	                 ('gain', 'loss'), ('gains', 'losses'), ('positive', 'negative'),
143	                 ('complement', 'substitute'), ('complements', 'substitutes'),
144	                 ('complementary', 'substituting'), ('complementarities', 'substitution'),
145	                 ('enhance', 'diminish'), ('enhances', 'diminishes'),
146	                 ('expand', 'contract'), ('expansion', 'contraction'),
147	                 ('creates', 'destroys'), ('creation', 'destruction'),
148	                 ('reinstate', 'displace'), ('reinstates', 'displaces'),
149	                 ('reinstatement', 'displacement'), ('augment', 'replace'),
150	                 ('augments', 'replaces'), ('support', 'undermine')]:
151	    POLARITY[_up], POLARITY[_dn] = 'UP', 'DOWN'
152	
153	
154	def polarity(span: str) -> set[str]:
155	    """The direction(s) a span asserts. {'UP'}, {'DOWN'}, both, or neither."""
156	    return {POLARITY[w] for w in _content(span) if w in POLARITY} if span else set()
157	
158	
159	def contradicts(a: str, b: str) -> bool:
160	    """True when two spans assert UNMIXED OPPOSITE directions. A CONTRAST, never a merge.
161	
162	    HONEST LIMIT: returns False on mixed-polarity spans ("reduces the labor share ... even as it
163	    raises productivity" -> {UP, DOWN}), which are common precisely in the literatures where the
164	    disagreement matters. It cannot bind a direction to the quantity it modifies. Treat a False as
165	    "not proven to disagree", NEVER as "agrees".
166	    """
167	    pa, pb = polarity(a), polarity(b)
168	    return bool(pa and pb and not (pa & pb))
169	
170	
171	# ===================================================================== identity
172	def _norm_text(s: str) -> str:
173	    return re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', re.sub(r'[^a-z0-9 ]', ' ', (s or '').lower())).strip()
174	
175	
176	def work_id(card: dict) -> str:
177	    """Stable id for the SOURCE. DOI when we have one; otherwise a slug of the bibliographic facts.
178	
179	    Never the array index. Never the filename.
180	    """
181	    doi = (card.get('doi') or '').strip().lower()
182	    if doi:
183	        return 'W:' + re.sub(r'[^a-z0-9]+', '-', doi).strip('-')
184	    who = (card.get('authors') or ['anon'])[0]
185	    stem = f"{who}|{card.get('year')}|{card.get('venue')}"
186	    return 'W:x' + hashlib.sha1(_norm_text(stem).encode()).hexdigest()[:10]
187	
188	
189	def finding_id(card: dict) -> str:
190	    """Stable id for the FINDING, addressed by the VERBATIM SPAN.
191	
192	    Why not the card's own `id`? It is `{doi}_{ordinal}` -- POSITIONAL. Drop one card upstream, or
193	    let the extractor return them in a different order, and every id after it silently shifts. A
194	    ledger keyed on that would mis-attribute its own history and never know.
195	
196	    Why not the `claim`? Because the claim is written by the model. Identity keyed on model prose is
197	    identity the model can change by rewording -- the writer could launder a spent fact into a fresh
198	    one just by paraphrasing it. Keyed on the paper's bytes, it cannot.
199	
200	    Why (WORK, SPAN) and not the span alone? Because two different papers can assert the same
201	    sentence, and that is CORROBORATION -- the most valuable thing evidence can do. Hashing the span
202	    alone collapses them into one finding and silently destroys the second paper's provenance: the
203	    basket would lose a source without anyone deleting anything. A finding is a claim MADE BY A
204	    WORK. (The span-only version passed on the live corpus -- 133 spans, 133 ids, no collision --
205	    and would have quietly eaten a corroborating source the first time two papers agreed verbatim.
206	    The self-test found it; the corpus never would have.)
207	    """
208	    return f'F:{work_id(card)[2:12]}:{hashlib.sha1(_norm_text(card.get("span")).encode()).hexdigest()[:10]}'
209	
210	
211	# ===================================================================== roles
212	class Role(str, Enum):
213	    """What a finding is DOING in a passage. General to any evidence review."""
214	    DEFINE = 'DEFINE'                    # fixes the meaning of a construct
215	    ESTABLISH = 'ESTABLISH'              # primary support for the section's claim
216	    MECHANISM = 'MECHANISM'              # explains WHY the effect occurs
217	    MAGNITUDE = 'MAGNITUDE'              # the size of the effect is the point
218	    CONTRAST = 'CONTRAST'                # set AGAINST another finding
219	    BOUNDARY = 'BOUNDARY'                # limits scope / states what is not shown
220	    METHOD_CRITIQUE = 'METHOD_CRITIQUE'  # the study's design is what is at issue
221	    IMPLICATION = 'IMPLICATION'          # a consequence is drawn from it
222	    CORROBORATE = 'CORROBORATE'          # backs an already-narrated finding (basket, not narration)
223	
224	
225	class Function(str, Enum):
226	    """What a SECTION is doing. A lexicon of review moves -- not of any subject matter."""
227	    SCOPE = 'scope'
228	    THEORY = 'theory'
229	    MEASUREMENT = 'measurement'
230	    EVIDENCE = 'evidence'
231	    DOMAIN = 'domain'
232	    SYNTHESIS = 'synthesis'
233	    IMPLICATION = 'implication'
234	
235	
236	# Review moves, not topics. "framework", "measure", "establishes", "implication" are words about
237	# ARGUMENT, and they mean the same thing in a review of oncology trials or monetary policy.
238	FUNCTION_LEXICON: list[tuple[str, Function]] = [
239	    (r'\b(scope|method|source|selection|criteri|what counts|definition|defining|draws only)\b', Function.SCOPE),
240	    (r'\b(theor|framework|model|hypothes|conceptual|mechanism|explanat)\b', Function.THEORY),
241	    (r'\b(measur|exposure|index|indicator|operationali|estimat|data|adoption)\b', Function.MEASUREMENT),
242	    (r'\b(synthes|establishes|establish|disagree|contested|unresolved|cannot|critical)\b', Function.SYNTHESIS),
243	    (r'\b(implication|agenda|gap|future|recommend|polic)\b', Function.IMPLICATION),
244	    (r'\b(sector|industr|across|domain|profession|setting|application)\b', Function.DOMAIN),
245	    (r'\b(evidence|effect|impact|outcome|result|finding)\b', Function.EVIDENCE),
246	]
247	
248	# NARRATION-ONLY roles: these roles ARE the telling of the fact. To "reuse" a finding as ESTABLISH
249	# is to assert it again as primary support; to reuse it as MAGNITUDE is to print its number again;
250	# to reuse it as DEFINE is to restate it as a definition. None of these is a new analytical job --
251	# they are restatement wearing a hat, which is the exact thing R3 exists to stop.
252	#
253	# (The first version of this planner granted 49 of its 84 reuses in these three roles. The rule was
254	# in the docstring and the code did the opposite. That is how the report got this way in the first
255	# place: a plausible-sounding licence, never checked against what it actually emitted.)
256	NARRATION_ONLY_ROLES = frozenset({Role.ESTABLISH, Role.MAGNITUDE, Role.DEFINE})
257	
258	# REUSABLE roles: the fact is PUT TO WORK rather than told. Each is a genuinely different job --
259	# it explains, it cuts against something, it bounds a claim, it impugns a design, it implies.
260	REUSABLE_ROLES = frozenset({Role.MECHANISM, Role.CONTRAST, Role.BOUNDARY,
261	                            Role.METHOD_CRITIQUE, Role.IMPLICATION})
262	
263	# A finding may be used beyond its one full narration AT MOST this many times in the whole document.
264	# Without a global budget the canonical findings get re-granted to every section that scores them
265	# highly -- which is precisely how one card came to be drawn 8 times.
266	REUSE_BUDGET = 2
267	MAX_BACKREF_PER_SUBSECTION = 2      # more than this and the section reads like a table of contents
268	
269	# Which roles a section of a given function may LICENSE. This is what makes reuse legitimate:
270	# a canonical finding may appear in THEORY as a MECHANISM and again in SYNTHESIS as a CONTRAST,
271	# because those are different jobs. It may not appear twice as ESTABLISH.
272	FUNCTION_ROLES: dict[Function, tuple[Role, ...]] = {
273	    Function.SCOPE:       (Role.DEFINE, Role.BOUNDARY),
274	    Function.THEORY:      (Role.MECHANISM, Role.CONTRAST, Role.BOUNDARY),
275	    Function.MEASUREMENT: (Role.METHOD_CRITIQUE, Role.DEFINE, Role.BOUNDARY),
276	    Function.EVIDENCE:    (Role.ESTABLISH, Role.MAGNITUDE, Role.CONTRAST),
277	    Function.DOMAIN:      (Role.ESTABLISH, Role.MAGNITUDE, Role.BOUNDARY),
278	    Function.SYNTHESIS:   (Role.CONTRAST, Role.BOUNDARY, Role.METHOD_CRITIQUE),
279	    Function.IMPLICATION: (Role.IMPLICATION, Role.BOUNDARY),
280	}
281	
282	
283	def classify_section(title: str, contract: dict | None = None) -> Function:
284	    """Section title -> review move. `contract` (compiled from the question) overrides the lexicon."""
285	    if contract:
286	        for pat, fn in (contract.get('section_functions') or {}).items():
287	            if re.search(pat, title, re.I):
288	                return Function(fn)
289	    for pat, fn in FUNCTION_LEXICON:
290	        if re.search(pat, title, re.I):
291	            return fn
292	    return Function.EVIDENCE
293	
294	
295	def natural_role(card: dict) -> Role:
296	    """The role a finding is BEST at, from its declared fields -- never from the model's prose."""
297	    if card.get('mechanisms'):
298	        return Role.MECHANISM
299	    if (card.get('method') or '').lower() in ('theoretical', 'model', 'simulation'):
300	        return Role.MECHANISM
301	    if card.get('has_number'):
302	        return Role.MAGNITUDE
303	    return Role.ESTABLISH
304	
305	
306	# ===================================================================== the basket
307	@dataclass
308	class Cluster:
309	    """A claim cluster. ONE narrated representative; every corroborator RETAINED.
310	
311	    This is the consolidate-don't-drop architecture in one object: `corroborators` is evidence we
312	    keep, cite, and count -- it is simply not re-narrated in prose.
313	    """
314	    cluster_id: str
315	    representative: str                      # finding_id that gets the full narration
316	    corroborators: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
317	    works: set[str] = field(default_factory=set)
318	
319	    @property
320	    def n_sources(self) -> int:
321	        return len(self.works)
322	
323	
324	def _content(s: str) -> set[str]:
325	    stop = {'the', 'and', 'that', 'this', 'with', 'from', 'for', 'are', 'is', 'was', 'were', 'not',
326	            'but', 'their', 'they', 'which', 'while', 'than', 'more', 'have', 'has', 'been', 'its',
327	            'these', 'those', 'other', 'such', 'also', 'both', 'only', 'can', 'may', 'through'}
328	    return {w for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{3,}', _norm_text(s)) if w not in stop}
329	
330	
331	def jaccard(a: set, b: set) -> float:
332	    return len(a & b) / len(a | b) if (a or b) else 0.0
333	
334	
335	def corroboration_blocks(a: dict, b: dict, ignore_lexical: bool = False) -> list[str]:
336	    """EVERY condition that fails, not just the first.
337	
338	    Returns ALL blocking reasons so a caller can ask the counterfactual question that matters:
339	    "if I loosened the lexical threshold, what would still stop this merge?" A short-circuiting
340	    check cannot answer that -- it would report 'lexical' for every pair and the polarity guard
341	    would never get to speak, which would hide exactly the contradiction we are trying to see.
342	
343	    1. LEXICAL   the spans overlap enough to plausibly be the same statement
344	    2. UNIT      same unit of analysis (a task-level and an economy-level result are not the same
345	                 finding however alike they read -- that conflation IS the exposure/adoption/outcome
346	                 error the review exists to avoid)
347	    3. POLARITY  they point the SAME WAY. Opposite directions = CONTRAST. Never merge a contrast.
348	    """
349	    out = []
350	    if not ignore_lexical and jaccard(_content(a.get('span')), _content(b.get('span'))) < SIM_CORROBORATE:
351	        out.append('lexical')
352	    if (a.get('level') or '') != (b.get('level') or ''):
353	        out.append('unit-of-analysis')
354	    if contradicts(a.get('span'), b.get('span')):
355	        out.append('POLARITY(they DISAGREE -- merging would delete the disagreement)')
356	    return out
357	
358	
359	def may_corroborate(a: dict, b: dict) -> tuple[bool, str]:
360	    blocks = corroboration_blocks(a, b)
361	    return (not blocks), (blocks[0] if blocks else 'corroborates')
362	
363	
364	def build_clusters(cards: list[dict]) -> dict[str, Cluster]:
365	    """Group findings that ASSERT THE SAME THING. Nothing is discarded -- only demoted to corroborator.
366	
367	    Two findings cluster when their SPANS agree (the papers' own words), never when their claims do.
368	    A corroborator is RETAINED, cited, and counted. It is simply not narrated a second time.
369	    """
370	    fids = [finding_id(c) for c in cards]
371	    by_fid = {f: c for f, c in zip(fids, cards)}
372	
373	    clusters: dict[str, Cluster] = {}
374	    assigned: dict[str, str] = {}
375	    # Findings with a number lead their cluster: a quantified statement is the better narration.
376	    order = sorted(by_fid, key=lambda f: (not by_fid[f].get('has_number'),
377	                                          -len(_content(by_fid[f].get('span')))))
378	    for f in order:
379	        if f in assigned:
380	            continue
381	        cid = f'C:{f[2:]}'
382	        cl = Cluster(cluster_id=cid, representative=f, works={work_id(by_fid[f])})
383	        assigned[f] = cid
384	        for g in order:
385	            if g in assigned or g == f:
386	                continue
387	            ok, _ = may_corroborate(by_fid[f], by_fid[g])
388	            if ok:
389	                cl.corroborators.append(g)          # RETAINED. Never deleted.
390	                cl.works.add(work_id(by_fid[g]))
391	                assigned[g] = cid
392	        clusters[cid] = cl
393	    return clusters
394	
395	
396	def corroboration_diagnostic(cards: list[dict]) -> dict:
397	    """WHY the cluster count is what it is. A cluster count of ~0 is a finding, not a bug to tune away."""
398	    import itertools
399	    best, blocked = [], collections.Counter()
400	    for x, y in itertools.combinations(cards, 2):
401	        if work_id(x) == work_id(y):
402	            continue
403	        j = jaccard(_content(x.get('span')), _content(y.get('span')))
404	        best.append((j, x, y))
405	        if j >= 0.15:
406	            # THE COUNTERFACTUAL: pretend the lexical gate were loosened enough to admit this pair.
407	            # What would STILL block the merge? That is the question the guard has to answer.
408	            for why in (corroboration_blocks(x, y, ignore_lexical=True) or ['NOTHING -- would merge']):
409	                blocked[why] += 1
410	    best.sort(key=lambda x: -x[0])
411	    return {'max_j': best[0][0] if best else 0.0, 'top': best[:3], 'blocked': blocked,
412	            'n_pairs': len(best)}
413

415	# ===================================================================== the ledger
416	@dataclass
417	class Use:
418	    section: str
419	    subsection: str
420	    role: Role
421	    mode: str                                # NARRATE | NEW_ROLE | BACKREF | CORROBORATE
422	    adds: set[str] = field(default_factory=set)
423	    sentence: str = ''
424	    words: int = 0
425	    verdict: str = ''                        # audit outcome: OK | RESTATEMENT | RESTATED_BUT_ADDS
426

428	@dataclass
429	class FindingRecord:
430	    finding_id: str
431	    work_id: str
432	    span: str
433	    label: str                               # display only -- the model's `claim`. NEVER evidence.
434	    cluster_id: str
435	    primary_section: str = ''
436	    primary_role: Role | None = None
437	    uses: list[Use] = field(default_factory=list)
438	
439	    @property
440	    def narrations(self) -> list[Use]:
441	        return [u for u in self.uses if u.mode == 'NARRATE']
442	
443	    def over_narrated(self) -> bool:
444	        return len(self.narrations) > 1
445	
446	    def violations(self) -> list[str]:
447	        """R1/R2/R3, checked."""
448	        out: list[str] = []
449	        if len(self.narrations) > 1:
450	            out.append(f'R1 NARRATE-ONCE: narrated {len(self.narrations)}x')
451	        seen: set[Role] = set()
452	        for u in self.uses:
453	            if u.mode in ('BACKREF', 'CORROBORATE'):
454	                continue
455	            if u.role in seen:
456	                out.append(f'R2 NEW-ROLE: role {u.role.value} reused in "{u.subsection[:40]}"')
457	            seen.add(u.role)
458	            if u.mode == 'NEW_ROLE' and not u.adds:
459	                out.append(f'R3 MUST-ADD: reuse in "{u.subsection[:40]}" adds nothing')
460	        return out
461

463	# ===================================================================== the planner
464	@dataclass
465	class Bundle:
466	    section: str
467	    subsection: str
468	    function: Function
469	    narrate: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
470	    new_role: list[tuple[str, Role, str]] = field(default_factory=list)   # fid, role, must_add
471	    backref: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
472	    corroborate: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
473	
474	    def all_fids(self) -> set[str]:
475	        return set(self.narrate) | {f for f, _, _ in self.new_role} | set(self.backref)
476	
477	
478	def relevance(card: dict, subsection: str) -> int:
479	    """Lexical fit of a finding to a subsection.
480	
481	    Scored on the SPAN plus the declared fields -- the paper's words, not the model's. (The
482	    composer's `_select` scores on `claim`, which means today's selection is driven by the model's
483	    paraphrase of the paper rather than the paper.)
484	    """
485	    want = _content(subsection)
486	    blob = f"{card.get('span')} {card.get('level')} {card.get('method')} {' '.join(card.get('mechanisms') or [])}"
487	    return len(want & _content(blob))
488	
489	
490	def plan_bundles(cards: list[dict], outline=OUTLINE, contract: dict | None = None,
491	                 max_narrate: int = 6) -> tuple[list[Bundle], dict[str, FindingRecord]]:
492	    """Deal every subsection a DELIBERATELY DIFFERENT evidence bundle.
493	
494	    Narration is a PARTITION: each finding is narrated in exactly one subsection (R1), so no two
495	    subsections narrate the same fact. Reuse is then granted -- but only as a NEW ROLE that must ADD
496	    something (R2/R3), and everything else degrades to an OWNED BACKWARD REFERENCE (R4).
497	    """
498	    clusters = build_clusters(cards)
499	    fid_to_cluster = {}
500	    for cl in clusters.values():
501	        fid_to_cluster[cl.representative] = cl.cluster_id
502	        for g in cl.corroborators:
503	            fid_to_cluster[g] = cl.cluster_id
504	    reps = {cl.representative for cl in clusters.values()}
505	
506	    by_fid = {finding_id(c): c for c in cards}
507	    ledger = {
508	        f: FindingRecord(finding_id=f, work_id=work_id(c), span=c.get('span', ''),
509	                         label=c.get('claim', ''), cluster_id=fid_to_cluster[f])
510	        for f, c in by_fid.items()
511	    }
512	
513	    jobs = [(sec, sub) for sec, subs in outline for sub in subs]
514	    bundles = [Bundle(section=sec, subsection=sub, function=classify_section(f'{sec} {sub}', contract))
515	               for sec, sub in jobs]
516	
517	    # ---- pass 1: PRIMARY ASSIGNMENT. Every representative finding is narrated exactly once, in the
518	    # subsection that fits it best AND that licenses the role it is naturally best at.
519	    scored = []
520	    for f in reps:
521	        c = by_fid[f]
522	        nrole = natural_role(c)
523	        for b in bundles:
524	            fit = relevance(c, f'{b.section} {b.subsection}')
525	            if nrole in FUNCTION_ROLES[b.function]:
526	                fit += 2                      # the section can actually USE what this finding is for
527	            scored.append((fit, f, b))
528	    scored.sort(key=lambda x: -x[0])
529	
530	    placed: set[str] = set()
531	    cap = collections.Counter()
532	    for fit, f, b in scored:
533	        if f in placed or cap[b.subsection] >= max_narrate or fit <= 0:
534	            continue
535	        b.narrate.append(f)
536	        placed.add(f)
537	        cap[b.subsection] += 1
538	        rec = ledger[f]
539	        rec.primary_section, rec.primary_role = b.subsection, natural_role(by_fid[f])
540	        rec.uses.append(Use(b.section, b.subsection, rec.primary_role, 'NARRATE'))
541	    # overflow: findings that fit nowhere with capacity still get a home (nothing is dropped)
542	    for f in reps - placed:
543	        b = min(bundles, key=lambda x: cap[x.subsection])
544	        b.narrate.append(f)
545	        cap[b.subsection] += 1
546	        rec = ledger[f]
547	        rec.primary_section, rec.primary_role = b.subsection, natural_role(by_fid[f])
548	        rec.uses.append(Use(b.section, b.subsection, rec.primary_role, 'NARRATE'))
549	
550	    # ---- pass 2: LEGITIMATE REUSE. Theory and synthesis sections need the canonical findings. They
551	    # get them -- but ONLY in a REUSABLE role (never a narration role), only if the finding has not
552	    # already played that role, only if the use ADDS something, and only within a GLOBAL budget.
553	    MUST_ADD = {Role.CONTRAST: 'comparison', Role.BOUNDARY: 'boundary',
554	                Role.METHOD_CRITIQUE: 'method', Role.IMPLICATION: 'implication',
555	                Role.MECHANISM: 'mechanism'}
556	    reuse_count: collections.Counter = collections.Counter()
557	
558	    for b in bundles:
559	        # Rank candidates by fit, but PENALISE findings already spent elsewhere. This is what drives
560	        # sibling sections apart: without it, "What the evidence establishes" and "What the evidence
561	        # cannot yet resolve" score every finding identically and are dealt the SAME bundle (measured
562	        # Jaccard 0.750). The tie-break on prior reuse is the only thing that makes them diverge.
563	        want = sorted(((relevance(by_fid[f], f'{b.section} {b.subsection}'), -reuse_count[f], f)
564	                       for f in reps if f not in b.narrate),
565	                      key=lambda t: (-t[0], -t[1]))
566	        for score, _, f in want:
567	            if score <= 0 or (len(b.new_role) >= 3 and len(b.backref) >= MAX_BACKREF_PER_SUBSECTION):
568	                continue
569	            if reuse_count[f] >= REUSE_BUDGET:
570	                continue                      # this fact is spent. Some other section may not have it.
571	            rec = ledger[f]
572	            played = {u.role for u in rec.uses if u.mode in ('NARRATE', 'NEW_ROLE')}
573	            free = [r for r in FUNCTION_ROLES[b.function]
574	                    if r in REUSABLE_ROLES and r not in played]
575	            if free and len(b.new_role) < 3:
576	                role = free[0]
577	                b.new_role.append((f, role, MUST_ADD[role]))
578	                rec.uses.append(Use(b.section, b.subsection, role, 'NEW_ROLE',
579	                                    adds={MUST_ADD[role]}))
580	                reuse_count[f] += 1
581	            elif len(b.backref) < MAX_BACKREF_PER_SUBSECTION:
582	                # R4: the fact is spent, or this section has no new job for it. Point at it; do not
583	                # say it again. An OWNED backward reference names no source and carries no new
584	                # particular -- it is a legal OWNED sentence under THE LAW, by construction.
585	                b.backref.append(f)
586	                rec.uses.append(Use(b.section, b.subsection, Role.ESTABLISH, 'BACKREF'))
587	                reuse_count[f] += 1
588	
589	    # ---- pass 3: THE BASKET. Corroborators ride along with their representative. Retained, cited,
590	    # never re-narrated.
591	    for b in bundles:
592	        for f in b.narrate:
593	            for g in clusters[fid_to_cluster[f]].corroborators:
594	                b.corroborate.append(g)
595	                ledger[g].uses.append(Use(b.section, b.subsection, Role.CORROBORATE, 'CORROBORATE'))
596	
597	    return bundles, ledger
598	
599	
600	# ===================================================================== the auditor
601	ANAPHORA = re.compile(r'^\s*(they|these authors|the same (study|authors|paper)|the authors|he|she)\b', re.I)
602	REPORTING = re.compile(
603	    r'\b(show(s|ed)? that|report(s|ed)? that|note(s|d)? that|find(s)? that|found that|'
604	    r'establish(es)? that|argue(s)? that|demonstrate(s)? that|examine(s)?)\b', re.I)
605	
606	ADDS_MARKERS: dict[str, re.Pattern] = {
607	    'comparison':  re.compile(r'\b(whereas|by contrast|in contrast|unlike|differ(s|ing)?|diverge|'
608	                              r'jointly|together|relative to|compared (with|to)|both .* and|'
609	                              r'contrasts?|mirrors?|aligns? with)\b', re.I),
610	    'boundary':    re.compile(r'\b(only if|only when|limited to|cannot|does not establish|'
611	                              r'is not established|conditional on|restricted to|does not distinguish|'
612	                              r'remains? unresolved|no evidence|insufficient|not sufficient|'
613	                              r'bounded by|does not preclude|fails? to)\b', re.I),
614	    'method':      re.compile(r'\b(observational|experimental|randomi[sz]ed|quasi-experiment|'
615	                              r'difference-in-difference|sample of|cross-section|panel|'
616	                              r'theoretical|simulation|survey of|controlled setting)\b', re.I),
617	    'implication': re.compile(r'\b(therefore|thus|hence|implies|it follows|consequently|'
618	                              r'suggests? that|means that|the upshot|as a result)\b', re.I),
619	}
620	
621	
622	@dataclass
623	class Narration:
624	    finding_id: str
625	    section: str
626	    subsection: str
627	    sentence: str
628	    words: int
629	    residue: set[str]                # the sentence MINUS its attribution -- i.e. the FACT it states
630	    adds: set[str]
631	    order: int                       # SENTENCE index. Two Narrations sharing an `order` are the SAME
632	    #                                  sentence telling two findings -- dedupe on this before summing
633	    #                                  words, or a compound sentence is billed once per fact it tells.
634	    anaphoric: bool = False
635	    multi: bool = False              # this sentence narrates >1 finding
636	    verdict: str = ''                # '' | OK | RESTATEMENT | RESTATED_BUT_ADDS. Declared, not
637	    #                                  monkey-patched: the first narration of every finding never
638	    #                                  gets one assigned, and any consumer reading it would explode.
639	
640	
641	def _strip_attribution(sent: str, card: dict) -> str:
642	    """Remove the attribution furniture, leaving the FACT.
643	
644	    The attribution is *supposed* to repeat -- every attributed sentence must name its source. The
645	    waste is repeating the FACT. So we compare residues, not sentences.
646	    """
647	    s = sent
648	    attr = (card.get('attribution') or '').strip()
649	    if attr:
650	        s = re.sub(re.escape(attr), ' ', s, flags=re.I)
651	    # ...and the reordered variant the writer emits: "Bresnahan et al., writing in the QJE in 2002,"
652	    s = re.sub(r'\b[A-Z][\wÀ-ɏ\'-]+(?:\s+(?:and|et al\.?)\s*[\wÀ-ɏ\'-]*)?,?\s*'
653	               r'writing in the [^,]{3,60}?in \d{4},?', ' ', s, flags=re.I)
654	    s = re.sub(r'\bwriting in the [^,]{3,60}?in \d{4},?', ' ', s, flags=re.I)
655	    s = REPORTING.sub(' ', s)
656	    s = re.sub(r'\*\*\[[^\]]+\]\*\*', ' ', s)          # epistemic labels
657	    return s
658	
659	
660	def _parse_sections(md: str) -> list[tuple[str, str, str]]:
661	    """-> [(section, subsection, paragraph)] for PROSE only (no headings, no table rows)."""
662	    out, sec, sub = [], '', ''
663	    for block in md.split('\n\n'):
664	        b = block.strip()
665	        if not b:
666	            continue
667	        if b.startswith('## '):
668	            sec, sub = b[3:].strip(), ''
669	            continue
670	        if b.startswith('### '):
671	            sub = b[4:].strip()
672	            continue
673	        if b.startswith('#') or b.lstrip().startswith('|'):
674	            continue                                    # title, or the evidence table
675	        out.append((sec, sub, b))
676	    return out
677	
678	
679	def audit_report(report_md: str, cards: list[dict]) -> dict:
680	    """THE TRUTH PASS. Which findings are over-narrated, the exact repeated sentences, the waste.
681	
682	    Attribution -> which WORK. Then, within that work, span-overlap -> which FINDING. If two
683	    findings of the same work tie, we fall back to the claim as a tiebreak AND SAY SO -- an
684	    unflagged fallback to model prose is how the last loop closed on itself.
685	    """
686	    by_fid = {finding_id(c): c for c in cards}
687	    surnames: dict[str, list[str]] = collections.defaultdict(list)   # surname -> [finding_id]
688	    for f, c in by_fid.items():
689	        for a in (c.get('authors') or []):
690	            surnames[a.lower()].append(f)
691	
692	    narrations: list[Narration] = []
693	    unresolved = 0
694	    owned = 0
695	    order = 0
696	
697	    for sec, sub, para in _parse_sections(report_md):
698	        last_work: str | None = None
699	        for sent in split_sentences_safe(para):
700	            if len(sent.split()) < 6:
701	                continue
702	            order += 1
703	            low = sent.lower()
704	            hits = {f for sn, fs in surnames.items() if re.search(rf'\b{re.escape(sn)}\b', low) for f in fs}
705	            anaphoric = False
706	            if not hits and ANAPHORA.match(sent) and last_work:
707	                hits = {f for f in by_fid if work_id(by_fid[f]) == last_work}
708	                anaphoric = True
709	            if not hits:
710	                owned += 1                                # OWNED voice: legal, and not a narration
711	                continue
712	
713	            # WHICH finding(s) is this sentence telling? Score every candidate against its SPAN.
714	            #
715	            # MULTI-LABEL, because sentences are. Measured, in "Why large task gains coexist":
716	            #
717	            #   "Autor ... reports that automation creates strong COMPLEMENTARITIES ... , YET
718	            #    technological changes ... have POLARIZED the labor market ..."
719	            #
720	            # -- one 57-word sentence narrating TWO distinct Autor findings, both already spent.
721	            # Charging it to the argmax alone (0.286 vs 0.242 -- nearly a coin-flip) books one
722	            # restatement and misses the other. So a sentence is credited to EVERY finding it
723	            # substantially tells, and the WASTE is then deduped by sentence so no word is billed
724	            # twice. This mirrors the composer's own _gate_multi, which gates each clause against
725	            # the source it names rather than blaming the whole sentence on the first author it
726	            # recognises.
727	            #
728	            # This also retires the claim-tiebreak that used to sit here. `claim` now touches NO
729	            # logic in this module -- it is a display label and nothing else.
730	            scored = sorted(((jaccard(_content(_strip_attribution(sent, by_fid[f])),
731	                                      _content(by_fid[f].get('span'))), f) for f in hits),
732	                            key=lambda x: -x[0])
733	            best = scored[0][0]
734	            if best < 0.10:
735	                unresolved += 1                           # names a source but matches no span: say so
736	                continue
737	            told = [f for s, f in scored if s >= 0.10 and s >= 0.70 * best]
738	
739	            last_work = work_id(by_fid[told[0]])
740	            adds = {k for k, p in ADDS_MARKERS.items() if p.search(sent)}
741	            for fid in told:
742	                card = by_fid[fid]
743	                residue = _content(_strip_attribution(sent, card))
744	                narrations.append(Narration(fid, sec, sub, sent, len(sent.split()), residue, adds,
745	                                            order, anaphoric, len(told) > 1))
746	
747	    # ---- who is over-narrated, and what did the repeats cost?
748	    by_finding: dict[str, list[Narration]] = collections.defaultdict(list)
749	    for n in narrations:
750	        by_finding[n.finding_id].append(n)
751	
752	    hard, soft = [], []
753	    for fid, ns in by_finding.items():
754	        ns.sort(key=lambda n: n.order)
755	        first = ns[0]
756	        for n in ns[1:]:
757	            sim = max(jaccard(n.residue, p.residue) for p in ns if p.order < n.order)
758	            new_adds = n.adds - first.adds
759	            if sim >= SIM_RESTATE and not new_adds:
760	                n.verdict = 'RESTATEMENT'
761	                hard.append((fid, n, sim))
762	            elif sim >= SIM_RESTATE:
763	                n.verdict = 'RESTATED_BUT_ADDS'
764	                soft.append((fid, n, sim))
765	            else:
766	                n.verdict = 'OK'
767	
768	    # exact byte-level repeats (after normalisation) -- the unarguable floor. Dedupe by sentence:
769	    # a compound sentence appears once per finding it tells, and must be counted ONCE.
770	    seen_sent = {n.order: n for n in narrations}
771	    norm_counts = collections.Counter(_norm_text(n.sentence) for n in seen_sent.values())
772	    exact = {k: v for k, v in norm_counts.items() if v > 1}
773	
774	    # A SENTENCE is wasted only if EVERY finding it tells was already spent and it adds nothing.
775	    # Billing per (sentence, finding) pair would charge a 57-word compound twice. Dedupe on `order`.
776	    hard_orders = {n.order for _, n, _ in hard}
777	    soft_orders = {n.order for _, n, _ in soft}
778	    ok_orders = {n.order for n in narrations if n.verdict == 'OK'}
779	    hard_orders -= ok_orders        # a sentence that does new work for ANY finding is not waste
780	    soft_orders -= (ok_orders | hard_orders)
781	
782	    hard_words = sum(seen_sent[o].words for o in hard_orders)
783	    hard_waste = max(0, hard_words - BACKREF_COST_WORDS * len(hard_orders))
784	    soft_words = sum(seen_sent[o].words for o in soft_orders)
785	
786	    return {
787	        'narrations': narrations,
788	        'by_finding': by_finding,
789	        'hard': hard, 'soft': soft, 'exact': exact,
790	        'hard_orders': hard_orders, 'soft_orders': soft_orders, 'sent_by_order': seen_sent,
791	        'owned': owned, 'unresolved': unresolved,
792	        'multi': len({n.order for n in narrations if n.multi}),
793	        'anaphoric': len({n.order for n in narrations if n.anaphoric}),
794	        'n_sentences': len(seen_sent),
795	        'hard_words': hard_words, 'hard_waste': hard_waste, 'soft_words': soft_words,
796	        'excess_exact': sum(v - 1 for v in exact.values()),
797	    }
798	
799	
800	# ===================================================================== report
801	def _rule(t=''):
802	    print('\n' + '=' * 78)
803	    if t:
804	        print(t)
805	        print('=' * 78)
806	
807	
808	def self_test() -> int:
809	    """Adversarial checks. A rule that is only asserted in a docstring is not a rule.
810	
811	    (Both real defects this module shipped with -- 49 of 84 reuses granted in narration-only roles,
812	    and a polarity guard that never fired -- were rules that existed ONLY in prose. Test the rules.)
813	    """
814	    fails = []
815	
816	    def ck(name, cond):
817	        print(f'  [{"PASS" if cond else "FAIL"}] {name}')
818	        if not cond:
819	            fails.append(name)
820	
821	    print('--- identity: the span is the evidence; the claim is a display cache')
822	    base = {'span': 'robot adoption reduced employment by 0.2 percent per thousand workers',
823	            'claim': 'Robots cut jobs.', 'doi': '10.1/x', 'authors': ['A'], 'year': 2020,
824	            'level': 'firm', 'method': 'observational', 'mechanisms': [], 'has_number': True}
825	    reworded = dict(base, claim='A COMPLETELY DIFFERENT PARAPHRASE, invented by the model.')
826	    respanned = dict(base, span='robot adoption RAISED employment by 0.2 percent')
827	    ck('rewording `claim` does NOT change finding_id (the model cannot forge a fresh identity '
828	       'for a spent fact by paraphrasing it)', finding_id(base) == finding_id(reworded))
829	    ck('changing `span` DOES change finding_id (identity tracks the paper, not the prose)',
830	       finding_id(base) != finding_id(respanned))
831	    ck('work_id is DOI-stable across differing spans',
832	       work_id(base) == work_id(respanned))
833	
834	    print('\n--- the basket: consolidation must never delete a disagreement')
835	    up = dict(base, span='automation increases labor demand and raises earnings for workers')
836	    dn = dict(base, span='automation decreases labor demand and reduces earnings for workers')
837	    ck('unmixed opposite polarity is detected as a contradiction',
838	       contradicts(up['span'], dn['span']))
839	    ck('a contradictory pair is BLOCKED from merging even at zero lexical distance',
840	       'POLARITY' in ' '.join(corroboration_blocks(up, dn, ignore_lexical=True)))
841	    mixed = dict(base, span='automation reduces the labor share even as it raises productivity')
842	    ck('mixed-polarity span does NOT report a contradiction (honest limit, documented)',
843	       not contradicts(up['span'], mixed['span']))
844	    diff_level = dict(up, level='economy')
845	    ck('same claim at a DIFFERENT unit of analysis is blocked from merging',
846	       'unit-of-analysis' in corroboration_blocks(up, diff_level, ignore_lexical=True))
847	    cl = build_clusters([base, dict(base, doi='10.1/y', authors=['B'])])
848	    ck('a corroborator is RETAINED in the basket, never dropped',
849	       sum(1 + len(c.corroborators) for c in cl.values()) == 2)
850	
851	    print('\n--- the rules')
852	    rec = FindingRecord('F:1', 'W:1', 'span', 'label', 'C:1')
853	    rec.uses = [Use('S', 'a', Role.ESTABLISH, 'NARRATE'), Use('S', 'b', Role.ESTABLISH, 'NARRATE')]
854	    ck('R1 fires: a finding narrated twice is a violation',
855	       any(v.startswith('R1') for v in rec.violations()))
856	    rec.uses = [Use('S', 'a', Role.CONTRAST, 'NARRATE'), Use('S', 'b', Role.CONTRAST, 'NEW_ROLE',
857	                                                             adds={'comparison'})]
858	    ck('R2 fires: the same analytical role reused is a violation',
859	       any(v.startswith('R2') for v in rec.violations()))
860	    rec.uses = [Use('S', 'a', Role.ESTABLISH, 'NARRATE'), Use('S', 'b', Role.CONTRAST, 'NEW_ROLE')]
861	    ck('R3 fires: a new role that ADDS NOTHING is a violation',
862	       any(v.startswith('R3') for v in rec.violations()))
863	    ck('narration-only roles are excluded from reuse (ESTABLISH/MAGNITUDE/DEFINE)',
864	       not (NARRATION_ONLY_ROLES & REUSABLE_ROLES))
865	
866	    print('\n--- the plan over the REAL corpus')
867	    cards = json.loads(CARDS_PATH.read_text())
868	    bundles, ledger = plan_bundles(cards)
869	    ck('the plan itself contains ZERO rule violations',
870	       sum(len(r.violations()) for r in ledger.values()) == 0)
871	    ck('narration is a PARTITION: every finding narrated exactly once',
872	       all(len(r.narrations) == 1 for r in ledger.values()))
873	    ck('no reuse is granted in a narration-only role',
874	       not any(r in NARRATION_ONLY_ROLES for b in bundles for _, r, _ in b.new_role))
875	    ck('no finding exceeds the global reuse budget',
876	       all(sum(1 for u in r.uses if u.mode in ('NEW_ROLE', 'BACKREF')) <= REUSE_BUDGET
877	           for r in ledger.values()))
878	    ck('every finding we hold is used somewhere (nothing is silently dropped)',
879	       all(r.uses for r in ledger.values()))
880	
881	    print('\n--- waste accounting')
882	    au = audit_report(REPORT_PATH.read_text(), cards)
883	    ck('compound sentences are billed ONCE, not once per fact they tell',
884	       au['hard_words'] == sum(au['sent_by_order'][o].words for o in au['hard_orders']))
885	    ck('a sentence doing new work for ANY finding is not counted as waste',
886	       not (au['hard_orders'] & {n.order for n in au['narrations'] if n.verdict == 'OK'}))
887	
888	    print(f'\n{"** ALL CHECKS PASS **" if not fails else "** " + str(len(fails)) + " FAILED **"}')
889	    return 1 if fails else 0
890	
891	
892	def main() -> int:
893	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
894	    ap.add_argument('--cards', default=str(CARDS_PATH))
895	    ap.add_argument('--report', default=str(REPORT_PATH))
896	    ap.add_argument('--audit-only', action='store_true')
897	    ap.add_argument('--plan-only', action='store_true')
898	    ap.add_argument('--self-test', action='store_true')
899	    a = ap.parse_args()
900	    if a.self_test:
901	        return self_test()
902	
903	    cards = json.loads(Path(a.cards).read_text())
904	    report_md = Path(a.report).read_text()
905	    by_fid = {finding_id(c): c for c in cards}
906	    clusters = build_clusters(cards)
907	
908	    print(f'cards={len(cards)}  distinct findings (span-addressed)={len(by_fid)}  '
909	          f'works={len({work_id(c) for c in cards})}  claim clusters={len(clusters)}')
910	    dupe_spans = len(cards) - len(by_fid)
911	    if dupe_spans:
912	        print(f'  ! {dupe_spans} cards share a span with another card -- the SAME sentence of the '
913	              f'same paper, extracted twice. Positional ids hid this; span-addressed ids cannot.')
914	    multi = [c for c in clusters.values() if c.n_sources > 1]
915	    merged = sum(len(c.corroborators) for c in clusters.values())
916	    print(f'  corroborated clusters (>=2 independent works say it): {len(multi)}  '
917	          f'-- RETAINED in the basket, narrated once')
918	    print(f'  findings demoted to corroborator (retained, not re-narrated): {merged}')
919	
920	    if len(multi) == 0:
921	        d = corroboration_diagnostic(cards)
922	        _rule('WHY ZERO CORROBORATION CLUSTERS -- AND WHY I DID NOT TUNE THE THRESHOLD TO FIX IT')
923	        print(f'  max cross-work span Jaccard over {d["n_pairs"]:,} pairs: {d["max_j"]:.3f} '
924	              f'(threshold {SIM_CORROBORATE}).')
925	        print('  If the lexical gate were loosened to admit them, what would STILL block these?\n')
926	        for j, x, y in d['top']:
927	            stops = corroboration_blocks(x, y, ignore_lexical=True)
928	            flag = ' + '.join(stops) if stops else '*** NOTHING -- WOULD MERGE ***'
929	            print(f'    J={j:.3f}  still blocked by: {flag}')
930	            print(f'      {x["authors"][0]} {x.get("year")}: {x["claim"][:76]}')
931	            print(f'      {y["authors"][0]} {y.get("year")}: {y["claim"][:76]}')
932	        print(f'\n  near-miss pairs (J>=0.15), what would STILL block each: {dict(d["blocked"])}')
933	        print('\n  READ THAT AGAIN: at a loosened threshold, most near-miss pairs merge with NOTHING')
934	        print('  stopping them -- and one of them (Pillai/Baakeel) is not the same claim at all.')
935	        print('\n  AND THE POLARITY GUARD IS WEAKER THAN IT LOOKS. I wrote it believing it caught')
936	        print('  Autor("...INCREASE productivity, RAISE earnings, AUGMENT demand") against')
937	        print('  Acemoglu("...REDUCES the labor share ... EVEN AS IT RAISES productivity").')
938	        print('  It does not. Acemoglu\'s span is MIXED ({UP,DOWN}), the bags intersect, and')
939	        print('  contradicts() returns False. That pair is saved by the UNIT-OF-ANALYSIS guard')
940	        print('  (worker vs economy) -- NOT by polarity. Direction words bind to OBJECTS, and a bag')
941	        print('  of words loses the binding. Sound detection needs (direction, quantity) tuples.')
942	        print('\n  Note what the model did to that sentence: its CLAIM reads "reduces labor\'s share')
943	        print('  and may diminish labor demand" -- the "even as it raises productivity" hedge is')
944	        print('  GONE. polarity(claim)={DOWN} but polarity(span)={UP,DOWN}. THE CLAIM IS MORE')
945	        print('  POLARIZED THAN THE PAPER. A detector run on `claim` would have "found" a')
946	        print('  contradiction the source does not contain -- and been more confident for having')
947	        print('  lost the evidence. This is why the span is the only evidence.')
948	        print('\n  The basket is BUILT and CORRECT; the DETECTOR is the missing piece and it is not')
949	        print('  lexical. Zero is the honest count. A tuned threshold would delete disagreements.')
950	
951	    # ---------------------------------------------------------------- what the SELECTOR does today
952	    if not a.audit_only:
953	        _rule('WHAT THE CURRENT SELECTOR DOES (cellcog_composer._select, no memory)')
954	        jobs = [(sec, sub) for sec, subs in OUTLINE for sub in subs]
955	        slots, sel_count = 0, collections.Counter()
956	        for sec, sub in jobs:
957	            for c in _select(cards, sub):
958	                slots += 1
959	                sel_count[finding_id(c)] += 1
960	
961	        never = len(by_fid) - len(sel_count)
962	        print(f'  {len(jobs)} subsections draw {slots} card slots from {len(sel_count)} distinct '
963	              f'findings.')
964	        print(f'  {never} of {len(by_fid)} findings are NEVER selected -- '
965	              f'{never / len(by_fid) * 100:.0f}% of the evidence we paid to extract is dead weight.')
966	        print(f'  selection histogram (times selected -> n findings): '
967	              f'{dict(sorted(collections.Counter(sel_count.values()).items()))}')
968	        print('  the most over-drawn findings:')
969	        for fid, n in sel_count.most_common(5):
970	            c = by_fid[fid]
971	            print(f'    {n}x  {c["authors"][0]:<12} {c["claim"][:66]}')
972	
973	    # ---------------------------------------------------------------- THE AUDIT
974	    if not a.plan_only:
975	        _rule('THE AUDIT: WHAT THE SHIPPED REPORT ACTUALLY DID')
976	        au = audit_report(report_md, cards)
977	        print(f'  attributed SENTENCES              : {au["n_sentences"]}')
978	        print(f'  (finding, sentence) narration pairs : {len(au["narrations"])}  '
979	              f'-- {au["multi"]} sentences narrate MORE THAN ONE finding')
980	        print(f'    ...anaphoric ("They also find that...") : {au["anaphoric"]}')
981	        print(f'  OWNED sentences (name no source -- legal, not narrations) : {au["owned"]}')
982	        print(f'  names a source but matches no span (UNRESOLVED, not counted): {au["unresolved"]}')
983	
984	        over = {f: ns for f, ns in au['by_finding'].items() if len(ns) > 1}
985	        print(f'\n  distinct findings narrated  : {len(au["by_finding"])} of {len(by_fid)} held')
986	        print(f'  findings narrated MORE THAN ONCE : {len(over)}')
987	
988	        _rule('OVER-NARRATED FINDINGS (ranked by wasted words)')
989	        rank = []
990	        for fid, ns in over.items():
991	            w = sum(n.words for n in ns[1:] if n.verdict == 'RESTATEMENT')
992	            rank.append((w, len(ns), fid, ns))
993	        rank.sort(key=lambda x: (-x[0], -x[1]))
994	        for w, k, fid, ns in rank[:10]:
995	            c = by_fid[fid]
996	            verdicts = collections.Counter(n.verdict for n in ns[1:])
997	            print(f'\n  {fid}  narrated {k}x  [{w}w restated]  {c["authors"][0]} {c.get("year")}')
998	            print(f'    FACT (verbatim span): "{re.sub(chr(10), " ", c["span"])[:96]}"')
999	            print(f'    repeats: {dict(verdicts)}')
1000	            for n in ns:
1001	                tag = 'FULL NARRATION ' if n is ns[0] else f'{n.verdict:15s}'
1002	                print(f'      [{tag}] {n.subsection[:44]:<44} ({n.words}w)')
1003	
1004	        _rule('THE EXACT REPEATED SENTENCES (byte-identical after normalisation)')
1005	        print(f'  {len(au["exact"])} sentence forms appear more than once; '
1006	              f'{au["excess_exact"]} excess instances.\n')
1007	        for k, v in sorted(au['exact'].items(), key=lambda x: -(x[1] * len(x[0].split())))[:8]:
1008	            hitset = [n for n in au['sent_by_order'].values() if _norm_text(n.sentence) == k]
1009	            print(f'  {v}x [{len(k.split())}w] "{hitset[0].sentence[:104]}..."')
1010	            print(f'       in: {" | ".join(n.subsection[:30] for n in hitset)}\n')
1011	
1012	        _rule('THE MEASURED RESTATEMENT WASTE')
1013	        body_words = len(re.sub(r'(?m)^#.*$|^\|.*$', '', report_md).split())
1014	        print(f'  report body                                     : {body_words:,} words')
1015	        print(f'  [1] EXACT duplicate sentences (excess instances) : {au["excess_exact"]} sentences')
1016	        print(f'  [2] HARD RESTATEMENT -- every fact it tells was already spent, and it adds nothing:')
1017	        print(f'         {len(au["hard_orders"])} sentences, {au["hard_words"]:,} words')
1018	        print(f'         replaced by an OWNED backward reference (~{BACKREF_COST_WORDS}w each):')
1019	        print(f'         RECLAIMABLE = {au["hard_waste"]:,} words '
1020	              f'({au["hard_waste"] / body_words * 100:.1f}% of the report)')
1021	        print(f'  [3] SOFT -- fact restated but the sentence DOES add something:')
1022	        print(f'         {len(au["soft_orders"])} sentences, {au["soft_words"]:,} words.')
1023	        print(f'         NOT counted as waste. The fact could still be compressed to a pointer while')
1024	        print(f'         keeping the new clause, but I am not going to put a number on that.')
1025	        print(f'\n  HONEST TOTAL (defensible, [2] only) : {au["hard_waste"]:,} words '
1026	              f'= {au["hard_waste"] / body_words * 100:.1f}% of the report says nothing new.')
1027	        print(f'  Charging one backward reference per restatement OVERSTATES the replacement cost')
1028	        print(f'  (a section restating a fact 5x needs one pointer, not five), so this UNDERSTATES')
1029	        print(f'  the recoverable words. Words are billed per SENTENCE, never per (sentence,fact).')
1030	
1031	    # ---------------------------------------------------------------- THE PLAN
1032	    if not a.audit_only:
1033	        _rule('THE LEDGER PLAN: WHAT THE BUNDLES WOULD BE INSTEAD')
1034	        bundles, ledger = plan_bundles(cards)
1035	        tot_n = sum(len(b.narrate) for b in bundles)
1036	        tot_r = sum(len(b.new_role) for b in bundles)
1037	        tot_b = sum(len(b.backref) for b in bundles)
1038	        tot_c = sum(len(b.corroborate) for b in bundles)
1039	        narrated_once = sum(1 for r in ledger.values() if len(r.narrations) == 1)
1040	        print(f'  NARRATE (full, once)    : {tot_n}   <- a PARTITION: no fact is narrated twice')
1041	        print(f'  NEW_ROLE (reuse, adds)  : {tot_r}   <- theory/synthesis DO get the canonical facts')
1042	        print(f'  BACKREF (owned pointer) : {tot_b}   <- the fact is spent; point, do not restate')
1043	        print(f'  CORROBORATE (basket)    : {tot_c}   <- retained evidence, never re-narrated')
1044	        print(f'  findings narrated exactly once: {narrated_once} / {len(ledger)}')
1045	        viol = [v for r in ledger.values() for v in r.violations()]
1046	        print(f'  RULE VIOLATIONS IN THE PLAN: {len(viol)}   <- the plan is legal by construction')
1047	
1048	        # bundle distinctness
1049	        overlaps = []
1050	        for i, x in enumerate(bundles):
1051	            for y in bundles[i + 1:]:
1052	                overlaps.append(jaccard(x.all_fids(), y.all_fids()))
1053	        import statistics as st
1054	        print(f'\n  bundle distinctness (pairwise Jaccard over 28 subsections):')
1055	        print(f'    mean {st.mean(overlaps):.3f} | max {max(overlaps):.3f}  '
1056	              f'-- each section argues from DIFFERENT evidence')
1057	
1058	        print('\n  sample bundles:')
1059	        for b in bundles[:3]:
1060	            print(f'\n    [{b.function.value.upper():<11}] {b.subsection[:56]}')
1061	            for f in b.narrate[:3]:
1062	                print(f'       NARRATE  {ledger[f].label[:62]}')
1063	            for f, role, add in b.new_role[:2]:
1064	                print(f'       {role.value:<9}(must add a {add})  {ledger[f].label[:38]}')
1065	            for f in b.backref[:2]:
1066	                print(f'       BACKREF  (owned pointer, no restatement)  {ledger[f].label[:34]}')
1067	
1068	        LEDGER_PATH.write_text(json.dumps({
1069	            'findings': {f: {'work_id': r.work_id, 'cluster_id': r.cluster_id,
1070	                             'primary_section': r.primary_section,
1071	                             'primary_role': r.primary_role.value if r.primary_role else None,
1072	                             'span': r.span,
1073	                             'uses': [{'subsection': u.subsection, 'role': u.role.value,
1074	                                       'mode': u.mode, 'adds': sorted(u.adds)} for u in r.uses]}
1075	                         for f, r in ledger.items()},
1076	            'clusters': {c: {'representative': cl.representative, 'corroborators': cl.corroborators,
1077	                             'n_sources': cl.n_sources} for c, cl in clusters.items()},
1078	            'bundles': [{'section': b.section, 'subsection': b.subsection,
1079	                         'function': b.function.value, 'narrate': b.narrate,
1080	                         'new_role': [(f, r.value, a_) for f, r, a_ in b.new_role],
1081	                         'backref': b.backref, 'corroborate': b.corroborate} for b in bundles],
1082	        }, indent=1))
1083	        print(f'\n  wrote {LEDGER_PATH}  (the composer consumes this; it is not prose)')
1084	
1085	    return 0
1086	
1087	
1088	if __name__ == '__main__':
1089	    raise SystemExit(main())
